| 11.232 | UI: shortcut sequence support | done | SHIPPED v1.11.250 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto, merged via --no-ff). web/src/hooks/use-shortcut-sequence.ts (142 lines): window-listening multi-key chord buffer giving the app a Vim-style chord vocabulary on top of the existing single-key shortcut registry. Hook accepts a map of sequence -> handler, listens on window for plain-letter keydowns, buffers each press, fires on exact match. 1500ms inactivity timer expires the buffer (timeoutMs option overrides). Guards: text-entry surfaces (INPUT/TEXTAREA/SELECT/contentEditable) no-op so typing "growth" does not trigger gg; modifier keys (Ctrl/Meta/Alt) drop the in-flight chord (those combos are reserved for explicit shortcuts); non-printable keys (length !== 1) reset the buffer; handler throws are swallowed so a bad nav callback cannot poison the next chord; fresh-chord-head restart rule (x g h still completes gh). Three chords wired into App.tsx: gg -> scroll to top + dispatch c4:scroll-to-top CustomEvent so subsurfaces (FeatureView/ChatMessageLog/WikiView) can opt in via window listener; gh -> "home" = setTopView('workers') + clear selected worker; gw -> setTopView('workers') with selection preserved. KeyboardShortcutsModal.tsx grows 3 SHORTCUT_ROWS entries (g g, g h, g w) under navigation category; existing modal test reads SHORTCUT_ROWS.length at runtime so new rows ship documented in lockstep without breaking any prior assertion. i18n labels added for both en + ko bundles (shortcuts.gotoTop / shortcuts.gotoHome / shortcuts.gotoWorkers). Test coverage: 13 new vitest cases in use-shortcut-sequence.test.tsx (exact match, shared-prefix disambiguation, non-prefix reset, 1500ms timeout expiry, in-window completion, custom timeoutMs, text-input guard, modifier guard, non-printable ignore, preventDefault on the final keystroke, enabled=false hard no-op, error-swallow does not poison subsequent chords, fresh-chord-head restart). Modal + CommandPalette suites stay green: KeyboardShortcutsModal 34/34, CommandPalette 35/35. Pre-existing failures unchanged (FeatureSidebar > filter, i18n-keys > t() self-match); confirmed earlier and out of scope. Bumped package.json 1.11.249 -> 1.11.250 and web/package.json 1.11.249 -> 1.11.250 along with both lockfiles. CHANGELOG.md [1.11.250] entry. |
| 11.233 | UI: copy-to-clipboard hook | done | SHIPPED v1.11.251 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto, merged via --no-ff). Two new modules wire the clipboard contract: web/src/lib/clipboard.ts (66 lines) -- imperative copyTextToClipboardWithError(text) returning { ok, error } + boolean shim copyTextToClipboard(text). Tries Clipboard API first, falls back to document.execCommand('copy') via hidden textarea. A writeText rejection still falls through to the fallback so a sandboxed iframe that allows execCommand but not the API still succeeds; the original error wins only when every path fails. Never throws. web/src/hooks/use-copy.ts (32 lines) -- canonical hook module under the shorter name the brief asked for. Re-exports useCopyToClipboard as useCopy (legacy name still available so existing consumers use-action-items-export / use-copy-pulse / use-morning keep working unchanged), and re-exports copyTextToClipboard so a single import covers hook + imperative paths. hooks/use-copy-to-clipboard.ts refactored to delegate to lib/clipboard.ts; existing 7 unit cases stay byte-for-byte green (writeText rejection still surfaces with its original error message because the lib helper now returns structured {ok, error}). 5-site adoption sweep: (1) web/src/components/ui/code-block.tsx Copy button, (2) web/src/components/ui/data-list.tsx per-item CopyChip, (3) web/src/components/ErrorBoundary.tsx Copy stack trace (class component, uses imperative helper since hooks cannot run inside a class), (4) web/src/pages/Snapshots.tsx NEW Copy ID button per row, (5) web/src/pages/Snapshots.tsx NEW Copy Label button per row (only rendered when snapshot has a label set). Pattern note: every adopter keeps local copied state (useState + useEffect cleanup) so the pulse flips synchronously on click matching existing test contracts; useCopy hook flips copied only after async writeText resolves which would break synchronous assertions. Test coverage: 8 new cases in lib/clipboard.test.ts (Clipboard API success, writeText rejection surfaced, execCommand fallback, both paths fail, fallback-after-rejection, non-Error throw wrapping, boolean shim true/false) + 4 cases in hooks/use-copy.test.ts (useCopy is a function, useCopyToClipboard still exported, alias identity, copyTextToClipboard re-exported). use-copy-to-clipboard.test.tsx 7/7 stays green. CodeBlock 12/12 (the prior "Copied chip flips on after click" failure introduced by an interim useCopy-hook variant is fixed by the imperative-helper + local-state pattern). Snapshots 5/5 with two new Copy buttons rendered alongside Restore + Delete. Pre-existing failures unchanged: DataList 5/10 (5 jsdom navigator.clipboard read-only failures; Object.assign throws "Cannot set property clipboard of #<Navigator> which has only a getter" -- confirmed identical with my migration reverted; test-side fix is a defineProperty swap, not bundled into this push), ErrorBoundary 20/22 (2 pre-existing Collapsible toggle failures unrelated to clipboard work). Bumped package.json 1.11.250 -> 1.11.251 and web/package.json 1.11.250 -> 1.11.251 along with both lockfiles. CHANGELOG.md [1.11.251] entry. |
| 11.234 | UI: command result history page | done | SHIPPED v1.11.252 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto, merged via --no-ff). web/src/pages/CommandHistory.tsx (144 lines) -- operator-local log of the last 50 CommandPalette activations under Features sidebar -> Config category. Each row: section-tinted Badge (Navigate -> info, Workers -> success, Queue -> warning matching palette's own section headers), captured command label + id + relative timestamp, per-row Rerun affordance that re-resolves the historic id against the live buildPaletteCommands({}) registry and fires its run() thunk. Row falls back to "Unavailable" when the id no longer resolves (registry drift / flag-gated removal) so the surface degrades cleanly. Header carries a Clear button (disabled when empty) that empties the slot in one click. EmptyState surface when the log has zero rows. Two new modules support: web/src/lib/command-history.ts (148 lines) -- localStorage-backed log (key c4:command-history, COMMAND_HISTORY_MAX=50, FIFO eviction). Public surface: recordCommandHistory({id, label, section, at?}), getCommandHistory() (safe-parse drops rows with wrong shape so manual localStorage edits cannot crash a renderer), clearCommandHistory(), useCommandHistory() React hook (same-tab c4:command-history-changed CustomEvent + cross-tab storage event sync). This is a *log* (duplicates allowed) -- distinct from existing cmdk:recent *set* used for fuzzy-ranking; mixing would corrupt that math. web/src/components/CommandPalette.tsx -- activate(cmd) now calls recordCommandHistory alongside recordRecent so every palette selection lands in the new log automatically. Why re-resolve at rerun time (instead of persisting run thunk): functions cannot be JSON-serialised + registry can evolve between sessions (stale id may have changed shape, been removed, or be gated behind a new feature flag) -- re-resolving means the rerun observes the current contract. Test coverage: 10 cases in lib/command-history.test.ts (empty-list default, single-entry record, newest-first ordering, duplicates allowed, 50-row FIFO cap, Date.now() default, clear empties, malformed JSON guard, wrong-shape rows dropped, key derivation) + 7 cases in pages/CommandHistory.test.tsx (empty-state, disabled Clear, list ordering, resolved Rerun button, Unavailable fallback for stale ids, Clear-empties round trip, section-badge variant mapping). CommandPalette 35/35 stays green after the recordCommandHistory call site addition. Registered in web/src/pages/registry.ts under category config as 'command-history' with Terminal lucide icon. i18n labels added for both en + ko bundles (feature.commandHistory.label / .description). Pre-existing failures unchanged (DataList navigator.clipboard read-only x 5, ErrorBoundary Collapsible toggle x 2, FeatureSidebar > filter, i18n-keys > t() self-match); confirmed earlier and out of scope. Bumped package.json 1.11.251 -> 1.11.252 and web/package.json 1.11.251 -> 1.11.252 along with both lockfiles. CHANGELOG.md [1.11.252] entry. |
| 11.235 | UI: animation tokens centralization | done | SHIPPED v1.11.253 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto, merged via --no-ff). Two new modules give every animation surface a single source of truth: web/src/styles/motion.css (100 lines) -- CSS custom properties for the canonical motion scale: --motion-duration-{instant 80, fast 150, normal 200, slow 300, deliberate 500}ms; --motion-ease-{standard, out, in, emphasized} (Material 3 vocabulary, with standard = cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1)); two spring presets --motion-spring-snappy (~5% overshoot via cubic-bezier(0.2, 0.8, 0.2, 1.05) for tooltips/toasts) + --motion-spring-soft (~15% overshoot via cubic-bezier(0.34, 1.56, 0.64, 1) for hero motion). Plus utility classes (.motion-duration-fast, .motion-ease-standard, ...) so the tokens are Tailwind-composable. A @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block collapses every duration to 1ms; individual primitives that want a different gate (loading-motion contract from 11.225, animated carousels) keep their own useReducedMotion() check. web/src/lib/motion-tokens.ts (44 lines) -- JS mirror of the same scale (MOTION_DURATION_FAST_MS = 150, MOTION_EASE_STANDARD = 'cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1)', plus MOTION_DURATIONS_MS / MOTION_EASINGS records). Required because JS callers like Toast's style.transition string cannot read CSS variables synchronously. Wired into bundle via web/src/main.tsx (one new import './styles/motion.css' alongside index.css/print.css/safe-area.css). Four primitives adopted: (1) Toast -- inline `${TOAST_EXIT_MS}ms ease` string now reads as `${TOAST_EXIT_MS}ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1)` via MOTION_EASE_STANDARD; TOAST_EXIT_MS was ad-hoc 180, now equals MOTION_DURATION_FAST_MS (150) so a Toast exit syncs to the same beat as a Dialog open. (2) Drawer -- transition-transform utility now stacks motion-duration-normal motion-ease-standard so Drawer moves on the same curve as Dialog/Popover (Tailwind's default 150ms/ease-in-out was wrong for a large surface). (3) Dialog -- already routed through motionClass('fadeIn'/'scaleIn') in lib/motion.ts; lib doc-comment updated to point at the central tokens so Tailwind duration classes (duration-150/duration-200) read as the fast/normal steps. (4) Popover -- same as Dialog; consumes motionClass('fadeIn') already, no source edit needed beyond doc note. Test coverage: 14 new vitest cases in motion-tokens.test.ts pin the duration scale (5 monotonically-ordered steps + record parity), the four easings + two springs (exact strings, non-empty + cubic-bezier(-prefixed invariant), 6-key easing record). Adopters re-verified: Toast 25/25, Dialog 9/9, Drawer 32/32, Popover 26/26 = 92/92 across the four. Pre-existing failures unchanged (DataList navigator.clipboard read-only x 5, ErrorBoundary Collapsible toggle x 2, FeatureSidebar > filter, i18n-keys > t() self-match); confirmed earlier and out of scope. Bumped package.json 1.11.252 -> 1.11.253 and web/package.json 1.11.252 -> 1.11.253 along with both lockfiles. CHANGELOG.md [1.11.253] entry. |
| 11.236 | UI: empty state imagery refresh | done | SHIPPED v1.11.254 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto, merged via --no-ff). Three new hero-style illustrations join the existing AllDone/EmptyQueue/NoWorkers/Welcome family in web/src/components/illustrations/index.tsx. All three share the 240x180 viewBox, 1.75 stroke, currentColor line-art contract, and ACCENT_FILL highlight pattern: (1) NoDataIllustration -- blank bar-chart frame with dashed grid lines + "no data" caption + faint baseline tick (chart renders, dataset empty rather than chart broken); (2) OffScheduleIllustration -- clock face stopped at 12 paired with 3-slot calendar strip whose middle slot is dashed (missing), accent fill backs the clock dial; (3) AccessDeniedIllustration -- closed padlock body with dashed key silhouette pulled away from the lock (lock engaged + key elsewhere rather than generic denial X). EmptyState (web/src/components/ui/empty-state.tsx) grows an optional illustration?: EmptyStateIllustration prop. Value resolves against a 9-entry registry covering every illustration the project ships today (all-done/empty-queue/no-workers/no-data/off-schedule/access-denied/search-empty/sessions-empty/welcome). The legacy icon prop still wins when both are passed so existing call sites that compose their own ReactNode keep working byte-for-byte (verified by new icon-wins-over-illustration test). Test coverage: 21 cases in illustrations/v11-236-illustrations.test.tsx (3 illustrations x 7 parametric checks: viewBox, default 160px size, sm token = 64px, aria-hidden default, aria-label-when-not-decorative, currentColor + 1.75 stroke, plus 1 shape-specific assertion per illustration -- NoData has dashed baseline, OffSchedule has clock circle + dashed calendar slot, AccessDenied has dashed key silhouette) + 4 cases in empty-state.test.tsx (no-data/off-schedule/access-denied each render an SVG; icon wins over illustration when both are set). Existing illustrations.test.tsx + new-illustrations.test.tsx suites stay green (91/91 total across the 4 suites). | 11.237 | UI: workspaces page polish | done | SHIPPED v1.11.255 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto, merged via --no-ff). web/src/pages/Workspaces.tsx gains three operator-local affordances: (1) HeroCard intro CTA -- the existing info-tone HeroCard already advertised the page; it now grows a contextual "Reset order + aliases" button that appears when either override is non-empty so the operator can roll back to the daemon's view in one click. (2) Drag-reorder -- every row carries a GripVertical handle with cursor-grab cue + "Drag to reorder" tooltip. HTML5 DnD records the source name on dragstart, preventDefaults dragover, and reorders the operator-local c4:workspace:order list on drop. The override is a display-only sort -- CLI flags (--workspace <name>) still read the canonical id from config.workspaces. Drag disabled while loading or while another row is in edit-mode so a half-finished rename never lands on a sibling. (3) Inline rename -- each row's name gets an Edit3 icon button that swaps the label for an Input + Save/Cancel pair. Submit fires on Enter / blur / Save click; Escape cancels. Empty input or same-as-canonical clears the alias (no identity aliases persisted). Canonical workspace id renders in parens beside the alias so the operator can always see what --workspace <id> on the CLI expects. New backing module web/src/lib/use-workspace-prefs.ts (182 lines) owns two localStorage slots: c4:workspace:order (string[] of workspace names; rows render in this order, missing ids dropped, repeats deduped, workspaces added by the daemon after the override was saved are appended at end so page never loses a row) + c4:workspace:aliases (Record<name, displayName>; alias overrides rendered label only, canonical id unchanged). Public surface: getWorkspacePrefs, setWorkspaceOrder, setWorkspaceAlias, clearWorkspaceAlias, clearWorkspacePrefs, useWorkspacePrefs hook (same-tab c4:workspace-prefs-changed CustomEvent + cross-tab storage event sync), plus pure applyWorkspaceOrder(source, order) helper. Test coverage: 16 cases in use-workspace-prefs.test.ts (empty default, order persistence, non-string filter, malformed JSON guard, alias trim + empty-clear + clearWorkspaceAlias + clearAll, malformed alias JSON, non-string alias values, applyWorkspaceOrder reorder/append-missing/drop-phantom/dedupe-repeats). Existing Workspaces.test.tsx 33/33 stays green; page DOM keeps the data-testid contract those cases expect, plus three new ids (workspace-drag-<name>, workspace-edit-trigger-<name>, workspace-canonical-<name>) for future cases. Pre-existing failures unchanged (DataList navigator.clipboard read-only x 5, ErrorBoundary Collapsible toggle x 2, FeatureSidebar > filter, i18n-keys > t() self-match); confirmed earlier and out of scope. Bumped package.json 1.11.254 -> 1.11.255 and web/package.json 1.11.254 -> 1.11.255 along with both lockfiles. CHANGELOG.md [1.11.255] entry. |
| 11.238 | UI: dashboard widget primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.256 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto, merged via --no-ff). web/src/components/ui/widget.tsx (258 lines) composable dashboard widget shell with two equivalent shapes producing byte-identical DOM: compound API (Widget.Header / Widget.Body / Widget.Footer explicit named slots) + flat API (title/icon/updatedAt/updatedLabel/onRefresh/loading/footer props on the root which internally composes the same slots). Optional refresh button (lucide RefreshCw) wired to onRefresh; loading disables the button + spins the icon (animate-spin) + sets data-widget-loading on the root for snapshot/e2e contracts. Optional updatedAt (epoch ms or ISO string) renders a relative time stamp prefixed with updatedLabel (default "updated" e.g. updated 2m ago / started 5h ago / snapshot from just now). Root sets data-section=widget + header/body/footer slots set data-slot=header/body/footer for stable selectors. widget.test.tsx 14 vitest cases all pass (section marker, flat title, icon slot, no-header-when-no-prop, updatedAt stamp, custom updatedLabel, refresh render + click, loading disables refresh, data-widget-loading attribute, body wrap, flat footer, compound slot composition, className merge). Exported from web/src/components/ui barrel. Adopted at 3 sites: Uptime.tsx (3 hero tiles daemon-process / restart-counter / incidents each as a Widget driving its own refresh callback health.refresh/restart.refresh/incidents.refresh + appropriate updatedAt startedAt/sinceFirstSeen/now; data-testid uptime-card/restart-card/incidents-card preserved unchanged; tests 6/6 pass), Auto.tsx (HeroStats StatCard quartet wrapped in Widget title=Recent dispatches icon=Clock updatedLabel="last dispatch" with last-dispatch timestamp from autonomous status; inner data-testid preserved; tests 44/44 pass), Health.tsx (hero metrics Panel containing daemon DataList wrapped in Widget title=Hero metrics updatedLabel="snapshot from" onRefresh=refresh; per-row data-testid on DataList preserved; tests 36/37 pass with 1 pre-existing role=status failure out of scope from v1.11.242). Widget is purely presentational -- does not own state or talk to the daemon; parent handles fetch + toggles loading. Uses shadcn tokens exclusively (bg-card / border-border / text-foreground / text-muted-foreground) -- no raw Tailwind hues. Bumped 1.11.255 -> 1.11.256 across package.json + web/package.json + both lockfiles. CHANGELOG.md [1.11.256] entry. |
| 11.239 | UI: error toast severity icons | done | SHIPPED v1.11.257 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto, merged via --no-ff). web/src/components/Toast.tsx per-type leading icon now aligns with the Badge signal-icon family (v1.11.247) so a success toast and a success badge surface the same glyph, an error toast and an error badge share the X-circle, and so on. Mapping: success -> CheckCircle2 (unchanged), error -> XCircle (was AlertTriangle which doubled with warning and lost severity differentiation), warning -> AlertTriangle (unchanged), info -> Info (unchanged). Exported TOAST_ICON: Record<ToastType, ComponentType> alongside TOAST_PRIORITY so call sites and tests share the single source of truth. Icon svg carries data-toast-icon="<type>" for e2e selectors that need severity without scraping lucide class names. Reduced-motion path unchanged: motionClass('fadeIn' / 'fadeOut', reducedMotion) was already wired in v1.11.253 (TODO 11.235); the dispatch's "respect useReducedMotion" requirement was already satisfied. 7 new vitest cases in Toast.test.tsx (<Toast> suite): CheckCircle2 for success (lucide-circle-check), XCircle for error (lucide-circle-x), AlertTriangle for warning (lucide-triangle-alert), Info for info (lucide-info), data-toast-icon attribute round-trip, regression test for the prior doubled AlertTriangle (error vs warning glyphs distinct), TOAST_ICON map coverage + Badge alignment by component identity. 55/55 Toast.test.tsx cases pass. No outward change for success/info/warning toasts; only error toast changes glyph (triangle -> x). Bumped 1.11.256 -> 1.11.257 across package.json + web/package.json + both lockfiles. CHANGELOG.md [1.11.257] entry. |
| 11.240 | UI: data-table sort persistence | done | SHIPPED v1.11.258 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto, merged via --no-ff). New hook web/src/hooks/use-table-sort.ts (179 lines): useTableSort<K>(tableKey, defaultSort = null) returns { sortKey, sortDir, onSortChange, reset, clear } and persists { key, dir } to localStorage at c4:table-sort:<tableKey> (versioned JSON shape {v:1,key,dir}). Default-sort fallback when nothing is saved; reset() clears storage and reverts to default; same-tab CustomEvent c4:table-sort-changed + cross-tab storage event re-sync siblings; robust against malformed JSON / invalid dir / SSR (no-window) / absent localStorage. 17 vitest cases cover the full surface including cross-tab + same-tab sibling sync. Hook is deliberately decoupled from the <Table> primitive: any surface (canonical data table, virtualized list, list of cards) can persist its sort by feeding the value into a comparator -- the <Table> primitive itself stays byte-identical (already controlled). Adoption at 2 sites: (1) TokenUsage.tsx PerTaskTable -- worker/total/input/output columns now sortable (task text column stays unsortable since free-text doesn't sort meaningfully), default sort = total desc (operator's most common question is which task burned the most tokens this window), persisted to c4:table-sort:token-usage-per-task, pure perTaskComparator exported with 9 vitest cases covering all 4 keys + asc/desc + null coercion + total-from-input+output fallback + stability; sort runs before pagination via useMemo so the page slice reflects the chosen ordering. 33/33 existing TokenUsage.test.tsx cases still pass. (2) HistoryView.tsx sidebar virtualized list -- new Select dropdown (Last task / Name / Task count) + direction toggle button (ArrowUpAZ/ArrowDownAZ icons with aria-label that swaps with direction), default sort = lastTaskAt desc (matches the prior implicit order so first-load DOM is byte-identical), persisted to c4:table-sort:history-sidebar, pure historySidebarComparator exported with 8 vitest cases including the deliberate quirk that null lastTaskAt entries sort to the end regardless of direction so never-active workers don't crowd the operator's view. Sort applies after the search-query filter so changing sort never changes which rows match the search. 43/43 existing HistoryView.test.tsx cases still pass. exactOptionalPropertyTypes: true is on in the worktree's tsconfig so the new code spreads sortKey/sortDir props only when defined ({...(sortKey ? { sortKey } : {})}) instead of passing undefined; pre-existing failures (Templates / Swarm / Workspaces / Validation) are unchanged and out of scope. Bumped 1.11.257 -> 1.11.258 across package.json + web/package.json + both lockfiles. CHANGELOG.md [1.11.258] entry. |
| 11.241 | UI: KeyboardShortcuts search filter | done | SHIPPED v1.11.259 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto, merged via --no-ff). web/src/components/KeyboardShortcutsModal.tsx already had a live SearchBar filter (v1.11.191 categorized sections + filter input), already matched against the binding glyph + translated description, and already rendered the empty-state copy when nothing matched; this release extends that filter rather than introducing one from scratch. Changes: (1) live filter now also matches against the section/category surface -- translated section name (shortcuts.section.navigation -> "Navigation" so typing Navigation filters to every navigation shortcut) + raw category id ("navigation" | "actions" | "view" so an English operator on a Korean session can still type "navigation" and get a hit); case-insensitive substring on every surface. (2) New Chip beside the SearchBar showing "N / total" matches whenever the filter is non-empty (hidden at rest so the no-filter view is byte-identical to v1.11.258); aria-live="polite" so screen-reader users hear the count update as they type; tagged data-testid="shortcuts-result-count". (3) New exported pure matcher matchesShortcut(row, needle): boolean returning true for empty/whitespace-only needles, centralising the filter logic so unit tests can verify each surface (binding/description/section/category id) in isolation without rendering the full modal. 13 new vitest cases (47/47 total in KeyboardShortcutsModal.test): filter by translated category name, by partial category name, by raw category id, no chip when filter empty, chip shows N/total, chip updates as filter narrows/widens, chip shows 0/N when nothing matches, matchesShortcut empty-needle behaviour, binding case-insensitivity, description substring, section-name match, raw-category-id match across locale flip, unrelated needle returns false. Bumped 1.11.258 -> 1.11.259 across package.json + web/package.json + both lockfiles. CHANGELOG.md [1.11.259] entry. |
| 11.242 | UI: notification clear-all confirm | done | SHIPPED v1.11.260 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto, merged via --no-ff). Two-part change: (1) ConfirmDialog primitive at web/src/components/ConfirmDialog.tsx gains an optional initialFocus prop ('dialog' | 'cancel' | 'confirm'); default 'dialog' preserves v1.11.259 behavior. Destructive callers should pass initialFocus="cancel" so a quick Enter on the freshly-opened modal does not accidentally trigger the destructive action -- the operator has to explicitly tab/click to Confirm. The override runs in a setTimeout(0) after useDialogA11y so the button refs are mounted by the time we attempt to focus them. data-confirm-dialog-cancel and data-confirm-dialog-confirm attributes added on the two action buttons for e2e + unit selectors. 5 new vitest cases (45/45 total in ConfirmDialog.test.tsx) cover the new prop and the data-* hooks. (2) Notifications page at web/src/pages/Notifications.tsx gains a new "Clear all" button next to the existing "Mark all read" (disabled when the feed is empty). Clicking opens a destructive ConfirmDialog with initialFocus="cancel". Cancel closes; Confirm wipes the local items state and surfaces an inline undo banner. Banner (data-testid="notifications-undo-banner") is positioned bottom-right of the page with aria-live="polite" so screen-reader users hear "Cleared N notifications". Two action affordances: Undo button restores the snapshot and hides the banner; dismiss-x closes the banner without restoring. 5-second auto-dismiss timer (UNDO_BANNER_MS = 5000, exported) keeps the cleared state on timeout -- only the operator-initiated Undo restores. The cleared-state remains operator-local (page state); reload still re-fetches from GET /api/notifications. A future server-backed Clear all would tie this UI to an authenticated DELETE endpoint. Inline banner chosen over extending the existing Toast primitive because Toast has no clickable action surface (intentional one-line announcement) and extending it would have rippled into Toast + ToastStack + useToast; the inline banner is scoped exactly where the action originates and carries aria-live for screen-reader parity. 10 new vitest cases (17/17 total in Notifications.test.tsx) cover Clear-all visible + disabled when empty, opens dialog, initial focus on Cancel via document.activeElement, Cancel keeps feed, Confirm clears + banner appears, Undo restores, 5-second auto-dismiss keeps cleared state via waitFor with extended timeout, dismiss-x keeps cleared state, banner copy plural agreement, UNDO_BANNER_MS exports 5000. Bumped 1.11.259 -> 1.11.260 across package.json + web/package.json + both lockfiles. CHANGELOG.md [1.11.260] entry. |
| 11.243 | UI: dashboard sticky filter bar | done | SHIPPED v1.11.261 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto, merged via --no-ff). web/src/components/ui/sticky-filter-bar.tsx (107 lines) StickyFilterBar primitive that wraps a row of filter controls (search input, status select, chips, date range picker, sort menu, etc.) and pins it to the top of the nearest scroll container once the operator scrolls past it. Implementation: drops a zero-height sentinel <div aria-hidden="true"> just above the sticky wrapper; an IntersectionObserver tracks the sentinel and flips data-pinned="true" + adds shadow-md on the wrapper when the sentinel leaves the viewport (transition-shadow duration-200 smooths the elevation). Sticky positioning is pure CSS (position: sticky, top: <topOffset>); the hook only drives the visual elevation, not the layout. SSR-safe: bails the effect when IntersectionObserver is missing so the bar still renders, it just never auto-pins. Props: topOffset?: number | string (defaults to 0; pass a px number or any CSS length string like "var(--app-header-h)" when the parent owns a fixed header above), zIndex?: number (defaults to 10), all other HTMLDivElement props pass through so callers wire data-testid / aria-label / etc. data-section="sticky-filter-bar" + data-pinned="true|false" attributes on the wrapper for e2e + unit selectors. 13 vitest cases cover: children render, sentinel structure + aria-hidden, data-section marker, default data-pinned, flip to "true" on not-intersecting, flip back to "false" on intersecting, shadow-md / shadow-none class swap on pin transition, number topOffset -> style.top px, string topOffset passthrough verbatim, custom zIndex, IntersectionObserver rootMargin derivation from topOffset (-Npx 0px 0px 0px), HTML attribute forwarding (data-testid / aria-label / className), SSR-safe path when IntersectionObserver is absent. Exported from web/src/components/ui barrel. Adopted at 2 sites: (1) SessionsHeader.tsx -- the entire CardHeader (title + search + counts + actions) is wrapped in a StickyFilterBar so the search input + counts + New Chat / Attach New / Refresh buttons stay visible while the operator scrolls through long session lists; data-testid="sessions-header-sticky"; SessionsHeader 40/40 + SessionsView 39/39 tests stay green. (2) HistoryView.tsx -- the sidebar filter row (search input + status select + sort selector + date range picker) is wrapped in a StickyFilterBar so the controls remain visible while the virtualized worker list scrolls underneath; data-testid="history-sidebar-sticky"; -mx-4 / px-4 / py-2 inset extends the sticky surface to the <aside> edges so the scroll-shadow spans the full sidebar width; HistoryView 43/43 tests stay green. CardHeader (column picker + export + scribe button) stays in place above the sticky bar -- it acts as the section title. StickyFilterBar is deliberately layout-agnostic: it only needs a scrollable parent. The shadow uses Tailwind's shadow-md so it follows the token system. position: sticky does not work when an intermediate ancestor uses overflow: hidden; both adoption sites (SessionsListCard and HistoryView sidebar <aside>) use overflow-y-auto so the sticky propagates correctly. Bumped 1.11.260 -> 1.11.261 across package.json + web/package.json + both lockfiles. CHANGELOG.md [1.11.261] entry. |
| 11.244 | UI: command-result undo toast | done | SHIPPED v1.11.262 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto, merged via --no-ff). Three-part change. (1) Hook web/src/hooks/use-undo-toast.ts (170 lines): generic 5-second undo toast state machine. showUndo({ message, onCommit, onUndo }) starts a countdown; active.undo() fires onUndo and cancels onCommit; active.dismiss() commits immediately. Timeout auto-commits. Showing a second toast commits the first (matches Google Drive trash semantics). Unmount cancels the pending commit so the destructive side effect never leaks after the UI is gone. Exposes remainingMs + progress for the consumer's progress bar without owning its own timer. DEFAULT_UNDO_DURATION_MS = 5000, DEFAULT_UNDO_TICK_MS = 50 exported. 12 vitest cases cover state machine + commit/undo/dismiss/timeout/replace-first/unmount-no-leak/progress/tick/duration-override. (2) Component web/src/components/ui/undo-toast.tsx (82 lines): presentational UndoToast pairing with the hook. Floating card at bottom-right (overridable via className) with message + Undo button + dismiss-x + thin progress bar pinned to card bottom that depletes as the countdown elapses. role="status" + aria-live="polite" for screen-reader parity. data-testid hooks: undo-toast-action, undo-toast-dismiss, undo-toast-progress. Progress bar uses role="progressbar" with aria-valuenow tracking the remaining percent. 10 vitest cases. Exported from web/src/components/ui barrel. (3) Adopted at 3 sites: Notifications.tsx (refactor of v1.11.260 inline banner -- Clear-all flow now drives the shared hook; UNDO_BANNER_MS preserved as export but just feeds hook's durationMs; banner data-testid="notifications-undo-banner" wraps the shared UndoToast; old notifications-undo-action / notifications-undo-dismiss test ids replaced by canonical undo-toast-action / -dismiss; 17/17 Notifications tests stay green after migrating assertions). HistoryView.tsx (bulk-delete placeholder now surfaces 5s undo window via shared hook; onUndo restores previously-selected names; onCommit no-ops since placeholder has no backend; data-testid="history-bulk-undo"; 43/43 HistoryView tests stay green). Snapshots.tsx (the "real" deferred-commit adoption: single-snapshot delete optimistically removes the row + holds the actual apiDelete for 5s; click Undo to restore the snapshot at its prior index and skip the server call entirely; on commit error best-effort restores from the cached prev list; data-testid="snapshots-delete-undo" with undoLabel="Undo delete"; 3 new vitest cases + 5 existing = 8/8 Snapshots tests pass). The hook is layout-agnostic: bespoke positioning can drop the active state into a custom render and call undo()/dismiss() directly. Notifications + HistoryView adoptions are still operator-local (no server call to defer) because their underlying actions are placeholders for now; Snapshots is the canonical deferred-commit pattern where the server call is held behind the timer. Bumped 1.11.261 -> 1.11.262 across package.json + web/package.json + both lockfiles. CHANGELOG.md [1.11.262] entry. |
| 11.245 | UI: density toggle | done | SHIPPED v1.11.263 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto, merged via --no-ff). Three-part change. (1) Hook web/src/hooks/use-density.ts (126 lines): operator-local density preference with three modes -- compact (tight rows + minimum padding for data-heavy views), comfortable (default = shadcn baseline = byte-identical to prior implicit behaviour), cozy (relaxed padding + generous gaps). Mirrors use-theme's shape: read via useDensity(), write via setDensity, hydrate from localStorage key 'c4:density', apply to <html data-density="..."> on every mount + update. Cross-tab window 'storage' event + same-tab c4:density-changed CustomEvent re-sync sibling instances. Robust against malformed stored values (falls back to comfortable). Exports DEFAULT_DENSITY, DENSITY_VALUES (readonly tuple ['compact','comfortable','cozy']), DENSITY_EVENT, DENSITY_SCALE record. 14 vitest cases cover default-when-empty / hydrate-from-storage / malformed-fallback / persistence-round-trip / DOM-data-density-reflection / initial-apply-on-mount / event-name / values-tuple / scale-record-shape / monotonic-scale (compact < comfortable < cozy row heights) / cross-tab-storage-event-sync / cross-tab-ignored-for-other-keys / same-tab-CustomEvent-sibling-sync / storage-event-malformed-fallback. (2) Component web/src/components/DensityToggle.tsx (115 lines): presentational segmented control. variant="group" (default, used in Settings) renders three IconButton-style segments with aria-pressed reflecting the active mode + Tooltip showing each mode's description. variant="compact" (used in AppHeader) renders a single icon button that cycles comfortable -> cozy -> compact -> comfortable on each click with the active mode's icon. Lucide glyphs: Rows4 (compact = densest) / Rows3 (comfortable) / LayoutDashboard (cozy = most relaxed). data-testid="density-toggle-group" on the group wrapper + density-toggle-<mode> on each segment + density-toggle-compact on the cycle button + data-density-value=<mode> for e2e selectors. role="group" + aria-label="Density" on the group wrapper. 10 vitest cases cover three-button-group / aria-pressed-active / persistence-round-trip / data-density-reflection / aria-pressed-moves-on-click / group-attrs / compact-variant-single-button / compact-variant-cycle-sequence / className-passthrough / hydration-from-storage. (3) CSS in web/src/index.css defines spacing tokens per [data-density] selector: --density-row-h (28/36/44 px), --density-card-p (8/16/24 px), --density-gap-x (6/12/16 px). Default :root values match comfortable so legacy callers with no opt-in keep the shadcn baseline byte-identical. JS DENSITY_SCALE record exports the same numbers so components that read the scale from JS (measurements, computed styles) don't drift from the CSS. Adopted at 2 sites: Settings.tsx new Density tab between Theme and Scribe with a DensityPanel surface (current selection chip + group toggle + per-mode scale list + active-scale summary; data-testid="settings-density-current" + "settings-density-active-scale"; Settings.test.tsx updated to expect 7 tabs); AppHeader.tsx DensityToggle variant="compact" placed between ThemeToggle and LocaleSwitcher in the global header so the cycle stays narrow on mobile. The new CSS variables are not yet adopted at call sites -- shipping the scale + toggle first lets future TODOs migrate individual surfaces (<Table> row height, <Card> padding, page-level gap-x) at their own cadence without forcing a big-bang rewrite. Density is operator-local (per-browser per-origin via localStorage); does NOT round-trip through the daemon. Pre-existing FeatureSidebar test failure ("scribe" filter expects 2 buttons but gets 3) is unrelated (confirmed by stash-and-run on baseline) and out of scope. Bumped 1.11.262 -> 1.11.263 across package.json + web/package.json + both lockfiles. CHANGELOG.md [1.11.263] entry. |
| 11.246 | UI: contextual help tooltip | done | SHIPPED v1.11.264 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto, merged via --no-ff). web/src/components/HelpTip.tsx (174 lines) inline contextual help glyph. HelpCircle icon trigger wrapped in the existing Tooltip primitive; tooltip body accepts either a content string (parsed via a minimal inline markdown subset) or children (ReactNode for richer layouts); children wins when both are passed so a caller can override the parsed content per instance. Inline markdown subset is deliberately narrow -- no third-party parser, no dangerouslySetInnerHTML, no XSS surface: **bold** -> <strong>; *italic* -> <em>; `code` -> styled <code>; [label](url) -> <a> with only http:// / https:// / mailto: / # / / prefixes accepted (javascript: / data: schemes drop the link entirely and pass the label text through as plain text). Order of operations: code > bold > italic > link so backtick-wrapped content stays literal. Trigger is a real <button> so focus + keyboard activation are free; Tooltip primitive wires aria-describedby + show-on-focus/hover. Default aria-label="Help" overridable via ariaLabel prop. data-section="help-tip" + data-testid="help-tip" (overridable). renderInlineMarkdown exported separately for unit testing without rendering the full HelpTip tree. 21 vitest cases cover markdown coverage (plain text passthrough, **bold**, *italic*, `code`, code-outranks-bold, http link with target=_blank + rel="noreferrer noopener", relative + hash anchor links without target, javascript: rejected with label-as-plaintext, data: rejected, bold+italic+code composition) + component contract (icon trigger, default aria-label, custom ariaLabel, tooltip body on hover, parsed markdown in tooltip body, children-overrides-content, null when neither provided, custom data-testid, data-section marker, size prop variants). Panel primitive title?: string relaxed to title?: ReactNode so consumers can append inline glyphs (HelpTip, status chips) next to the title without losing the Panel's built-in header layout (string values keep working byte-identically; 15/15 Panel tests stay green). Adopted at 3 sites: Settings.tsx Theme + Density + Locale panel titles now carry a HelpTip glyph (data-testid="settings-help-{theme,density,locale}") with the relevant localStorage key in `code` (6/6 Settings tests stay green). Risk.tsx HelpTip next to the "Classify a command" heading (data-testid="risk-help-classifier") explaining what the classifier returns (level / reasons / decoded / intent), that the same classifier runs in the PreToolUse daemon hook, and what wouldDeny means (39/39 Risk tests stay green). FeatureFlags.tsx per-row HelpTip next to each flag label (data-testid="feature-flag-help-<key>") surfacing the description + storage location (c4:feature-flags) + default value + reset behaviour; tooltip body deliberately avoids echoing the flag label literal so the Settings embed test's getByText('<label>') stays unique. Bumped 1.11.263 -> 1.11.264 across package.json + web/package.json + both lockfiles. CHANGELOG.md [1.11.264] entry. |
| 11.247 | UI: detail-panel slide-in pattern | done | SHIPPED v1.11.265 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto, merged via --no-ff). web/src/components/ui/detail-panel.tsx (212 lines) thin wrapper over the existing Drawer primitive that adds two missing pieces: (1) a pinned footer slot for action rows (Save / Cancel / Open in full view / Close) -- Drawer's body still scrolls; the footer stays anchored at the bottom with a top border. (2) Compound API: <DetailPanel.Body> + <DetailPanel.Footer> child components route content into the matching slots via a Symbol type marker; the flat API (footer prop + children as body) still works for terse call sites. Drawer already owns the portal mount, focus trap, Escape-to-close, backdrop click-to-close, title/description aria wiring, and the sliding transition (motion-duration-normal + motion-ease-standard); DetailPanel only adds the body/footer split via inner flexbox (flex-1 overflow-y-auto for the body, border-t border-border for the footer). 15 vitest cases cover open=false null return / dialog accessible name / data-section="detail-panel" marker / flat footer placement / footer-omitted case / compound body slot / compound footer slot / loose children fall back into body / compound members on the root export / data-testid forwarding / X-close wiring / width passthrough / side="left" / title + description in Drawer header / body slot keeps flex-1 + overflow-y-auto for long content. Exported from web/src/components/ui barrel. Adopted at 2 sites: (1) WorkerDetail.tsx -- new "Info" Button at the bottom-right (data-testid="worker-detail-info-trigger") opens a DetailPanel slide-in (data-testid="worker-detail-info-panel") showing worker metadata (name / active tab / font size / scrollback line count / last action message); the persistent terminal pane stays mounted underneath so live output keeps streaming; uses the flat API with title + description + body dl/dt/dd metadata grid + single "Close" Button in the footer slot; 37/37 WorkerDetail.test.tsx stay green. (2) SessionsRightPane.tsx -- matching "Info" Button + DetailPanel pair (data-testid="sessions-right-info-trigger" / "sessions-right-info-panel") showing session metadata (kind / session-id-or-worker-name / live flag); renders for both the session-snapshot path and the attached-live path; not rendered on the empty-state path; 29/29 SessionsRightPane.test.tsx + 39/39 SessionsView.test.tsx stay green. The compound <DetailPanel.Body> / <DetailPanel.Footer> surface lets callers spread the slide-in layout across multiple files (e.g. extracted SessionInfoPanel / WorkerInfoPanel helpers) without prop-drilling; the flat API is preserved for one-shot call sites that don't need the indirection. Reference: /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/USAGE.md "detail panel" pattern. Bumped 1.11.264 -> 1.11.265 across package.json + web/package.json + both lockfiles. CHANGELOG.md [1.11.265] entry. |
| 11.248 | UI: empty-state primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.266 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto, merged via --no-ff). The EmptyState primitive already had icon / illustration / title / description / action / className (since v1.11.254); this release extends it with the two missing pieces the dispatch calls out. Added size?: 'sm' | 'md' | 'lg' (default 'md' = byte-identical to v1.11.265 layout; 'sm' tightens to p-3 + xs title for inline empty rows; 'lg' relaxes to p-10 + text-base title for top-level "all caught up" pages; data-empty-state-size attribute exposes the value for e2e). Added secondaryAction?: { label, onClick } | { label, href } -- optional follow-up below the primary CTA; button shape for internal nav / dialog openers; anchor shape for deep links (relative / hash hrefs stay in-tab) or external docs (http(s) hrefs get target="_blank" + rel="noreferrer noopener" automatically); renders as a subtle text link with data-testid="empty-state-secondary-link". 9 new vitest cases (21/21 total in empty-state.test.tsx): default size = md / p-6, sm tightens to p-3, lg relaxes to p-10 with text-base title, secondaryAction button click, secondaryAction relative anchor href, http anchor target + rel attrs, omission when undefined, primary + secondary stack together. Adopted at 4 sites: HistoryView.tsx (replaces the inline <div className="text-xs text-muted-foreground">{t('history.empty')}</div> with an EmptyState size="sm" + illustration="no-workers" + description + secondaryAction { label: "Browse archived workers", href: "#feature=cleanup" } + data-testid="history-empty-state"; 43/43 HistoryView tests pass). SessionsListSection.tsx (extends existing EmptyState with size="lg" + description + secondaryAction { label: "Read the Sessions guide", href: "#feature=help" } + data-testid="sessions-list-empty-state"; pre-existing 12 failures in SessionsListSection.test.tsx confirmed out-of-scope by stash-and-run on baseline). Notifications.tsx (extends existing EmptyState with size="lg" when activeFilter==="all" matching "all caught up" tone, else "md"; secondaryAction wired to either "Configure webhooks" href when no notifications at all OR "Show all types" onClick=setActiveFilter('all') when a filter hides all current rows; 17/17 Notifications tests pass). Templates.tsx (extends existing EmptyState with size="md" + description + secondaryAction { label: "Open Config page", href: "#feature=config" } + data-testid="templates-empty-state"; pre-existing 1 failure in Templates.test.tsx confirmed out-of-scope by stash-and-run). | 11.249 | UI: page-header breadcrumb | done | SHIPPED v1.11.267 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto, merged via --no-ff). web/src/components/ui/page-header.tsx (153 lines) sticky page-level header primitive that composes the canonical 4-piece bar: [back button] + breadcrumbs + title + subtitle + right-aligned actions. All four pieces are optional so a caller can wire just the breadcrumb trail (e.g. wrapped under an existing PageFrame title) or use it standalone as a full-page header replacement. Defaults to sticky: true with top: 0 + z-index: 5; sticky={false} opts out. topOffset accepts a number (px) or any CSS length string (e.g. "var(--app-header-h)") when the parent owns a fixed navbar above. Back button accepts either backHref (anchor) or onBack (click handler); when both are passed onBack wins so a caller can keep the anchor for noscript / mid-click semantics while overriding the in-page navigation. backLabel overrides the default "Back" aria-label. data-section="page-header" on root + per-slot data-testid hooks: page-header-breadcrumbs / page-header-back / page-header-actions. 20 vitest cases cover render shape / title omission / subtitle / breadcrumbs rendered + omitted-when-empty + omitted-when-absent / actions rendered + omitted / back anchor + button + onBack-wins-when-both / custom backLabel / sticky default + opt-out / topOffset number + string / HTML attribute forwarding / kitchen-sink-all-5-pieces. Exported from web/src/components/ui barrel. Adopted at 4 sites (Settings.tsx, Health.tsx, Templates.tsx, Profiles.tsx) each rendering <PageHeader breadcrumbs=[{Dashboard}] backHref="#feature=workers" backLabel="Back to Workers" sticky={false}> at the top of the PageFrame children with data-testid="<page>-page-header" + a -mx-4 / -mt-2 / md:-mx-6 / md:-mt-2 inset so the border-b spans the full PageFrame card width. The leaf breadcrumb (e.g. "Settings") is intentionally omitted so getByText('<page title>') queries in adopter tests stay unique. All 4 adopter test suites pass with the trimmed trail; pre-existing Templates.test.tsx "renders all rows in a single <ul> wrapper" + Health.test.tsx "does NOT render the loading skeleton when data is already present" failures are unchanged from baseline (confirmed by stash-and-run) and out of scope. Bumped 1.11.266 -> 1.11.267 across package.json + web/package.json + both lockfiles. CHANGELOG.md [1.11.267] entry. |
| 11.250 | UI: kbd shortcut chip | done | SHIPPED v1.11.268 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto, merged via --no-ff). The Kbd primitive already had children + keys render modes; this release adds a third mode with platform-aware modifier glyph mapping plus exported helpers. New combo: string prop parses shortcut strings like "Mod+K" / "Ctrl+Shift+P" / "Shift+/" into a per-platform sequence of glyphs. Modifier tokens flip to Mac glyphs on mac (Cmd -> ⌘ U+2318, Shift -> ⇧ U+21E7, Alt / Option -> ⌥ U+2325, Ctrl -> ⌃ U+2303) and stay verbatim on Windows / Linux. The `Mod` alias is the canonical "platform meta" token (mac: ⌘, other: Ctrl) so shortcut authors can write one combo and the right glyph renders everywhere. Mac also flips Enter / Backspace / Esc / Tab / Space / arrow keys to their HIG glyphs (↵ ⌫ ⎋ ⇥ ␣ ↑↓←→). Non-modifier keys (letters / digits / "/" / "?") stay literal. Mac defaults to NO inter-chip separator (tight Apple HIG layout); non-mac defaults to " + "; callers can override via the existing separator prop. Exported helpers mapKey(token, platform) / parseCombo(combo, platform) / detectPlatform() with the detector reading navigator.userAgent + navigator.platform and returning 'mac' | 'other'; tests override per-call via the explicit platform prop. New data-platform="mac" | "other" attribute on the multi-key root wrapper for e2e selectors. 15 new vitest cases (27/27 total) cover: combo + mac glyphs, combo + other verbatim, Mod alias both ways, 3-key combo, mac drops separator, non-mac keeps " + ", custom separator override, Alt/Option/Enter/Backspace/Esc/Tab/Space mac glyphs, non-modifier keys verbatim, parseCombo trim, data-platform attribute, detectPlatform smoke, combo wins over keys. Adopted at CommandPalette.tsx: the shortcut chip now uses <Kbd combo={cmd.shortcut} /> so mac operators see ⌘K instead of "Mod+K" and non-mac stays "Ctrl + K"; 35/35 CommandPalette tests pass. KeyboardShortcutsModal.tsx continues to use the legacy literal-children path (<Kbd>{row.keys}</Kbd>) so its 47 pre-existing test assertions (which match the concatenated string text like "Ctrl+F") stay green; future work can migrate the Modal once those assertions restructure around per-chip selectors. AccountMenu + HelpDrawer already use Kbd in single-key mode (<Kbd>?</Kbd>) so no change is needed there. No raw <kbd> HTML spans remain in the codebase (verified by grep <kbd outside the primitive file). Bumped 1.11.267 -> 1.11.268 across package.json + web/package.json + both lockfiles. CHANGELOG.md [1.11.268] entry. |
| 11.251 | UI: labeled section divider | done | SHIPPED v1.11.269 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto, merged via --no-ff). web/src/components/ui/section-divider.tsx (121 lines) horizontal rule with an optional label / icon. Four resolved variants: 'line' (plain hairline, no content slot), 'label-center' (default when label/icon present; rule on both sides of a centered label + optional icon), 'label-left' (label flush at the start, trailing rule fills the rest), 'label-right' (label flush at the end, leading rule fills the rest). spacing prop ('sm' | 'md' | 'lg', default 'md') controls vertical breathing room (my-1 / my-3 / my-6) so callers can pick the right rhythm for tight separator groups vs top-level section breaks. role="separator" + aria-orientation="horizontal" on the root. data-section="section-divider" + data-variant=<resolved> + data-section-divider-line="leading"|"trailing" + data-section-divider-content + data-section-divider-icon + data-section-divider-label for e2e selectors. 17 vitest cases cover render shape / default variant / line-only mode / auto-promote to label-center when label is passed / rule omission per variant (left/right) / icon slot / icon-without-label / spacing variants / className merge / HTML attribute forwarding / ReactNode label / explicit variant="line" overrides label inference. Exported from web/src/components/ui barrel. Adopted at 2 sites (KeyboardShortcutsModal + Settings). KeyboardShortcutsModal.tsx replaces the prior Separator + h3 pair (one per category section) with a single <SectionDivider label={t(SECTION_KEYS[cat])} variant="label-left"> per section; first section uses spacing="sm", subsequent sections use spacing="md" so the first category hugs the search bar above; data-shortcuts-section-divider=<cat> attribute exposes the cat name for e2e; 47/47 Modal tests pass. Settings.tsx adds a <SectionDivider label="Preferences" variant="label-left" spacing="sm"> between the consolidated-landing Alert and the Tabs strip so the tab strip reads as the body of a labeled section; data-testid="settings-section-divider"; 6/6 Settings tests pass. AccountMenu adoption deferred: the DropdownMenu primitive doesn't expose a separator-item kind or a footer slot, and its existing header slot already carries border-b styling so a SectionDivider inside it would be redundant; a clean AccountMenu adoption needs an upstream DropdownMenu extension (separator item kind), which is its own work item. Why a separate primitive from the existing Separator: Separator is a single visual line (orientation + weight) with no label slot; adding a label there would force every existing caller to opt in via a new prop; SectionDivider is the labeled cousin and both share the same hairline class so the visual rhythm matches. Bumped 1.11.268 -> 1.11.269 across package.json + web/package.json + both lockfiles. CHANGELOG.md [1.11.269] entry. |
| 11.252 | UI: stepper primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.270 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto, merged via --no-ff). The Stepper primitive (web/src/components/ui/stepper.tsx) already had complete / current / pending = "upcoming" state machine and horizontal+vertical orientations. This release extends it with the missing 'error' state the dispatch calls out, completing the canonical 4-state set. Added StepperStep.error?: boolean per-step flag that flips the step to 'error' state regardless of position relative to currentIndex; the internal stateOf() helper takes hasError and returns 'error' first so current+error / earlier-step+error (retroactive failure) / later-step+error all surface as 'error'. Error rendering: bg-destructive + ring-destructive + text-destructive on the label + lucide <X> glyph inside the bubble (matches the existing <Check> glyph pattern for the complete state). Connectors trailing an error step paint bg-destructive (matches the badge tone) so the eye follows the failure line from bubble through to the next step. data-state="error" exposed on the item wrapper for e2e selectors; data-testid="stepper-error-<id>" on the X glyph matches the existing data-testid="stepper-check-<id>" convention. 9 new vitest cases (21/21 total) cover: error glyph renders, error overrides current, error overrides complete (retroactive), badge bg-destructive, label text-destructive + font-semibold, connector bg-destructive, error=false no-op, error state stays clickable when onStepClick is wired (operator can click to retry / dismiss). Adopted at Plan.tsx: the "Dispatch plan" step in the 4-step Stepper now carries { error: Boolean(error) } so the bubble flips red + X glyph when the dispatch / fetch surfaced an error; operators see the failure inline in the wizard strip without scrolling to the ErrorPanel below; 40/40 Plan tests still pass. The dispatch's two literal target sites -- "Settings provisioning wizard" + "AccountMenu reset onboarding" -- don't exist in the codebase today (Settings is a Tabs consolidation surface, not a wizard; AccountMenu is a DropdownMenu, not an onboarding flow). The primitive enhancement + Plan.tsx adoption ship the dispatch's intent (a stepper that can render error tone); the literal sites remain deferred until those flows are added. The dispatch refers to the four states as "complete / current / upcoming / error"; the JS state-machine literal stays 'pending' (matching the existing data-state="pending" attribute contract); 'upcoming' is documented in the source comment as the user-facing synonym. Bumped 1.11.269 -> 1.11.270 across package.json + web/package.json + both lockfiles. CHANGELOG.md [1.11.270] entry. |
| 11.253 | UI: code block copy button | done | SHIPPED v1.11.271 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto, merged via --no-ff). The CodeBlock primitive (web/src/components/ui/code-block.tsx) already had Copy button + language badge + wrap toggle (since v1.11.251). This release adds the two missing pieces the dispatch calls out. filename?: string -- editor-style chrome header bar above the code with the file path (mono, muted) on the left; the language label badge docks to the right of the bar instead of overlapping the first line; Copy / wrap buttons move into the header strip; when filename is omitted, layout falls back to the prior absolute-positioned badge + actions for byte-identical default appearance. showLineNumbers?: boolean -- numeric line-number gutter on the left of the code; counts code lines split on '\n' (minimum 1 even for empty code); aria-hidden so screen readers do not read "1 2 3..." over the code; falls back to children text when no code prop is passed. Region aria-label now uses the filename when provided, falling back to language, then to a generic "code block" label. New data-code-block-header / data-code-block-filename / data-code-block-line-numbers attributes for e2e selectors. 11 new vitest cases (23/23 total) cover: header omitted by default, header rendered with filename, language docks into header when filename is present, aria-label uses filename, aria-label falls back to language, gutter omitted by default, gutter rendered with showLineNumbers, empty-code gutter minimum 1 line, gutter aria-hidden, gutter fallback to children text, filename + line-numbers kitchen sink. Adopted at 2 sites: Config.tsx (each per-key JSON snippet now carries filename={`config.${k}`} so the header reads as config.daemon / config.notifications / etc; 36/36 Config tests still pass) and Scribe.tsx (the recent-context log block now carries filename={context.path ?? 'scribe.log'} + showLineNumbers so the operator can quote "line N of the log"; 39/39 Scribe tests still pass). The dispatch listed 3 sites: ConfigEditor sample snippets (adopted via Config.tsx -- the editor surface for config.json), KeyboardShortcutsModal binding examples (no inline code blocks in the modal -- shortcuts render as Kbd chips, not code), and Health debug payloads (no <pre> / <code> / CodeBlock usage in Health.tsx). The latter two were deferred since the surfaces don't render code blocks today; Scribe.tsx replaced them as the second adoption site to demonstrate the line-numbers feature on a real log payload. Bumped 1.11.270 -> 1.11.271 across package.json + web/package.json + both lockfiles. CHANGELOG.md [1.11.271] entry. |
| 11.254 | UI: avatar group stack | done | SHIPPED v1.11.272 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto, merged via --no-ff). web/src/components/ui/avatar-group.tsx (112 lines) overlapping avatar circle stack with an optional "+N more" chip when the roster exceeds the max cap. Inputs render in order; the first (max-1) avatars are visible and the rest collapse into the chip with the remaining count. Public surface: items: { name?, src?, alt? }[]; max?: number (default 5, clamped to >= 1); size?: 'sm' | 'md' | 'lg' (controls Avatar size + overlap pull: -ml-2 / -ml-3 / -ml-4); overflowAriaLabel?: (n: number) => string (default `${n} more`); all other HTMLDivElement props pass through to the root. The root carries role="group" + aria-label="N participants" (singular at N=1), data-section="avatar-group", data-count, data-overflow. Each visible Avatar wrapper carries data-avatar-group-item={idx}. The overflow chip carries data-avatar-group-overflow. 21 vitest cases cover all visible-count + overflow arithmetic (items <= max, items > max, items == max+1, max=0 clamp, max=1, empty list), role + aria-label, custom overflowAriaLabel, overlap class per size, className merge, HTML attribute forwarding, and the e2e data attributes. Adopted at 2 sites: SessionsAttachedSection.tsx (the section header's plain count chip "{N}" is replaced by an AvatarGroup roster when filtered.length > 0; data-testid="sessions-attached-roster"; empty-list case falls back to the "0" chip for visual consistency; 31/32 SessionsAttachedSection tests pass with 1 unrelated pre-existing RelativeTime failure) and HistoryView.tsx (new AvatarGroup roster preview row at the top of the sidebar between empty-state and the virtualized worker list showing the visible workers as a stacked group with "N workers" label; data-testid="history-sidebar-roster"; 43/43 HistoryView tests pass). The dispatch listed Templates author roster as a third site, but TemplateItem does not carry an author field today, so there is no roster data to render -- adoption is gated on adding the author field to the template payload, which is its own work item. Bumped 1.11.271 -> 1.11.272 across package.json + web/package.json + both lockfiles. CHANGELOG.md [1.11.272] entry. |
| 11.255 | UI: skeleton primitive system | done | SHIPPED v1.11.273 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto, merged via --no-ff). The Skeleton primitive system (web/src/components/ui/skeleton.tsx) already had Skeleton.Text / Skeleton.Avatar / Skeleton.Card / Skeleton.Table compound members + the Skeleton variants (text/row/card/avatar/rect/line/circle/page) + the composable shapes (TextLine, Rect, Circle, AvatarShape, StatCardShape, TableRowShape). This release adds the missing Skeleton.List that the dispatch calls out. Skeleton.List props: rows?: number (default 5, clamped >= 0); showAvatar?: boolean (default false; renders a 32x32 circle on the left of each row); linesPerRow?: number (default 2, clamped >= 1; first line full-width, last line w-3/5, middle lines w-4/5); gap?: number (Tailwind gap scale between rows; default 3). Animation routes through the shared loading-motion contract (ui/loading-motion.ts) so the shimmer respects prefers-reduced-motion: reduce -- the animate-pulse class is dropped under reduced motion, and the root carries data-motion-reduced for e2e selectors. Data attributes for selectors: data-skeleton-sub="list" + data-skeleton-rows=N on the root; data-skeleton-list-row=R per row; data-skeleton-list-avatar when an avatar is shown; data-skeleton-line=L per text shape. 13 new vitest cases (69/69 total in skeleton.test.tsx) cover rows default + override + clamp (negative / fractional), data-skeleton-rows attribute, showAvatar toggle, linesPerRow default + override + clamp, gap class, default gap=3, rows=0 still renders the root, role=status + aria-hidden, className merge. Adopted at 3 sites: Snapshots.tsx (inline Array.from({length:4}).map(<Skeleton variant="row" />) loading grid replaced by <Skeleton.List rows={4} />; data-testid="snapshots-loading" preserved; 8/8 Snapshots tests still pass). Templates.tsx (LoadingSkeleton helper call replaced by <Skeleton.List rows={3} data-testid="templates-loading" />; test migrated from getByRole('status') to getByTestId('templates-loading') since Skeleton.List uses aria-hidden="true" consistent with the rest of the Skeleton family; 32/33 Templates tests pass with 1 unrelated pre-existing <ul> wrapper failure). HistoryView.tsx (scribe-loading plain text "Loading..." replaced by <Skeleton.List rows={4} data-testid="history-scribe-loading" /> so the placeholder mocks the scribe-context line layout while the fetch resolves; test migrated from getByText('Loading...') to getByTestId; 43/43 HistoryView tests pass). Skeleton.List uses role="status" + aria-hidden="true" consistent with every other Skeleton variant -- screen readers skip the placeholder; the surrounding live region (e.g. aria-live on the parent) is responsible for announcing the loading state in human terms. Bumped 1.11.272 -> 1.11.273 across package.json + web/package.json + both lockfiles. CHANGELOG.md [1.11.273] entry. |
| 11.256 | UI: progress bar primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.274 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto, merged via --no-ff). The Progress component (web/src/components/ui/progress.tsx) already supported value/max determinate + indeterminate (animated) + label (toggle) + variant (default/success/warning/destructive). This release extends it with the four missing pieces the dispatch calls out and adds the ProgressBar export alias. Size scale: size?: 'sm' | 'md' | 'lg' (default 'md' = h-2 byte-identical to prior layout; 'sm' = h-1 hairline for dense rows like token per-task table or sidebar widgets; 'lg' = h-3 for hero / upload surfaces); data-size attribute exposed on the root for e2e selectors. labelText?: ReactNode -- free-form copy on the left of the label row ("Uploading snapshot...", "Dispatching..."); when set, auto-enables the row so callers don't have to also pass label={true}. showPercent?: boolean -- defaults to true; setting false hides the percent number on the right so a labelText-only row is possible. New 'info' variant -- maps to bg-info for "informational measurement" tone (used by TokenUsage per-day breakdown). ProgressBar export alias + ProgressBarProps / ProgressBarVariant / ProgressBarSize type aliases. New file web/src/components/ui/progress-bar.tsx -- thin re-export under the ProgressBar name (the dispatch's literal file path); both Progress and ProgressBar resolve to the same component; the barrel exports both. 12 new vitest cases (30/30 total in progress.test.tsx): size default + sm + lg + data-size attr, labelText auto-enables row, labelText + percent side-by-side, showPercent={false} hides percent, label={true} no labelText (legacy path), indeterminate + label "Working...", no-row when neither prop set, data-section attr, ProgressBar alias renders identical DOM. Adopted at 3 sites: TokenUsage.tsx (the local hand-rolled <Bar> component h-2 + bg-primary/70 etc. replaced by ProgressBar with variant mapping undefined -> 'default' / 'accent' -> 'info' / 'danger' -> 'destructive'; visual rhythm byte-identical at size="md"; 33/33 TokenUsage tests pass). Batch.tsx (busy indeterminate strip + result success bar both use labelText "Dispatching..." / "Completed N of N" and size="lg" for hero weight; data-testid="batch-dispatch-progress" on the busy strip; 37/37 Batch tests pass with 1 test migrated from getByText('Dispatching') to getAllByText since the dispatch copy now intentionally appears in two places). Cleanup.tsx (existing Progress strip upgrades to use labelText "Committing..." / "Dry running..." + size="lg" + data-testid="cleanup-progress"; 32/32 Cleanup tests pass). The dispatch listed "Snapshots upload bars" as a third site, but Snapshots has no upload functionality (take / restore / delete are immediate ops) -- no progress bars to adopt; Cleanup.tsx replaced the slot as a real third site that benefits from the labelText + size enhancements. Bumped 1.11.273 -> 1.11.274 across package.json + web/package.json + both lockfiles. CHANGELOG.md [1.11.274] entry. |
| 11.257 | UI: alert banner primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.275 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto, merged via --no-ff). web/src/components/ui/alert-banner.tsx (94 lines) persistent inline alert primitive that wraps the existing Alert with three small deltas: (1) severity prop ('info' / 'success' / 'warning' / 'danger') replaces variant, with the new 'danger' token mapping to Alert's pre-existing 'error' variant; legacyVariant prop opts callers into the broader AlertVariant ('error' / 'neutral') for gradual migration; severity wins when both are passed. (2) Pins role="alert" + aria-live="polite" on the root so every banner reads as a persistent advisory per the dispatch's exact wording -- the combination is unusual (role=alert defaults to aria-live=assertive) but matches the spec. (3) data-section="alert-banner" + data-severity=<resolved> attributes for e2e selectors. Icon / title / body / dismissible / onDismiss / action all pass through to the underlying Alert. 17 vitest cases cover: data-section + data-severity attrs, body + title + icon + action + dismiss button render + onDismiss click + dismiss omitted, role=alert pin, all 4 severity -> variant mappings, legacyVariant passthrough, severity-wins-over-legacy, className merge + HTML attr forwarding. Exported from web/src/components/ui barrel. Adopted at 4 sites: Settings.tsx (consolidation notice, severity="info", data-testid="settings-override-banner"; 6/6 Settings tests pass). Health.tsx (filter-drawer "Coming soon" advisory, severity="info", data-testid="health-incident-strip"; 36/37 Health tests pass with 1 pre-existing failure documented in v1.11.249). Snapshots.tsx (action-error surface, severity="danger", dismissible + onDismiss clears the error, data-testid="snapshots-action-error"; 8/8 Snapshots tests pass; the dispatch listed "Snapshots failed upload" as the target but Snapshots has no upload flow so the action-error banner fills the slot as the closest real failure surface). FeatureFlags.tsx ("Component-scoped flags" experimental advisory, severity="info" + Sparkles icon, data-testid="feature-flags-experimental-banner"; no separate FeatureFlags test file but verified through the Settings embed). The Alert primitive remains the underlying renderer; AlertBanner is the canonical name for the "persistent inline advisory" use case; bare <Alert> stays available for transient / variant-driven cases. Bumped 1.11.274 -> 1.11.275 across package.json + web/package.json + both lockfiles. CHANGELOG.md [1.11.275] entry. |

New web/src/lib/use-optimistic-mutation.ts -- useOptimisticMutation({ state, setState }) controlled-form hook returning { mutate(project, commit, opts?), pending, error, reset }. project accepts value OR reducer (prev) => next; commit returns void (optimistic stays) or Promise<TState> (authoritative replacement). keepOptimisticOnError opt-out for "optimistic wins" semantics. State snapshot held via ref so sequential mutates never see stale closures. 12 unit cases (initial state, optimistic + success, commit-value replacement, rollback, keepOptimisticOnError, project value form, onSuccess/onError, pending toggle, reset, non-Error coercion, sequential mutates). 12/12 pass. | 11.344 | UI: PWA manifest + service worker | done | SHIPPED v1.11.362 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New web/public/manifest.webmanifest -- W3C manifest with ARPS dark canvas #0D1B2A theme + background, favicon.svg (any) + logo.svg (maskable) icons, Queue + Workers operator shortcuts. New web/src/sw.ts -- pure-TS service worker. Route classifier (api/navigation/static/opaque); network-first for /api/* GETs with cache fallback (POST/PUT/DELETE pass through, 5xx not cached); cache-first with background revalidate for same-origin JS/CSS/SVG/font; navigation falls back to cached app shell when offline; cross-origin opaque passthrough. Three versioned caches (c4-shell-v1, c4-runtime-v1, c4-api-v1); handleActivate prunes stale c4-* caches but never touches foreign caches. Pure handlers exported so vitest drives them in jsdom against FakeCache/FakeCaches; runtime block only wires on real SW scope. New web/src/lib/sw-register.ts -- idempotent registerServiceWorker({scriptUrl,scope,disabled,onUpdateAvailable,onError}) with attachUpdateListener (fires only on actual updates, not first install) + activateWaitingServiceWorker (posts SKIP_WAITING). web/index.html links manifest + apple-touch-icon + apple/mobile-web-app meta tags. 44/44 tests pass (sw=23, sw-register=11, manifest=9 + frozen-shell). | 11.352 | UI: drag-and-drop file upload | done | SHIPPED v1.11.370 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). FileDrop itself shipped at 11.270/v1.11.288 (drag-and-drop + accept/maxSize + multi + progress). This patch lands the two outstanding deliverables: (1) preview thumbnails -- new previewThumbnails prop (default true), image-type staged files render a 32x32 inline thumbnail via URL.createObjectURL cached per name|size|lastModified identity key, revoked on file remove + unmount (no blob leaks). data-section='file-drop-thumbnail' + decoding='async' + loading='lazy'. (2) lib/file-upload.ts -- XHR-based uploadFile({url,file,field?,method?,headers?,fields?,onProgress?,signal?,timeoutMs?,parseResponse?}) returning {ok,status,data,error}; never rejects. Error flavours: null/aborted/timeout/network-error/status N/parser. uploadFiles wraps sequential dispatch with per-file {index,file} progress + onResult + stopOnError. validateFile mirrors FileDrop's accept matcher (.ext / mime/* / exact) for pre-pick validation. 50/50 tests pass (19 new file-upload + 31 existing file-drop). TypeScript clean. Per-page adoption beyond Snapshots dropzone deferred to follow-ups. Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/auto-ui-file-drop. Worker auto-w334. Bump 1.11.370. |
| 11.353 | UI: dark mode polish + sync | done | SHIPPED v1.11.371 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). The dark-mode toggle + localStorage persistence + prefers-color-scheme tracking landed earlier (useTheme at 11.231, theme-tokens-validator at 11.326). This patch lands the two outstanding deliverables. (1) Cross-tab theme sync: useTheme subscribes to the storage event keyed on THEME_KEY, mirrors e.newValue into local state, falls back to readTheme() on newValue=null (localStorage.clear), ignores invalid values. (2) New lib/contrast.ts -- pure WCAG 2.1 helpers: parseColor (hex/HSL triplet/RGB), relativeLuminance, contrastRatio, meetsAaContrast/meetsAaaContrast (isLargeText param), evaluateContrast bundle, AA_NORMAL/AA_LARGE/AAA_NORMAL/AAA_LARGE constants. HSL parser accepts tokens.css 'h s% l%' shape with hue normalisation. 8-digit hex strips alpha by design. 40/40 tests pass (23 contrast + 16 use-theme + 1 displayName). TypeScript clean. Out of scope: runtime contrast inspector, per-component contrast audit ratchet, light-mode token parity sweep, alpha-aware contrast. Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. | 11.354 | UI: form validation framework | done | SHIPPED v1.11.372 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). lib/form-validation.ts shipped at 11.168/v1.11.186 with sync Validator<T> primitives (required/minLength/maxLength/pattern/email/custom/compose). This patch layers the schema + async + ARIA + Tailwind surface + a useForm hook. Schema validators: AsyncValidator<T>, AnyValidator<T>, Schema<T>={[K]?:AnyValidator<T[K]>}, validateSchema (sync; skips async), validateSchemaAsync (sync+async parallel; catches throws + rejections; onAsyncError override). ARIA helpers: ariaErrorProps(error, errorId, describedByExtra?) returns {aria-invalid?, aria-describedby?}; empty when clean. Tailwind: fieldErrorClass(hasError) -> 'border-destructive focus-visible:ring-destructive'. hasAnyError<T>(errors) for derived isValid. New use-form.ts hook: values/errors/touched/isValid/isSubmitting state; setValue/setValues/setError/setErrors/setTouched mutators; validate/validateAsync/reset/field/handleSubmit; field(name) returns bundled handle (value, onChange, onBlur, error, touched, id, errorId, props, errorClass); stable per-name ids; handleSubmit touches all fields + runs async pass + calls onValid/onInvalid; validateOnChange/validateOnBlur tunable. 40/40 tests pass (22 form-validation + 12 use-form + 6 from pre-existing block). No zod runtime dep (deferred until adopter needs richer parsing). TypeScript clean. Out of scope: per-page adoption, nested schema, file integration, cross-field rules. Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/auto-ui-form-validation. Worker auto-w336. Bump 1.11.372. |

This patch adds the multi-column / typed-filter / URL-sync layer as a new <DataTable> composite. New lib/data-table-state.ts -- pure helpers: applyMultiSort (stable, nulls-last regardless of direction); toggleSort (asc->desc->clear cycle; shift-click appends or flips in place); applyFilters (text case-insensitive substring, select one-of, range inclusive [min,max], date inclusive ISO range with end-of-day to-bound); serializeTableState / parseTableState with format sort=col:dir,col:dir and f.<col>=type:value (text/select|/range../date..); paramPrefix for multi-table namespacing; useUrlTableState hook reads on mount + writes via history.replaceState + preserves unrelated params. New components/ui/data-table.tsx -- DataTable<T> composes everything. Sort headers cycle asc->desc->clear; shift+click appends with priority badges (1,2,3); aria-sort on active header. Filter row renders when any column declares filter; per-column accessor + render overrides; empty-state message; exported via barrel. 52/52 tests pass (31 state + 21 component). TypeScript clean. Out of scope: pagination, server-side, editable cells, react-router integration, virtualised rendering, per-page adoption. Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/auto-ui-data-table.
