| 11.407 | UI: detail-list primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.425 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/detail-list.tsx` ships `<DetailList>` -- key-value pair list rendered as native `<dl>`/`<dt>`/`<dd>` so definition-list semantics flow into AT without an ARIA shim; role=list/listitem layered on top for defensive guards. API: `items: DetailListItem[]` (`{ id, label, value, copyValue?, copyable?, ariaLabel? }`); `orientation: 'horizontal'|'vertical'` (default 'vertical'); `size: 'sm'|'md'|'lg'` (default 'md'); `showCopyOnHover` (default true); `copyLabel(item)` override; `onCopy(item, text)`; `copyFeedbackMs` (default 1500); `emptyLabel` (default '(no details)'). Orientations: vertical = label on top (small uppercase tracked muted) + value below; horizontal = label left ~1/3 width on md+, value right-aligned, collapses to vertical on mobile (<md) so long labels never crowd values. Copy-on-hover: copy button always in DOM (row height stable) but opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100 + focus-visible:opacity-100 (keyboard reveals); click writes via navigator.clipboard.writeText AND fires onCopy(item, text) regardless of clipboard support; success flips icon to Check + row data-copied="true" for copyFeedbackMs then back. Copyable resolution: copyable:true wins; copyable:false wins; otherwise copyValue (string) > value (string/number/bigint coerced) > null; ReactNode values without copyValue are not copyable; copyable:true paired with non-string-coercible value + no copyValue still hides button (no text to write). Empty list: data-empty="true" + emptyLabel rendered + aria-label retained. Pure helpers exported: `getDetailListCopyText(item) -> string|null` (copyValue wins; string/number/bigint coerce; ReactNode -> null); `isDetailListItemCopyable(item) -> boolean` (mirrors hide-the-button gate). ARIA: root `<dl>` role=list + aria-label; per-row role=listitem; native dt/dd preserved; copy button aria-label "Copy <label>" with item.ariaLabel override + copyLabel function override. Data attrs throughout (data-section + data-orientation + data-size + data-empty + data-item-count on root; data-section + data-detail-id + data-orientation + data-copyable + data-copied per row; data-section per dt/dd/value-text/copy/copy-icon/copy-check/empty). forwardRef forwarded to dl root (HTMLDListElement). 36 new test cases in detail-list.test.tsx (5 getDetailListCopyText + 3 isDetailListItemCopyable + 28 component); 36/36 pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for touched files. Exported via components/ui barrel. Out of scope (documented): inline edit on value cell (separate primitive `<EditableDetailList>` or per-row renderValue slot follow-on), per-row badges/icons next to label (ReactNode label can inline; structured badge slot deferred), sort/reorder (source-array order canonical), group/section headers between rows (follow-on if adopter hits limit), custom copy icon (currently fixed lucide Copy/Check; future copyIcon/copiedIcon ReactNode props). Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/auto-ui-detail-list. Worker auto-w389. Bump 1.11.425. |
| 11.408 | UI: action-menu primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.426 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/action-menu.tsx` ships `<ActionMenu>` -- page-level action bar with primary/secondary action grouping, overflow handling via More button, single-tabstop WAI-ARIA toolbar pattern. API: `actions: ActionMenuItem[]` (`ActionMenuAction { id, label, onClick, icon?, variant?: 'primary'|'secondary'|'destructive', disabled?, shortcut?, ariaLabel? }` or `ActionMenuSeparator { id, type: 'separator' }`); `maxVisible` (default 3); `align: 'start'|'end'` (default 'end'); `overflowLabel` (default 'More'); `ariaLabel` (default 'Page actions'). Pure helper `partitionActionMenu(items, maxVisible) -> { visible, overflow }`: when action count (separators do NOT count) <= maxVisible all visible; else first maxVisible actions visible + rest overflow; trailing separators in visible + leading in overflow are dropped to avoid orphan dividers; empty input -> empty partitions; non-finite/negative maxVisible coerces to 0. Overflow menu: More button toggles popover (role=menu + aria-label='<ariaLabel> overflow'); items render as role=menuitem; click fires onClick + closes + returns focus to More; outside click closes; Escape closes + returns focus; aria-haspopup=menu + aria-expanded mirrors open state. Toolbar contract: root role=toolbar + aria-orientation=horizontal + aria-label; single-tabstop pattern (one VISIBLE action tabIndex=0 at a time, rest -1); ArrowLeft/Right cycle wrap; Home/End jump first/last; disabled actions + separators skipped in focus cycle. Variants: primary (filled brand) / secondary (outlined, default) / destructive (filled destructive); variant IS the contract (no arbitrary class smuggling). Disabled actions flip native disabled + data-disabled + strip onClick + cursor-not-allowed + opacity-50; skipped in focus cycle. align prop mirrors data-align. Pure helpers exported: `isActionMenuSeparator(item)` type guard + `partitionActionMenu(items, maxVisible)`. Data attrs throughout (data-section + data-align + data-visible-count + data-overflow-count + data-overflow-open on root; data-section + data-action-id + data-action-variant + data-disabled per action; role=separator + aria-orientation=vertical + data-section on inline separator; data-section on each overflow sub-node). forwardRef forwarded to root toolbar div. 41 new test cases in action-menu.test.tsx (2 isActionMenuSeparator + 8 partitionActionMenu + 31 component); 41/41 pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for touched files (pre-existing list-action-menu TS error unchanged). Exported via components/ui barrel. Out of scope (documented): per-action keyboard binding (shortcut is decorative kbd; host binds hotkey), resize-aware auto maxVisible (caller computes from container width and passes via prop; automatic measurement follow-on), multi-row overflow drawer variant (popover-only for v1), nested overflow sub-menus, drag-to-reorder (source-array canonical). Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/auto-ui-action-menu. Worker auto-w390. Bump 1.11.426. |
| 11.409 | UI: time-picker primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.427 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/time-picker.tsx` ships `<TimePicker>` -- three-field time picker (HH:MM:SS) with optional AM/PM toggle for 12-hour mode; each field native `<input>` carrying role=spinbutton + aria-valuemin/max/now/text reflecting padded current value (SR announces "Hours 12 of 23"). Modes: '24' (default, hours 0..23) and '12' (hours 1..12 + AM/PM button right of seconds; aria-pressed mirrors period==='PM'). Spinbutton inputs: mid-typing shows raw digits, blur snaps back to 2-digit pad; per-field aria-label override props. Keyboard: ArrowUp/Down increment/decrement focused field with wrap-around at bounds; step prop applies to minutes/seconds only (hours always by 1); ArrowUp/Down on AM/PM toggle flips period. Paste: onPaste on root passes clipboard text through pure helper parseTimeString(text, mode); accepts HH:MM, HH:MM:SS, HH.MM (dot separator), 'HH:MM AM/PM' (case-insensitive + a.m./p.m. dotted), 24-hour input in 12-mode auto-derives PM (15:30 -> 3:30 PM), 00:00 -> 12:00 AM; invalid pastes fall through to default. Wrap-around math via stepTimeField(current, delta, min, max) normalized through modulo across `range = max - min + 1` (e.g., stepTimeField(59, 5, 0, 59) -> 4 because 59+5=64; 64%60=4); 12-hour hour wrap (12->1 / 1->12); non-finite current -> min. Disabled/readOnly disable inputs + AM/PM toggle; data-disabled/data-read-only mirror on root. Controlled-or-uncontrolled via value/defaultValue + onChange. Pure helpers exported: normalizeTimeValue (mode-aware clamp + default period AM), stepTimeField (wrap-around increment), togglePeriod, parseTimeString (mode-aware parser; returns null on invalid), formatTimeValue (pad + colon + optional AM/PM suffix), to24HourFormat (12 AM -> 0; 12 PM -> 12; 3 PM -> 15), DEFAULT_TIME_VALUE_24 + DEFAULT_TIME_VALUE_12 constants. ARIA: root role=group + aria-label; inputs role=spinbutton + aria-valuemin/max/now/text + aria-label per field; separator aria-hidden=true; AM/PM toggle button aria-pressed + aria-label. Data attrs throughout (data-section + data-mode + data-show-seconds + data-disabled + data-read-only on root; data-section + data-time-field per input; data-section on separator; data-section + data-period on AM/PM toggle). forwardRef forwarded to root group div. 61 new test cases in time-picker.test.tsx (6 normalizeTimeValue + 6 stepTimeField + 2 togglePeriod + 10 parseTimeString + 3 formatTimeValue + 5 to24HourFormat + 29 component); 61/61 pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for touched files. Exported via components/ui barrel. Out of scope (documented): combined <DateTimePicker> (pair with DatePicker in host), time-zone selection (TimeValue is wall-clock; host owns zone), millisecond field (HH/MM/SS only for v1), range picker start+end time (separate composition), scroll-wheel mobile picker (native input + arrow keys canonical for v1), auto-advance to next field after two digits (adopter can layer via onChange + DOM ref). Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/auto-ui-time-picker. Worker auto-w391. Bump 1.11.427. |
| 11.410 | UI: date-picker primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.428 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). `<DatePicker>` (11.176) already shipped calendar grid + month/year navigation (Prev/Next month + Prev/Next year buttons) + min/max constraints + full keyboard navigation (Arrow keys for day/week, PageUp/Down for month, Shift+PageUp/Down for year, Home/End for week bounds, Enter to select) + DateRangePicker companion. This patch closes the remaining 'disabled-date predicate' bullet. New prop `isDateDisabled?: (date: Date) => boolean` on both `<DatePicker>` and `<DateRangePicker>`: receives start-of-day Date, returns true to mark cell unavailable. Pure helper exported `isDayAllowed(d, min, max, isDateDisabled): boolean` is the canonical combined gate -- date allowed only when within [min, max] AND predicate returns false; predicate throws are swallowed (cell treated as disabled) so a bad caller-supplied function cannot crash the calendar render. Cell behaviour: predicate-disabled cells render identically to min/max disabled (native disabled + aria-disabled=true + opacity-40 + pointer-events stripped); click on disabled cell is no-op (no onChange); Enter on predicate-disabled focused cell is no-op (Arrow nav still moves focus through so user sees what was excluded but cannot select); DateRangePicker honours predicate in both calendar panes simultaneously. 15 new test cases in date-picker.test.tsx (28 total: 13 legacy + 15 new -- 8 isDayAllowed pure helper cases including throw-swallow + min/max + predicate compound, 6 DatePicker component cases for disable/click no-op/Enter no-op/min+max+predicate compound/throw swallow, 1 DateRangePicker dual-pane disable); 28/28 pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for touched files. Legacy 13 tests pass byte-identical (no surface breakage). Out of scope (documented): memoization across cells (predicate called per cell per render; caller can useMemo if needed), async predicate (sync for v1; adopters needing async should fetch disabled-set upfront and close over it), multi-date selection (single + range only), year/decade picker jump-views (Prev/Next year handles common case; follow-on), per-cell tooltip on disabled cells (host layer). Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/auto-ui-date-picker. Worker auto-w392. Bump 1.11.428. |
| 11.411 | UI: color-picker primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.429 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/color-picker.tsx` ships `<ColorPicker>` -- HSL saturation/lightness canvas + hue slider + optional alpha slider + hex/rgb/hsl input modes + preset swatch row. SL canvas: square surface with X=saturation 0..100, Y=lightness inverted (top=100, bottom=0); background layered two CSS gradients (white -> hsl(h, 100%, 50%) horizontal + transparent -> black vertical); pointer drag updates s+l; role=slider + aria-valuetext='Saturation N%, Lightness N%'. Hue slider: full 360-degree rainbow horizontal strip; pointer drag updates h; role=slider + aria-valuemin/now/max. Alpha slider (showAlpha default true): gradient from transparent to current hue over checkerboard background; pointer drag updates a 0..1. Pointer capture: each surface calls setPointerCapture on pointerdown so drag exiting surface keeps updating until pointerup; pointermove no-op when dragging flag false. Input row: <select> for format (HEX/RGB/HSL) + text <input> mirrors current value in that format; mid-typing shows keystrokes; on blur OR Enter parses via parseColor(text, mode); invalid input snaps back to canonical formatted current value. Preset swatches: row of clickable buttons below input; click parses hex via parseHex + emits new color; presets=[] hides row; default presets are DEFAULT_COLOR_PRESETS (10 Tailwind tones). Disabled blocks all interactions (pointer/input/preset); data-disabled mirrors. Pure helpers exported: normalizeColorValue (wraps hue 360, clamps s/l 0..100 + a 0..1, fills missing from DEFAULT_COLOR_VALUE), hslToRgb / rgbToHsl (0..255 byte / 0..100 percent; grey/white/black edge cases handled; round-trip <=2-unit drift), parseHex (3/4/6/8 digit with/without leading hash), parseRgb / parseHsl (canonical rgb()/rgba()/hsl()/hsla() shapes; permissive whitespace; null on garbage), parseColor (preferred-mode fallback chain), formatHex / formatRgb / formatHsl / formatColor (alpha<1 switches to alpha variant: #rrggbbaa / rgba() / hsla()), DEFAULT_COLOR_VALUE + DEFAULT_COLOR_PRESETS constants. ARIA: root role=group + aria-label; surfaces role=slider + appropriate aria-valuemin/now/max + aria-valuetext; mode select + text input aria-labelled; presets role=list + per-swatch role=listitem + aria-label='Preset <hex>'. Data attrs throughout (data-section + data-disabled + data-show-alpha on root; data-section + data-saturation/lightness on SL; data-section + data-hue on hue; data-section + data-alpha on alpha; data-section per thumb/mode/input/presets/preset; data-preset-hex per swatch). forwardRef forwarded to root group div. 58 new test cases in color-picker.test.tsx (4 normalizeColorValue + 6 hslToRgb + 5 rgbToHsl + 5 parseHex + 3 parseRgb + 3 parseHsl + 2 parseColor + 5 format helpers + 25 component including pointer drag/input parse/Enter commit/preset click/disabled/aria attrs/displayName/ref); 58/58 pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for touched files. Exported via components/ui barrel. Out of scope (documented): EyeDropper API (browser-gated; follow-on), CMYK/Lab/OKlch color models (RGB/HSL/hex canonical for web v1), named CSS color parsing (e.g., 'rebeccapurple'; host can pre-resolve to hex), HSV canvas variant (HSL is dispatched contract; HSV follow-on if adopter prefers Photoshop coordinates), HTML5 DnD external swatch onto canvas, recent-colors memory (host can manage and pass via presets). Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/auto-ui-color-picker. Worker auto-w393. Bump 1.11.429. |
| 11.412 | UI: gauge + meter primitives | done | SHIPPED v1.11.430 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/gauge.tsx` + `meter.tsx` ship two paired progress primitives. `<Gauge>` SVG radial arc with min/max range and optional threshold zones (each [from, to] paints a coloured band); 270-degree sweep from 135deg to 405deg (canonical speedometer U-shape with 90-degree gap at bottom); track arc + threshold band arcs at 35% opacity + progress arc that picks matched threshold colour and paints over the band at full opacity. `<Meter>` linear bar with multi-segment colour stops; track background uses segmented linear-gradient (each segment hard-stops at its declared colour so bar reads as row of bands); indicator overlays at value/max width/height tinted with matched segment colour at 85% opacity; size sm/md/lg; horizontal or vertical orientation (vertical bottom-to-top with aria-orientation=vertical); optional value badge. Both `role="progressbar"` + aria-valuemin/max/now/text. Pure helpers exported: clampGaugeValue (NaN -> min; +Infinity -> max; -Infinity -> min; zero/inverted range -> min), valueToFraction (0..1), findThresholdColor / findMeterSegment (inclusive >= from && <= to; boundary lands on lower band), describeArc (SVG path with correct large-arc flag), buildMeterStops (CSS gradient stops string), GAUGE_START_ANGLE_DEG=135 / GAUGE_END_ANGLE_DEG=405 / GAUGE_SWEEP_DEG=270 constants. Edge cases: max<=min falls back to [min, min+100] so aria values stay sane; NaN/Infinity clamping centralised. Data attrs throughout (data-section + data-value + data-fraction on both roots; data-section per gauge-svg/track/threshold/progress/value with data-threshold-from/to + data-progress-color; data-section per meter-track/indicator/value with data-fill-color + data-segment + data-orientation + data-size). 62 new test cases (28 gauge + 34 meter); 62/62 pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for touched files. Exported via components/ui barrel. Out of scope (documented): animated needle/fill transitions (snap to value for v1; CSS transitions follow-on), multiple values on one meter/gauge (single value v1), tick marks/labels along arc/bar (adopter layers; built-in ticker follow-on), custom sweep angles for Gauge (semicircle/full circle; 270 canonical for v1), vertical gauge variant (Gauge always radial; Meter covers vertical), touch/mouse drag input (both read-only; use ColorPicker/Slider for input). Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/auto-ui-gauge-meter. Worker auto-w394. Bump 1.11.430. |
| 11.413 | UI: list-section primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.431 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/list-section.tsx` ships `<ListSection>` -- grouped list rows with section header (label + count badge), collapsible groups, optional sticky headers on scroll. API: `groups: ListSectionGroup[]` (`{ id, label, items, defaultCollapsed?, count?, description?, badge? }`); `collapsedGroups` (controlled), `defaultCollapsedGroups` + per-group `defaultCollapsed` (uncontrolled seed -- initial set is union); `collapsible` (default true); `showBadges` (default true); `stickyHeaders` (default true); `renderItem(item, group)` slot; `emptyLabel` (default '(no items)'). Header: `<button>` with chevron (right collapsed / down expanded) + label + count pill or custom badge slot + optional description; click/Enter/Space toggle; aria-expanded mirrors collapsed state; aria-controls points to body id. Sticky headers: `sticky top-0 z-10` so headers pin to top of scroll container while body scrolls past; stickyHeaders=false drops the classes. Collapsible=false: chevron hidden + header disabled + click no-op + aria-expanded omitted. Count badge: small pill with aria-label='N items'; explicit `group.count` (clamped >=0 + floored) wins; else items.length fallback; `group.badge` ReactNode replaces the pill; showBadges=false hides all. Per-group empty state renders emptyLabel inside body. Empty groups array renders emptyLabel at root + data-empty=true. Controlled state: onCollapsedGroupsChange(ids) fires on every toggle in both modes. Pure helpers exported: isGroupCollapsed (Set lookup), getGroupCount (count > items.length fallback; clamp+floor), totalItemCount (sum all), visibleItemCount (drops collapsed groups from sum). ARIA: root role=list + aria-label; each group role=region + aria-labelledby; header aria-expanded + aria-controls; body role=list; items role=listitem with per-item aria-label override. Data attrs throughout (data-section + data-empty + data-group-count + data-total-items + data-sticky-headers + data-collapsible on root; data-section + data-group-id + data-collapsed + data-count per group; data-section per chevron/label/count/badge-slot/description/body/empty-group; data-section + data-item-id + data-group-id per item). forwardRef forwarded to root div. 41 new test cases in list-section.test.tsx (2 isGroupCollapsed + 4 getGroupCount + 3 totalItemCount/visibleItemCount + 32 component); 41/41 pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for touched files. Exported via components/ui barrel. Out of scope (documented): nested groups (TreeView covers recursive trees), drag-and-drop reorder of items between groups (adopters via renderItem), per-group filter/search (caller pre-filters items), virtualized item rendering per group (pair with ListVirtualizer 11.401), sortable group order (source-array canonical), multi-select range (host manages selection). Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/auto-ui-list-section. Worker auto-w395. Bump 1.11.431. |
| 11.414 | UI: status-indicator primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.432 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/status-indicator.tsx` ships `<StatusIndicator>` -- coloured dot with optional pulse animation, optional label slot, three size variants; default `role="status"` + `aria-live="polite"` + `aria-atomic="true"` so screen readers announce kind transitions (pending -> success etc.) without operator focus. Six canonical kinds (success/warning/error/info/neutral/pending) mapped to emerald/amber/rose/sky/zinc/violet tokens via exported STATUS_INDICATOR_KIND_CLASS map (each entry has dot/ping/text class strings). Three sizes (sm/md/lg) gate dot dim + label text + inter-element gap. Pulse: false (default, no ping) / true (ping at 60% opacity with motion-safe:animate-ping) / 'subtle' (ping at 30% opacity); prefers-reduced-motion drops the ping animation but keeps static colour cue. Label slot: when supplied renders next to dot with kind text colour; omitted when no label. ARIA label resolution via exported `getStatusIndicatorAriaLabel(ariaLabel, kind, label)`: explicit ariaLabel wins > non-empty trimmed string label > "Status: <kind>" fallback. `decorative={true}` drops role + aria-live + aria-label entirely (use when adjacent text already announces state). `hideDot={true}` omits dot wrapper -- only label renders (dense table cells). Data attrs throughout (data-section + data-kind + data-size + data-pulse + data-decorative + data-hide-dot on root; data-section + data-dot-kind + data-pulse-kind per inner span; data-section for dot-wrapper/dot/pulse/label). forwardRef forwarded to root span; extra HTMLSpanElement attributes spread to root. 31 new test cases in status-indicator.test.tsx (1 kind class declaration + 4 getStatusIndicatorAriaLabel + 26 component including role/aria-live/decorative/label fallback/dot+pulse rendering/size+kind class match/data-pulse/displayName/ref/every kind has tokens); 31/31 pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for touched files. Exported via components/ui barrel. Out of scope (documented): auto-clear after timeout (host owns timer), multi-state transition animation (snap to new kind for v1; CSS transitions follow-on), tooltip slot on hover (wrap with existing Tooltip), per-kind icon glyph (adopters wrap with icon primitives; built-in icon slot follow-on), adoption sweep through existing status-line affordances (existing inline span dots ship until migration dispatch). | 11.415 | UI: timeline-step primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.433 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/timeline-step.tsx` ships `<TimelineStep>` -- single vertical step indicator that composes into a larger wizard/progress timeline. Distinct from `<Stepper>` (11.252/11.376) full multi-step composer; TimelineStep is the per-row primitive exposed for hosts that want to assemble custom layouts (audit log entries, release-cut milestones). API: state ('completed'|'current'|'pending'|'error' default 'pending'); icon (custom slot override); label / description / meta slots; showConnectorBefore / showConnectorAfter flags; connectorState override; size ('sm'|'md'|'lg' default 'md'); ariaLabel; HTMLLIElement attributes spread. States: completed (emerald + check), current (primary + ring, no default icon), pending (bg + border + muted), error (rose + alert). Sizes scale circle (h-5/6/8), icon (h-2.5/3/4), label text (xs/sm/base), gap (2/3/4). Per-state connector derivation: completed -> both sides 'completed' colour; current/error -> before 'completed' + after 'pending'; pending -> both 'pending'; connectorState prop overrides both sides uniformly. When connector hidden on a side, a same-height spacer renders so circle stays vertically centred regardless of label length. Custom icon prop replaces default; data-icon-kind attr distinguishes 'custom' vs 'default-<state>'. Right-rail content block omits entirely when label/description/meta all undefined (icon-only timeline strip). ARIA: `<li role="listitem">` so step fits into parent `<ol>`/`<ul>`; aria-current="step" only on current; ariaLabel prop for explicit step naming. Pure helpers exported: TIMELINE_STEP_STATE_CLASS map, isTimelineStepReachable (completed+current -> true), getTimelineStepDefaultIcon (Check/AlertCircle/null), getTimelineStepAriaCurrent ('step' for current). Data attrs throughout (data-section + data-state + data-size + data-show-connector-before + data-show-connector-after + data-reachable on root; data-section per rail/spacer/connector-before/connector-after/circle/icon/content/label/description/meta sub-nodes; data-connector-state per connector; data-icon-kind per icon). forwardRef forwarded to root li; extra HTMLLIElement attrs spread. 43 new test cases in timeline-step.test.tsx (2 TIMELINE_STEP_STATE_CLASS + 4 isTimelineStepReachable + 4 getTimelineStepDefaultIcon + 1 getTimelineStepAriaCurrent + 32 component); 43/43 pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for touched files. Exported via components/ui barrel. Out of scope (documented): horizontal orientation (Stepper covers), click handler/jump-to-step (adopter wraps with button/anchor; primitive stays presentational), animated connector fill (snap to new state for v1; CSS transitions follow-on), tooltip slot on circle (wrap with Tooltip), drag-to-reorder (wizard order canonical). Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/auto-ui-timeline-step. Worker auto-w397. Bump 1.11.433. |
| 11.416 | UI: rating primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.434 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). `<Rating>` already shipped star rating input with half-star support, readonly mode, keyboard nav (Arrow/Home/End), size variants. This patch closes the remaining 'custom icon slot' and 'ARIA radiogroup' bullets. New props: `icon?: ReactNode` (replaces filled glyph; default lucide Star), `emptyIcon?: ReactNode` (replaces unfilled background glyph), `ariaRole?: 'slider'|'radiogroup'` (default 'slider' for byte-identical legacy; opt into canonical W3C radiogroup pattern). ARIA radiogroup mode: root role=radiogroup instead of slider; each star role=radio + aria-checked ('true' fully covered, 'mixed' half-star with allowHalf, 'false' past value); aria-valuenow/min/max omitted; readonly + radiogroup renders stars as `<span role="radio" aria-disabled="true">`; aria-disabled mirrors on root. Custom icon slots accept any ReactNode; DOM structure preserved byte-identical (base glyph as direct child of glyph wrapper; fill wrapper contains filled glyph) so legacy tests selecting via `[data-rating-star] > span > span` keep working. Pure helpers exported: clampRating (NaN -> 0; neg max -> 0; clamps to [0,max]), getRatingStarFillPercent(starIndex, value, allowHalf) -> 0|50|100 (100 covered, 50 half allowHalf-only, 100 allowHalf=false+diff>0 snap, 0 past), getRatingAriaChecked(starIndex, value, allowHalf) -> 'true'|'false'|'mixed' (covered/past/half-star). New data attrs: data-section + data-aria-role + data-readonly + data-allow-half + data-value + data-max on root; data-section + data-star-index + data-fill-pct per star; legacy data-rating-star + data-hover retained byte-identical; data-section per glyph/fill sub-nodes with data-fill-pct. 26 new test cases in rating.test.tsx (46 total: 20 legacy + 26 new -- 5 clampRating, 4 getRatingStarFillPercent, 5 getRatingAriaChecked, 6 ariaRole=radiogroup including readonly+radiogroup, 2 custom icon slot, 3 root data attrs, 1 star data attrs); 46/46 pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for touched files. Legacy 20 tests pass byte-identical (no surface breakage). Out of scope (documented): per-star tooltip on hover (wrap with Tooltip), animated fill transition on value change (snap for v1; CSS transitions follow-on motion layer), granularity less than 0.5 (dispatch contract is half-star; finer follow-on), mass migration to ariaRole='radiogroup' (legacy stays slider; new adopters opt in until migration dispatch), multi-rating compositions (adopter composes multiple instances). | 11.417 | UI: comparison-slider primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.435 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/comparison-slider.tsx` ships `<ComparisonSlider>` -- before/after image slider with a draggable divider handle, keyboard navigation, optional percentage badge, and per-image lazy loading defaults. Two `<img>` layers (before underneath, after in a width-clipped wrapper) so the divider acts as a reveal mask. API: `beforeSrc`/`afterSrc`/`beforeAlt`/`afterAlt`; `value`/`defaultValue` (controlled or uncontrolled, default 50) + `onChange`; `ariaLabel` (default 'Comparison slider'); `beforeLabel`/`afterLabel` for corner labels; `showPercentage` (default true) + `formatValue` override; `lazy` (default true) gates `loading="lazy"|"eager"`; `step` (default 5); `aspectRatio` (default '16 / 9'). Pointer drag on the root region uses `setPointerCapture` (guarded for jsdom) and clamps to `[0, 100]` via `getPercentFromX`. Keyboard nav on the handle: Arrow Right/Up step `+step`, Arrow Left/Down step `-step`, Page Up/Down step `+/- 2*step`, Home -> 0, End -> 100. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_COMPARISON_VALUE=50` / `DEFAULT_COMPARISON_STEP=5` / `DEFAULT_COMPARISON_ASPECT_RATIO='16 / 9'`; `clampComparisonValue(value)` -- non-finite (NaN / +/-Infinity) falls back to 50; `getPercentFromX(clientX, { left, width })` -- zero-width rect returns 0; `stepComparisonValue(current, delta)`. ARIA: root `role="region"` + `aria-label`; handle `role="slider"` + `aria-valuemin=0` / `aria-valuemax=100` / `aria-valuenow` / `aria-valuetext` + `aria-orientation="horizontal"` + `tabIndex=0`. Data attrs throughout (data-section per node: `comparison-slider` / `-before` / `-after-clip` / `-after` / `-before-label` / `-after-label` / `-handle` / `-handle-grip` / `-handle-arrow` / `-percentage`; root mirrors props via `data-value` / `data-show-percentage` / `data-lazy`; handle has `data-handle-position`; badge has `data-percentage`). Images carry `decoding="async" draggable={false}` so dragging the slider does not start a browser image drag. forwardRef forwarded to the root region. 48 new test cases in comparison-slider.test.tsx (5 clampComparisonValue + 6 getPercentFromX + 3 stepComparisonValue + 3 constants + 31 component); 48/48 pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for touched files. Exported via components/ui barrel. Out of scope (documented): vertical orientation (horizontal-only by design), pinch-zoom on images, image preloading / placeholder shimmer (host owns loading state). Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/auto-ui-comparison-slider. Worker auto-w399. Bump 1.11.435. |
| 11.418 | UI: image-gallery primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.436 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/image-gallery.tsx` ships `<ImageGallery>` -- grid / masonry thumbnail layout with a portal-mounted lightbox modal, keyboard navigation, and touch-friendly swipe gestures. API: `items: ImageGalleryItem[]` (src/alt/thumb?/caption?/width?/height?); `layout`: 'grid' (default) or 'masonry'; `columns` (default 3) + `gap` (px, default 8); `defaultIndex`/`openIndex` controlled-or-uncontrolled + `onOpenChange`; `ariaLabel` (default 'Image gallery'); `lazy` (default true); `onSelect`; `swipeThreshold` (px, default 50); `closeOnBackdropClick` (default true); `containerId` portal root override; `wrap` (default true). Grid uses `grid-template-columns: repeat(N, minmax(0, 1fr))`; masonry uses CSS `column-count: N` with `break-inside: avoid` per item. Thumbs are buttons; clicking opens lightbox at that index. Per-item `thumb` overrides the thumbnail source while the lightbox still uses `src`. Lightbox renders through a portal (`app-portal-root` by default); body scroll locks while open and restores on close; auto-focuses the lightbox root on open so keyboard nav works immediately. Keyboard nav on the lightbox: Arrow Right (next), Arrow Left (prev), Home (first), End (last), Escape (close). Pointer / touch swipe: pointer-down at clientX1, pointer-up at clientX2; `dx <= -threshold` -> next; `dx >= threshold` -> prev; otherwise no-op. Wrap by default (next-from-last -> 0; prev-from-first -> last); `wrap={false}` clamps + visually disables the boundary nav button. `closeOnBackdropClick` closes only when the click target equals the backdrop (not a child). Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_GALLERY_COLUMNS=3` / `DEFAULT_GALLERY_GAP=8` / `DEFAULT_SWIPE_THRESHOLD=50`; `clampGalleryIndex(index, total)` -- returns 0 for empty totals, NaN, clamps, floors fractional; `nextGalleryIndex(current, total, wrap?)` / `prevGalleryIndex(current, total, wrap?)`; `isSwipeLeft(dx, threshold?)` / `isSwipeRight(dx, threshold?)` -- inclusive at threshold. ARIA: region `role="region"` + aria-label; thumb buttons `aria-label="Open image N: ALT"`; lightbox `role="dialog"` + `aria-modal="true"` + `aria-label="<label> lightbox"` + `tabIndex=-1`. Data attrs throughout (data-section per node: `image-gallery` / `-item` / `-thumb` / `-lightbox` / `-close` / `-prev` / `-next` / `-figure` / `-full` / `-caption` / `-counter`; root mirrors `data-layout` / `data-columns` / `data-lazy` / `data-item-count`; lightbox has `data-active-index`; thumb has `data-index`). Images carry `decoding="async" draggable={false}`. forwardRef forwarded to the region root. 63 new test cases in image-gallery.test.tsx (6 clampGalleryIndex + 4 nextGalleryIndex + 4 prevGalleryIndex + 3 isSwipeLeft/Right + 3 constants + 43 component); 63/63 pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for touched files. Exported via components/ui barrel. Out of scope (documented): pinch/wheel zoom inside the lightbox (host wraps), drag-to-reorder (read-only here), SSR of the lightbox (portal lazy-mounts after first effect), virtualisation for thousands of thumbs (host wraps with `<VirtualizedList>`). | 11.419 | UI: video-player primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.437 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/video-player.tsx` ships `<VideoPlayer>` -- HTML5 video with a custom controls bar (play/pause, scrubber, time, volume, mute, playback rate select, picture-in-picture toggle) plus keyboard shortcuts wired on the outer region and an imperative `VideoPlayerHandle` ref for programmatic control. API: `src`/`poster?`/`autoPlay?`/`loop?`/`muted?`/`defaultVolume?`/`defaultPlaybackRate?`/`playbackRates?` (default `[0.5, 0.75, 1, 1.25, 1.5, 2]`); `controls?` (default true); `ariaLabel?` (default 'Video player'); `seekStep?` (s, default 5); `volumeStep?` (0-1, default 0.1); `preload?` ('auto'|'metadata'|'none', default 'metadata'); `caption?`; events `onPlay`/`onPause`/`onEnded`/`onTimeUpdate(currentTime, duration)`/`onVolumeChange(volume, muted)`/`onPlaybackRateChange(rate)`. Imperative `VideoPlayerHandle`: `play()` / `pause()` / `seek(time)` / `getCurrentTime()` / `getDuration()` / `toggleMute()` / `togglePictureInPicture()`. Renders a `<video>` with `playsInline` + the supplied attrs; the custom controls bar overlays the bottom and `controls=false` hides it. Play / Pause button toggles icons via internal `isPlaying` state wired to the `<video>`'s play/pause/ended events. Scrubber is a native `<input type="range">`; mid-scrub updates `video.currentTime` directly. Volume slider auto-mutes at 0, un-mutes when raised; mute button is independent. Playback rate select renders the configured rate list. PiP button calls `video.requestPictureInPicture()` if available, toggles back to document via `document.exitPictureInPicture()`; browser declines swallowed so the player never throws. Enter/leave PiP wired via `addEventListener` in `useEffect` (React JSX does not type these events). Keyboard shortcuts on the outer region (`tabIndex=0`): Space/K (play-pause toggle), Arrow Right/Left (seek +/-seekStep), Arrow Up/Down (volume +/-volumeStep), M (mute toggle), P (PiP toggle). Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_PLAYBACK_RATES`/`DEFAULT_SEEK_STEP=5`/`DEFAULT_VOLUME_STEP=0.1`/`DEFAULT_VOLUME=1`/`DEFAULT_PLAYBACK_RATE=1`; `formatVideoTime(seconds)` -- `MM:SS` under 1h, `HH:MM:SS` past 1h, NaN/negatives -> `00:00`; `clampVolume(value)` -- `[0, 1]`, NaN -> 1; `clampSeek(time, duration)` -- `[0, duration]`, invalid duration -> 0; `nextPlaybackRate(current, rates?)` -- cycles + wraps, unknown current -> first entry, empty rates -> current. ARIA: region `role="region"` + aria-label + tabIndex=0; per-control aria-label ("Play"/"Pause", "Mute"/"Unmute", "Seek", "Volume", "Playback speed", "Picture in picture"). Data attrs throughout (data-section per node: `video-player` / `-element` / `-controls` / `-play` / `-current-time` / `-scrubber` / `-duration` / `-mute` / `-volume` / `-rate` / `-pip` / `-caption`; root mirrors live state via `data-playing` / `data-muted` / `data-pip` / `data-rate`). 53 new test cases in video-player.test.tsx (6 formatVideoTime + 4 clampVolume + 4 clampSeek + 5 nextPlaybackRate + 5 constants + 29 component); jsdom test setup wires `HTMLMediaElement.prototype.play/pause/requestPictureInPicture` mocks via `Object.defineProperty` (jsdom lacks native impls); 53/53 pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for touched files. Exported via components/ui barrel. Out of scope (documented): HLS/DASH adaptive streaming (host wraps), multi-audio/subtitle track picker (`<track>` children pass through), skin theming variants (host wraps for branding), fullscreen toggle (browsers vary; host owns `requestFullscreen`). Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/auto-ui-video-player. Worker auto-w401. Bump 1.11.437. |
| 11.420 | UI: countdown-timer primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.438 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/countdown-timer.tsx` ships `<CountdownTimer>` -- counts down from a positive duration to zero, formats remaining time as `HH:MM:SS` (auto-collapses to `MM:SS` when the run never exceeds an hour), supports pause/resume/reset, and fires `onExpire` exactly once at zero. Optional warning + critical thresholds drive state-derived colour transitions and an ARIA live region tier swap. API: `durationSeconds`; `autoStart?` (default true); `warningThresholdSeconds?` (default 30) + `criticalThresholdSeconds?` (default 10); `controls?` (default true) + `showReset?` (default false); `format?: 'auto'|'hh:mm:ss'|'mm:ss'` (default 'auto'); event callbacks `onExpire`/`onTick(remaining)`/`onPause`/`onResume`/`onReset(seconds)`; `label?`; `ariaLabel?` (default 'Countdown timer'); `tickIntervalMs?` (default 250). Imperative `CountdownTimerHandle`: `start()` / `pause()` / `resume()` / `reset(seconds?)` / `toggle()` / `getRemainingSeconds()` / `getState()`. Tick loop computes remaining from a wall-clock `endsAt` timestamp so display stays accurate across throttled tabs and uneven `setInterval` cadence. Pause captures a snapshot + tears down the interval; resume re-derives `endsAt = now + snapshot` so accumulated drift never leaks. `onExpire` fires exactly once at zero (guarded by a ref so React strict-mode double renders do not duplicate). State derivation: `<= 0` -> 'expired', `<= critical` -> 'critical', `<= warning` -> 'warning', otherwise 'normal'. Display text class swaps with state (`text-foreground` / `text-warning` / `text-destructive`); ARIA `aria-live` flips to 'assertive' when state is 'critical' so SRs announce the imminent expiration. Toggle button auto-disables when remaining=0 (no resume from expired without reset). Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_WARNING_THRESHOLD=30` / `DEFAULT_CRITICAL_THRESHOLD=10` / `DEFAULT_COUNTDOWN_FORMAT='auto'` / `DEFAULT_TICK_INTERVAL_MS=250`; `clampCountdownDuration(value)` floors negatives + `+Infinity` to 0; `formatCountdownTime(seconds, format?, thresholdHours?)` ceils fractional seconds (0.5s -> '00:01'), 'auto' includes hours only at 1h+, 'mm:ss' collapses ('62:05' for 1h2m5s); `getCountdownState(remaining, warning?, critical?)` returns 'expired' for 0/neg/NaN; `tickRemaining(remaining, deltaMs)` subtracts deltaMs/1000 and clamps to 0. ARIA: region `role="region"` + aria-label; display `role="timer"` + `aria-atomic="true"` + `aria-live` polite (or assertive in critical); toggle `aria-label="Pause countdown"|"Resume countdown"`; reset `aria-label="Reset countdown"`. Data attrs throughout (data-section per node: `countdown-timer` / `-label` / `-display` / `-controls` / `-toggle` / `-reset`; root mirrors live state via `data-state` / `data-running` / `data-format` / `data-remaining`). 48 new test cases in countdown-timer.test.tsx (5 clampCountdownDuration + 6 formatCountdownTime + 5 getCountdownState + 2 tickRemaining + 4 constants + 23 component synchronous + 3 fake-timer driven); 48/48 pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for touched files. Exported via components/ui barrel. Out of scope (documented): count-up timer (countdown-only by design), pre-expiration soft-warnings outside threshold tiers (host wraps `onTick`), persisted state across reloads (host owns), audible alarm on expire (host wraps `onExpire`), animated digit transition on tick (snap for v1). Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/auto-ui-countdown-timer. Worker auto-w402. Bump 1.11.438. |
| 11.421 | UI: progress-tracker primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.439 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/progress-tracker.tsx` ships `<ProgressTracker>` -- multi-step progress display with named milestones, horizontal or vertical orientation, per-step completed / active / pending / error states, and `role="progressbar"` on the root so screen readers announce overall completion alongside the milestone list. API: `steps: ProgressTrackerStep[]` (id/label/description?/state?); `activeIndex` (use -1 for "no step started yet"); `orientation`: 'horizontal' (default) or 'vertical'; `showLabels?` (default true) + `showDescriptions?` (default true); `size?: 'sm'|'md'|'lg'` (default 'md'); `onStepClick?(index)` toggles indicators from `<span>` to focusable `<button>` (active button gets `aria-current="step"`); `ariaLabel?` (default 'Progress tracker'). Steps render in a nested `<ol>` so each milestone is enumerable. State derivation: `step.state` override wins; otherwise `index < activeIndex` -> 'completed', `index === activeIndex` -> 'active', `index > activeIndex` -> 'pending'. Indicator glyph: Check icon for 'completed', X icon for 'error', the (1-based) step number for 'active' / 'pending'. Per-step visual class swaps with state for indicator background, ring, border, label text, and connector colour. Connectors render between consecutive steps -- a leading one before every non-first horizontal indicator, a trailing one after every non-last horizontal indicator, and a vertical bar between vertical indicators. Each connector carries `data-state` derived from the indicator before it. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_PROGRESS_TRACKER_ORIENTATION='horizontal'` / `DEFAULT_PROGRESS_TRACKER_SIZE='md'`; `clampProgressActiveIndex(index, total)` -- empty total or NaN -> -1, out-of-range clamps; `getProgressTrackerStepState(index, activeIndex, override?)` -- pure derivation reusable for custom indicator rendering; `getProgressTrackerPercent(activeIndex, total)` -- counts active step as half-complete (cleanly maps to a single ARIA `aria-valuenow`), rounded to 2 decimals. ARIA: root `role="progressbar"` + aria-label + `aria-valuemin=0` / `aria-valuemax=total` / `aria-valuenow=activeIndex+1` + `aria-valuetext="Step N of total"`; active indicator `aria-current="step"`; per-button indicator `aria-label` includes both step label and derived state ("Plan (completed)"). Data attrs throughout (data-section per node: `progress-tracker` / `-steps` / `-step` / `-indicator` / `-content` / `-label` / `-description` / `-connector`; root mirrors `data-orientation` / `data-size` / `data-active-index` / `data-total` / `data-percent`; each step mirrors `data-step-id` / `data-step-index` / `data-state`). forwardRef forwarded to the root region. 44 new test cases in progress-tracker.test.tsx (6 clampProgressActiveIndex + 5 getProgressTrackerStepState + 4 getProgressTrackerPercent + 2 constants + 27 component); 44/44 pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for touched files. Exported via components/ui barrel. Out of scope (documented): async per-step status fetching (host owns network -> state override), branching workflows (strictly linear; fork into multiple ProgressTrackers), click-to-jump validation (onStepClick fires unconditionally; host honours target), animated indicator transitions (snap for v1). | 11.422 | UI: scroll-spy primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.440 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/scroll-spy.tsx` ships `<ScrollSpy>` -- a `<nav>` whose active item tracks the section currently in the viewport. `IntersectionObserver` watches every linked section; clicking an item smooth-scrolls the target into view with an optional offset for sticky headers. API: `items: ScrollSpyItem[]` (id/label/disabled?); `activeId?` (controlled) / `defaultActiveId?` (uncontrolled seed) + `onActiveChange(id)`; `rootMargin?` (default '0px 0px -50% 0px') + `threshold?` (default 0); `scrollBehavior?: 'smooth'|'auto'` (default 'smooth'); `orientation?: 'vertical'|'horizontal'` (default 'vertical'); `ariaLabel?` (default 'Section navigation'); `scrollOffset?` (px, default 0); `renderItem?` render-prop replaces the default link. Items render as a `<ul>` of links by default; each link is `aria-current="location"` while matching the active id. `renderItem` swaps the default link for any host-rendered element while keeping the per-item `<li>` wrapper with its `data-section` markers. On click the matching section is found by id and scrolled into view: `scrollOffset=0` uses `element.scrollIntoView({ behavior, block: 'start' })`; otherwise `scrollIntoViewWithOffset` falls back to `window.scrollTo({ top: rect.top + scrollY - offset, behavior })` so sticky-header layouts can park the section below the header. `IntersectionObserver` observes only items whose matching `document.getElementById` exists; default `rootMargin '0px 0px -50% 0px'` treats the upper half of the viewport as the "active band" so the active id flips when the section reaches the middle line. When no entries intersect the active id is preserved unless the previously-active section is itself in the current batch (means it just scrolled out -> clear). Controlled `activeId` always wins for the rendered highlight; observer still emits `onActiveChange` so the host can use the callback for analytics/routing while keeping a one-way data flow. Observer disconnects on unmount and on every prop change that resets the watch list. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_SCROLL_SPY_ROOT_MARGIN` / `DEFAULT_SCROLL_SPY_THRESHOLD=0` / `DEFAULT_SCROLL_SPY_ORIENTATION='vertical'` / `DEFAULT_SCROLL_SPY_OFFSET=0`; `ScrollSpyEntry` interface (minimal shape consumed by helpers so adopters can unit-test their own observers without instantiating real DOM entries); `getMostVisibleEntry(entries)` picks the entry with the highest intersectionRatio that is also isIntersecting; `getActiveIdFromEntries(entries, previousActive)` -- consults getMostVisibleEntry first, when nothing intersects preserves previous (unless previous is in the batch, indicating it just left); `scrollIntoViewWithOffset(element, offset?, behavior?)` -- jsdom-safe, falls back to `element.scrollIntoView` when window.scrollTo is missing. ARIA: root `<nav>` + aria-label; default link `aria-current="location"` when active + `aria-disabled` when item.disabled. Data attrs throughout (data-section per node: `scroll-spy` / `-list` / `-item` / `-link`; each LI mirrors `data-item-id` / `data-active` / `data-disabled`; root mirrors `data-orientation` / `data-active-id` / `data-item-count`). forwardRef forwarded to the `<nav>`. 41 new test cases in scroll-spy.test.tsx (2 getMostVisibleEntry + 3 getActiveIdFromEntries + 3 scrollIntoViewWithOffset + 4 constants + 29 component); test file ships a `MockIntersectionObserver` class wired via window assignment with an `emit(entries)` test helper for deterministic viewport simulation; 41/41 pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for touched files. Exported via components/ui barrel. Out of scope (documented): scroll-restoration (host owns hash routing), nested/hierarchical spying (host composes multiple ScrollSpy instances), configurable observer `root` (always documentElement for v1), bidirectional auto-scroll (the spy follows scroll position; does not reverse-drive scroll from the rendered list). Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/auto-ui-scroll-spy. Worker auto-w404. Bump 1.11.440. |
| 11.423 | UI: back-to-top primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.441 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/back-to-top.tsx` ships `<BackToTop>` -- a floating action button that appears once the page (or a custom scroll container) has been scrolled past a configurable threshold and smooth-scrolls the watched target back to its top on click. API: `threshold?` (px, default 200); `scrollBehavior?: 'smooth'|'auto'` (default 'smooth'); `scrollTarget?: HTMLElement|Window|null` (default window); `ariaLabel?` (default 'Back to top'); `position?: 'bottom-right'|'bottom-left'|'bottom-center'` (default 'bottom-right'); `icon?` (default ArrowUp from lucide); `showLabel?` (default false) + `label?` (default 'Back to top'); `onClick?`; `initiallyVisible?` (SSR / test override; default false); `visible?` (fully controlled); `hideOnVisible?` (default true). Renders a `<button>` fixed to one of three corner positions with opacity-based fade transition. Subscribes to the watched target's scroll event with `{ passive: true }`; listener detaches on unmount. Initial visibility is computed from `initiallyVisible` and then upgraded to true on mount **only when the current scroll position already meets the threshold** -- preserves an explicit `initiallyVisible=true` override on fresh page (scrollY=0) while still revealing the button when the user navigates to a deep-link/restored scroll position past the threshold. Click handler fires `onClick`, calls `scrollTargetToTop(target, scrollBehavior)`, and (when uncontrolled + `hideOnVisible`) optimistically hides the button; next scroll event re-syncs once smooth-scroll lands at zero. Controlled `visible` always wins (clicks still call scroll target + onClick but do not flip local state). Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_BACK_TO_TOP_THRESHOLD=200` / `DEFAULT_BACK_TO_TOP_POSITION='bottom-right'` / `DEFAULT_BACK_TO_TOP_LABEL='Back to top'`; `shouldShowBackToTop(scrollY, threshold?)` -- inclusive at threshold (200px scroll with 200px threshold counts as "show"), negative thresholds collapse to 0, NaN inputs return false; `getScrollTop(target)` -- accepts window / HTMLElement / null, reads scrollY for window and scrollTop for elements, falls back to document.documentElement.scrollTop when window.scrollY is missing; `scrollTargetToTop(target, behavior?)` -- uses window.scrollTo / element.scrollTo, falls back to setting `element.scrollTop = 0` when scrollTo is missing (jsdom-safe). ARIA: button `aria-label` (default 'Back to top'); `aria-hidden="true"` when invisible (removed from accessibility tree until needed); `tabIndex=-1` when hidden, `0` when visible. Data attrs throughout (data-section per node: `back-to-top` / `-icon` / `-label`; button mirrors `data-visible` ('true'/'false') + `data-position`). forwardRef forwarded to the button. 46 new test cases in back-to-top.test.tsx (7 shouldShowBackToTop + 4 getScrollTop + 4 scrollTargetToTop + 3 constants + 28 component); 46/46 pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for touched files. Exported via components/ui barrel. Out of scope (documented): auto-hide after N seconds of inactivity (host wraps with hover/idle timer), animated scroll progress ring around button (host composes with `<Progress>`), multi-target (host renders multiple instances for nested scroll panes), keyboard global shortcut (host wires shortcut -> `ref.current?.click()`). | 11.424 | UI: notification-bell primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.442 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/notification-bell.tsx` ships `<NotificationBell>` -- bell trigger with an unread-count badge and a popover panel listing notifications. Each item carries a per-row "mark as read" action; panel header exposes a global "mark all as read" affordance. Unread badge animates on count *increase* via the `motion-safe` Tailwind variant (a fresh React `key` swaps in so `motion-safe:animate-bounce-once` re-fires; count decreases do NOT retrigger). API: `notifications: NotificationItem[]` (id/title/description?/timestamp?/read?/href?/icon?); `open?` (controlled) + `defaultOpen?` + `onOpenChange(open)`; `onMarkAsRead(id)` / `onMarkAllAsRead` / `onNotificationClick(notification)`; `ariaLabel?` (default 'Notifications'); `emptyState?` (default 'No notifications'); `maxBadgeCount?` (default 9); `animateBadge?` (default true); `align?: 'left'|'right'` (default 'right'); `width?: number|string` (default 320); `closeOnSelect?` (default true); `renderItem?` render-prop replaces default row while keeping per-item `<li>` wrapper + data attrs. Trigger `aria-label` includes the unread count when > 0 ("Notifications (2 unread)"). Badge formats with `formatBadgeCount(count, max)` -- shows `${max}+` when count exceeds cap; no badge for 0 unread. Items render newest-first via `sortNotificationsByTimestamp` (Date/number/ISO string accepted; missing timestamps sink to the back). Per-item "mark as read" button only renders for unread items; header "Mark all" button only renders when unread > 0. Click outside (mousedown outside both trigger and panel) and Escape close the panel; handlers attach only while open. Controlled `open` always wins; `onOpenChange` fires on every toggle. Item click fires `onNotificationClick(item)` and (default) closes panel; `closeOnSelect=false` keeps open. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_NOTIFICATION_MAX_BADGE=9` / `DEFAULT_NOTIFICATION_PANEL_WIDTH=320` / `DEFAULT_NOTIFICATION_ALIGN='right'`; `getUnreadCount(notifications)` (missing `read` -> unread); `formatBadgeCount(count, max?)` -- empty string for 0/neg/NaN, floors fractional, emits `${max}+` past cap; `sortNotificationsByTimestamp(notifications)` -- non-mutating, accepts string/number/Date/undefined (undefined sinks to back). ARIA: trigger `<button>` + count-aware aria-label + `aria-haspopup="dialog"` + `aria-expanded`; panel `role="dialog"` + `aria-label="<label> panel"`; per-item title wrapped in `<button>` so SRs land on each row independently; mark-as-read `aria-label="Mark as read"`; mark-all `aria-label="Mark all as read"`. Data attrs throughout (data-section per node: `notification-bell` / `-trigger` / `-badge` / `-panel` / `-header` / `-mark-all` / `-list` / `-item` / `-item-trigger` / `-item-title` / `-item-description` / `-item-mark` / `-empty`; root mirrors `data-open` / `data-unread-count` / `data-has-unread`; per-item `data-item-id` + `data-unread`; badge `data-badge-count`; panel `data-align`). forwardRef forwarded to trigger button. 50 new test cases in notification-bell.test.tsx (3 getUnreadCount + 5 formatBadgeCount + 4 sortNotificationsByTimestamp + 3 constants + 35 component); 50/50 pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for touched files. Exported via components/ui barrel. Out of scope (documented): server/SSE subscription to live notifications (host owns network), infinite scroll inside panel (host wraps with `<InfiniteScroll>`), bulk select + bulk actions beyond "mark all" (host wraps with bulk-action pattern), sound/desktop-notification fan-out (host wires onMarkAsRead/onNotificationClick). | 11.425 | UI: search-result-list primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.443 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/search-result-list.tsx` ships `<SearchResultList>` -- renders a list of search results with per-row title highlighting against the current query, optional snippet preview, per-row type icon slot, full keyboard navigation (ArrowUp/Down/Home/End/Enter), and infinite-scroll loader fed by an IntersectionObserver pinned to a sentinel at the bottom of the list. API: `results: SearchResultItem[]` (id/title/snippet?/type?/icon?/url?/metadata?); `query?` for title highlighting; `activeId?` (controlled) / `defaultActiveId?` (uncontrolled seed) + `onActiveChange`; `onSelect(item)`; `loading?` + `loadingState?` (default 'Loading...'); `hasMore?` + `onLoadMore`; `emptyState?` (default 'No results'); `loadMoreThreshold?` (default '200px'); `highlightSnippet?` (default false); `ariaLabel?` (default 'Search results'); `renderItem?` render-prop receives highlightedTitle/highlightedSnippet/isActive/onSelect. Root is `role="listbox"` + `tabIndex=0` + `aria-activedescendant` so keyboard focus stays on the outer container; each row is `role="option"` + aria-selected mirroring the active id. Click selects + sets active; mouse-enter sets active (no select); matches the standard combobox/command-palette navigation pattern. Keyboard nav: ArrowDown next (clamps to last), ArrowUp previous (clamps to first), Home first, End last, Enter selects active (no-op when no active). Title highlighting: `highlightMatches(text, query)` wraps matches in `<mark>` with `data-section="search-result-mark"`; case-insensitive + regex-special characters escaped so queries like `$10` or `a.b*` are matched literally; invalid regex constructions swallowed (never throws). Snippet highlighting opt-in via `highlightSnippet={true}`. Infinite scroll: when `hasMore={true}` a 1px sentinel renders at the bottom and an IntersectionObserver fires `onLoadMore` whenever the sentinel intersects the viewport; observer disconnects on unmount and re-attaches when `results.length` or `loadMoreThreshold` changes. Active row scroll-into-view on every active change. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_SEARCH_LOAD_MORE_THRESHOLD='200px'`; `escapeRegexForHighlight(query)` -- escapes `.*+?^${}()|[]\`; `highlightMatches(text, query?)` -- returns text unchanged for empty/null/whitespace-only, swallows invalid regex; `getNextActiveId(currentId, items, direction)` -- accepts 'next'|'previous'|'first'|'last', clamps at boundaries (no wrap), empty results -> null. ARIA: listbox root + per-option role; per-row `id="search-result-<item.id>"` matches active-descendant. Data attrs throughout (data-section per node: `search-result-list` / `-item` / `-icon` / `-content` / `-title` / `-snippet` / `-type` / `-mark` / `-list-empty` / `-list-loading` / `-list-sentinel`; root mirrors `data-active-id` / `data-result-count` / `data-loading` / `data-has-more`; per-row `data-item-id` / `data-active` / `data-type`). forwardRef forwarded to the listbox. 58 new test cases in search-result-list.test.tsx (3 escapeRegexForHighlight + 5 highlightMatches + 9 getNextActiveId + 1 constants + 40 component); test file ships a `MockIntersectionObserver` class wired via window assignment with an `emit(entries)` test helper for deterministic sentinel simulation; 58/58 pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for touched files. Exported via components/ui barrel. | 11.426 | UI: feature-tour primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.444 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/feature-tour.tsx` ships `<FeatureTour>` -- multi-step product tour. Each step targets an existing DOM element by CSS selector; the primitive paints a translucent mask over the page, spotlights the anchor with a ring, anchors a tooltip panel next to it, and exposes Prev/Next/Skip/Finish controls. Dismissal is persisted to `localStorage` under a per-tour id so users do not see the same tour twice. API: `tourId` (localStorage namespace); `steps: FeatureTourStep[]` (id/target/title/description?/placement?); `open?` (controlled) + `defaultOpen?` (default true; honours persisted dismissal) + `onOpenChange`; `stepIndex?` (controlled) + `defaultStepIndex?` (default 0) + `onStepChange(index)`; `onComplete` / `onSkip`; `storageKey?` overrides the namespaced key; `showMask?` (default true); `closeOnEscape?` (default true); `showProgress?` (default true); `panelOffset?` (default 12); `labels?: { prev?, next?, skip?, finish?, closeAria? }`; `containerId?` (portal root id, default 'app-portal-root'); `ariaLabel?` (default 'Feature tour'). The full overlay renders through a portal. Translucent mask covers the page (opt-out with `showMask=false`); mask click closes manually (no dismissal). Spotlight ring is drawn around the current anchor's bounding rect. Tooltip panel sits next to the anchor using `computeTourPanelPosition` (placement defaults to 'bottom'; 'auto' uses exported `resolveAutoPlacement` heuristic: bottom > top > right > left). Anchor + panel position re-measured on resize and capture-phase scroll. If selector misses, panel falls back to viewport centre + spotlight omitted + root carries `data-anchor-found="false"`. Controls: Prev disabled on first step; Next advances and becomes Finish on last step -> fires onComplete + persists dismissal + closes; Skip fires onSkip + persists dismissal + closes; X close button closes manually (no dismissal). Keyboard: Escape -> Skip (when closeOnEscape); ArrowRight -> Next; ArrowLeft -> Prev. `defaultOpen` honours persisted dismissal flag (second mount with same tourId stays closed); controlled `open` bypasses the flag for "Replay tour". Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_FEATURE_TOUR_STORAGE_PREFIX='c4:feature-tour:'` / `DEFAULT_FEATURE_TOUR_PLACEMENT='bottom'` / `DEFAULT_FEATURE_TOUR_PANEL_WIDTH=320` / `DEFAULT_FEATURE_TOUR_PANEL_OFFSET=12`; `getTourStorageKey(tourId, override?)`; `isTourDismissed` / `markTourDismissed` / `clearTourDismissal` -- swallow throws from private-mode/quota/null storage so the primitive never crashes the host; `clampStepIndex(index, total)` -- empty total -> 0, NaN -> 0, clamps to `[0, total-1]`; `resolveAutoPlacement(anchor, panel, viewport, offset?)` -- bottom > top > right > left heuristic; `computeTourPanelPosition(anchor, panel, placement?, viewport?, offset?)` -- placement math + viewport clamping so panel never scrolls off-screen. ARIA: panel `role="dialog"` + `aria-modal="true"` + aria-label + `aria-labelledby` pointing to step title id; close button `aria-label="Close tour"` (customisable). Data attrs throughout (data-section per node: `feature-tour` / `-mask` / `-spotlight` / `-panel` / `-header` / `-title` / `-description` / `-progress` / `-actions` / `-skip` / `-prev` / `-next` / `-close`; root mirrors `data-tour-id` / `data-step-index` / `data-step-id` / `data-step-count` / `data-anchor-found` / `data-placement`). 55 new test cases in feature-tour.test.tsx (2 getTourStorageKey + 6 dismissal round-trip + 6 clampStepIndex + 4 resolveAutoPlacement + 6 computeTourPanelPosition + 3 constants + 28 component); 55/55 pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for touched files. Exported via components/ui barrel. Out of scope (documented): multi-target highlighting per step (single anchor per step), branching/conditional steps based on user input (host drives stepIndex), animated panel transitions on step change (snap for v1), server-side configuration of tour content (host owns steps prop; persistent dismissal is the only state the primitive owns). Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/auto-ui-feature-tour. Worker auto-w408. Bump 1.11.444. |
| 11.427 | UI: announcement-banner primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.445 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/announcement-banner.tsx` ships `<AnnouncementBanner>` -- site-wide banner with dismissible state, variant colours (info/warning/success/error/neutral), optional link slot, and per-id dismissal persisted to localStorage so users do not see the same announcement twice. API: `id` (localStorage namespace); `variant?` (default 'info'); `title?` + `description?`; `link?: { href, label, external? }`; `icon?` (default per-variant from lucide: Info/AlertTriangle/CheckCircle2/AlertCircle/Megaphone); `dismissible?` (default true); `open?` (controlled) + `defaultOpen?` (default true; honours persisted dismissal) + `onOpenChange`; `onDismiss?`; `storageKey?` overrides namespaced key; `alignment?: 'left'|'center'` (default 'left'); `ariaLabel?` (default: title or 'Announcement'); `children?` overrides default title+description body. Renders inline as a full-width banner with top-and-bottom border. Variant colour set applies to container/icon/link via per-variant Tailwind classes; default per-variant icon replaced by `icon` prop. Default body renders title+description; `children` replaces the entire body. Link slot renders an anchor with `data-external` mirror; `external: true` swaps to `target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"`. Dismiss button (`aria-label="Dismiss announcement"`) visible by default; `dismissible=false` hides it. Clicking dismiss persists the flag via `markAnnouncementDismissed(id, storageKey)`, emits `onOpenChange(false)`, fires `onDismiss`, closes the banner (when uncontrolled). `defaultOpen` honours the persisted dismissal flag on mount; controlled `open` bypasses the flag (host re-shows the banner on demand). aria-label defaults to title string; falls back to 'Announcement' when title is a non-string ReactNode. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_ANNOUNCEMENT_STORAGE_PREFIX='c4:announcement:'` / `DEFAULT_ANNOUNCEMENT_VARIANT='info'` / `DEFAULT_ANNOUNCEMENT_ALIGNMENT='left'`; `getAnnouncementStorageKey(id, override?)`; `isAnnouncementDismissed` / `markAnnouncementDismissed` / `clearAnnouncementDismissal` -- mirror `<FeatureTour>` (11.426) pattern: namespaced key, override slot, optional storage param for tests, throws swallowed so primitive never crashes the host. ARIA: region `role="region"` + aria-label; dismiss button `aria-label="Dismiss announcement"`; external link adds target=_blank + rel=noopener noreferrer. Data attrs throughout (data-section per node: `announcement-banner` / `-icon` / `-content` / `-body` (when children) / `-title` / `-description` / `-link` / `-dismiss`; root mirrors `data-banner-id` / `data-variant` / `data-alignment` / `data-dismissible`; link mirrors `data-external`). forwardRef forwarded to root region. 42 new test cases in announcement-banner.test.tsx (2 getAnnouncementStorageKey + 6 dismissal round-trip + 3 constants + 31 component); 42/42 pass under vitest 4.1.5. Out of scope (documented): scheduled show/hide based on date ranges (host owns scheduling), server-side targeting rules (host decides which id to mount), multi-banner stacking (host composes multiple instances), A/B variant content (host picks variant + body before mount). | 11.428 | UI: changelog-viewer primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.446 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/changelog-viewer.tsx` ships `<ChangelogViewer>` -- renders a version history list with per-version expand/collapse, grouped per-entry sections (feat/fix/chore/docs/refactor/test/perf/breaking), optional diff link slot, and pluggable markdown body renderer. API: `versions: ChangelogVersion[]` (version/date?/title?/entries/body?/diffHref?); each entry has `kind`/`message`/`scope?`/`prNumber?`/`prHref?`; `defaultExpandedVersions?` + `expandedVersions?` (controlled) + `onExpandedChange`; `ariaLabel?` (default 'Changelog'); `renderMarkdown?` host-wired markdown renderer; `showDiffLinks?` (default true); `emptyState?` (default 'No release history'); `expandFirstByDefault?` (default true) opens the newest version on mount. Versions render newest-first via `sortVersionsDescending` (Date.parse; ties fall back to lexicographic version order so '1.0.2' sorts before '1.0.1'). Each version renders as an `<article>` with a clickable header (chevron + version label + optional title) that toggles a body region. Body groups entries by kind in canonical order `breaking > feat > fix > perf > refactor > docs > test > chore`; empty kind buckets skipped. Per-entry row: kind badge (per-variant Tailwind class) + optional scope + message + optional PR link (prHref swaps PR pill from span to a). Diff link renders when `diffHref` + `showDiffLinks` (aria-label="Diff for <version>"). Markdown body: when `renderMarkdown` is supplied the host renderer takes over; without it the body falls back to a `<pre>` block tagged with `data-section="changelog-viewer-markdown-fallback"`. Pure helpers exported: `CHANGELOG_ENTRY_KINDS` (canonical 8-kind order list) / `CHANGELOG_ENTRY_LABELS` (kind -> human label); `groupEntriesByKind(entries)` -- pure partition, unknown kinds fall into chore; `formatVersionTitle(version, date?)` -- 'vX.Y.Z' prefix + ' - date' when supplied; `sortVersionsDescending(versions)` -- non-mutating copy + sort. ARIA: region `role="region"` + aria-label; per-version toggle `aria-expanded` mirrors state + `aria-controls` points to body region; body `aria-labelledby` points to toggle id; diff link `aria-label="Diff for <version>"`. Data attrs throughout (data-section per node: `changelog-viewer` / `-version` / `-header` / `-toggle` / `-chevron` / `-version-label` / `-version-title` / `-diff` / `-body` / `-group` / `-group-title` / `-group-list` / `-entry` / `-entry-badge` / `-entry-scope` / `-entry-message` / `-entry-pr` / `-markdown` / `-markdown-fallback` / `-empty`; root mirrors `data-version-count`; per-version article mirrors `data-version` / `data-expanded`; each group mirrors `data-kind`; each entry mirrors `data-entry-kind`). forwardRef forwarded to root region. 44 new test cases in changelog-viewer.test.tsx (3 groupEntriesByKind + 2 formatVersionTitle + 3 sortVersionsDescending + 2 constants + 34 component); 44/44 pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.429 | UI: keyboard-shortcuts-overlay primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.447 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/keyboard-shortcuts-overlay.tsx` ships `<KeyboardShortcutsOverlay>` -- modal panel listing shortcuts grouped by category, with a platform-aware key display, an opt-in search filter, and a configurable trigger combo (`Cmd+/` on Mac, `Ctrl+/` elsewhere by default). API: `shortcuts: KeyboardShortcut[]` (id/keys/label/description?/category?); `open?` (controlled) + `defaultOpen?` (default false) + `onOpenChange`; `triggerShortcut?` (default 'mod+/'); `showSearch?` (default true) + `searchPlaceholder?`; `categoryOrder?`; `platform?: 'mac'|'windows'|'linux'|'auto'` (default 'auto'); `containerId?` portal root; `ariaLabel?` (default 'Keyboard shortcuts'); `emptyState?` (default 'No matching shortcuts'); `closeOnEscape?` (default true); `closeOnBackdropClick?` (default true); `defaultCategory?` (default 'General'). Panel renders through the canonical portal. Global `keydown` listener on `window` toggles the overlay when the configured `triggerShortcut` is hit. Default `mod+/` resolves to `Cmd+/` on Mac and `Ctrl+/` elsewhere via `resolvePlatform` + `matchesShortcut`. Each shortcut's key combo is rendered as a sequence of `<kbd>` chips wrapped in `<dt>` / `<dd>` pair so SRs announce the action label alongside the keys. Items group by `category`; entries with no category fall into `defaultCategory`. `categoryOrder` pins the section order; unlisted categories follow in insertion order. Search filters on label/description/category/raw keys (case-insensitive); empty results render the empty state. Search resets on every reopen. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_KEYBOARD_TRIGGER_SHORTCUT='mod+/'` / `DEFAULT_KEYBOARD_DEFAULT_CATEGORY='General'`; `detectPlatform()` + `resolvePlatform(platform)` -- auto detection from navigator with explicit pass-through; `getModKeyLabel` / `getAltKeyLabel` / `getShiftKeyLabel` -- platform-aware display labels; `parseShortcutString(str)` -- splits on `+`, trims, drops empties; `formatKeyLabel(key, platform?)` -- mod -> Cmd/Ctrl, alt -> Option/Alt, arrow keys -> directional, common named keys, single chars uppercase; `formatShortcut(shortcut, platform?)` -- combo formatter; `matchesShortcut(event, shortcut, platform?)` -- mod resolves to metaKey on mac / ctrlKey elsewhere, extra modifiers not in pattern fail the match (clean hotkey discrimination); `filterShortcuts(shortcuts, query)` -- trimmed case-insensitive search; `groupShortcuts(shortcuts, order?, defaultCategory?)` -- bucket + order. ARIA: panel `role="dialog"` + `aria-modal="true"` + aria-label + tabIndex=-1; search input `aria-label="Filter shortcuts"`; close button `aria-label="Close shortcuts overlay"`. Data attrs throughout (data-section per node: `keyboard-shortcuts-overlay` / `-panel` / `-header` / `-title` / `-trigger-hint` / `-trigger-key` / `-close` / `-search` / `-search-input` / `-grid` / `-empty` / `-category` / `-category-title` / `-list` / `-shortcut` / `-shortcut-label` / `-shortcut-description` / `-shortcut-keys` / `-key`; root mirrors `data-platform` / `data-shortcut-count` / `data-trigger`; per-category `data-category`; per-shortcut `data-shortcut-id`). 64 new test cases in keyboard-shortcuts-overlay.test.tsx (4 parseShortcutString + 3 resolvePlatform + 5 modKey/altKey + 8 formatKeyLabel + 2 formatShortcut + 7 matchesShortcut + 5 filterShortcuts + 3 groupShortcuts + 2 constants + 25 component); 64/64 pass under vitest 4.1.5. Out of scope (documented): capturing arbitrary shortcuts and dispatching actions (host wires handler with `matchesShortcut`), editing/customising bindings at runtime (host owns bindings array), cross-tab synchronisation of binding overrides (host owns storage), animated transitions on open/close (snap for v1). | 11.430 | UI: data-export primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.448 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/data-export.tsx` ships `<DataExport>` -- format selector (CSV/JSON/XLSX), filename input, and view-vs-all scope toggle. Export button fires `onExport({ format, filename, scope, onProgress })`; hosts stream progress back via the supplied onProgress(0..1) callback or pin a `progress` prop for fully-controlled behaviour. The primitive renders a progress bar + disabled button while the export is in flight. API: `formats?` (default ['csv','json','xlsx']); `format?` (controlled) + `defaultFormat?` (default 'csv') + `onFormatChange`; `filename?` (controlled) + `defaultFilename?` + `baseFilename?` (default 'data') + `onFilenameChange`; `scope?` (controlled) + `defaultScope?` (default 'view') + `onScopeChange`; `onExport(invocation): Promise<void>|void` (required); `progress?` (0..1 fully-controlled); `isExporting?` (controlled); `errorMessage?`; `ariaLabel?` (default 'Data export'); `scopeLabels?` (view/all); `formatLabels?`; `disabled?`; `showScopeToggle?` (default true). Filename sanitised on every keystroke via `sanitizeFilename` (strips path separators / shell-reserved chars / ASCII controls; collapses whitespace; trims). Changing format auto-rewrites filename extension via `ensureFilenameHasExtension` -- but ONLY when filename is uncontrolled; controlled filename leaves the rename to host. Export submits the form (Enter in input also submits); button disabled while in flight and when filename is empty. `onExport(invocation)` is awaited; internal isExporting flips while running (only when host has not pinned `isExporting`); invocation's `onProgress` callback updates an internal mirror so the progress bar reflects host-reported progress even when uncontrolled. `progress` prop fully-controlled when supplied. `errorMessage` opt-in renders an inline `role="alert"` row above the action button. `showScopeToggle=false` hides the radio group. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_EXPORT_FORMATS=['csv','json','xlsx']` / `DEFAULT_EXPORT_FORMAT='csv'` / `DEFAULT_EXPORT_SCOPE='view'` / `DEFAULT_EXPORT_BASE_FILENAME='data'` / `EXPORT_FORMAT_LABELS`; `getFileExtensionForFormat(format)`; `sanitizeFilename(filename)` -- conservative, removes chars that break cross-platform writes (path seps, shell meta, ASCII controls), collapses whitespace, trims; `ensureFilenameHasExtension(filename, format)` -- knows the three formats so flipping CSV -> JSON rewrites `orders.csv` -> `orders.json` instead of stacking `orders.csv.json`; `getDefaultExportFilename(format, scope, base?)` -- view -> `base.ext`, all -> `base-all.ext`, base sanitised; `formatProgressPercent(progress)` -- clamps to `[0%, 100%]`, rounds to integer, guards NaN. ARIA: form `role="form"` + aria-label; format select `aria-label="Export format"`; filename input `aria-label="Export filename"`; scope `role="radiogroup"` + each button `role="radio"` + `aria-checked`; progress `role="progressbar"` + aria-valuemin/max/now/text; submit `aria-label="Export data"`; error `role="alert"`. Data attrs throughout (data-section per node: `data-export` / `-row` / `-format-label` / `-format` / `-filename-label` / `-filename` / `-scope` / `-scope-view` / `-scope-all` / `-progress` / `-progress-track` / `-progress-fill` / `-progress-label` / `-error` / `-actions` / `-submit` / `-format-icon`; root mirrors `data-format` / `data-scope` / `data-exporting` / `data-disabled`; scope button mirrors `data-active`; progress wrapper mirrors `data-progress`). forwardRef forwarded to the `<form>`. 51 new test cases in data-export.test.tsx (1 getFileExtensionForFormat + 5 sanitizeFilename + 5 ensureFilenameHasExtension + 4 getDefaultExportFilename + 3 formatProgressPercent + 4 constants + 29 component); 51/51 pass under vitest 4.1.5.
