| 11.498 | UI: chart-line-smooth primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.516 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-smooth.tsx` ships `<ChartLineSmooth>` -- pure-SVG smoothed line chart with two distinct interpolation algorithms selectable per render. `'catmullRom'` (default) uses the canonical Catmull-Rom-to-Bezier conversion: for each segment from P[i] to P[i+1] (with P[i-1] and P[i+2] duplicated at endpoints), `CP1 = P[i] + tension/3 * (P[i+1] - P[i-1])` and `CP2 = P[i+1] - tension/3 * (P[i+2] - P[i])`. `tension` is clamped to `[0, 1]`; 0 collapses to straight lines, 0.5 (default) gives the standard Catmull-Rom weight, 1 gives full smoothing. `'monotone'` uses Fritsch-Carlson monotone cubic Hermite interpolation so the curve **never overshoots** adjacent y values -- correct for CDFs, probability data, monotonically-rising metrics, and any series where visual overshoot would mislead. Monotone algorithm: (1) compute secant slopes `d[i] = (y[i+1] - y[i]) / (x[i+1] - x[i])`; (2) initial tangents `m[i] = (d[i-1] + d[i]) / 2` interior, endpoints inherit nearest secant; (3) adjustment: if `d[i] === 0` force `m[i] = m[i+1] = 0`; else with `a = m[i]/d[i]`, `b = m[i+1]/d[i]`, if `a^2 + b^2 > 9` scale by `tau = 3 / sqrt(a^2 + b^2)`; (4) convert each Hermite segment to cubic Bezier: `CP1 = (x[i] + dx/3, y[i] + m[i]*dx/3)`, `CP2 = (x[i+1] - dx/3, y[i+1] - m[i+1]*dx/3)`. Robust against div-by-zero on flat segments (d=0 short-circuit) and vertical segments (dx=0 -> d=0). Monotone implementation sorts points by px before building the path so adopter data with out-of-order x still produces a valid monotone curve; catmullRom accepts any order and renders in input sequence. Distinct from `<ChartLine>` (linear segments only), `<ChartLineStep>` (11.497) (H+V step segments, no curve), `<ChartStream>` / `<ChartStreamgraph>` (stacked stream layers; chart-line-smooth is non-stacked per-series), `<ChartArea>` (filled area; chart-line-smooth strokes only), `<ChartScatter>` (no connecting line). Multi-series with per-series colour via 10-color palette + per-series override. `getLineSmoothBounds` auto-derives `(xMin, xMax, yMin, yMax)` from every finite point across visible series; collapsed axes expand `+/- 0.5`; empty -> unit square `(0..1, 0..1)`; explicit per-axis props override. Hover/focus on a dot opens a tooltip showing series label + formatted `x` + formatted `y` and dims every other dot to opacity 0.3 plus every other series stroke to opacity 0.3. Per-dot click handler fires with `{series, point}`. Legend toggles series visibility (controlled + uncontrolled). `onSeriesToggle({series, hidden})` fires on legend click. Mount fade via `motion-safe:animate-fade-in` (suppressed via `animate=false`). API: `series: readonly ChartLineSmoothSeries[]` ({id, label, data: readonly {x, y}[], color?}) / `curve?` ('catmullRom'\|'monotone'; default 'catmullRom') / `tension?` (0.5; clamped to [0, 1]) / `xMin?` / `xMax?` / `yMin?` / `yMax?` / `width?` (560) / `height?` (320) / `padding?` (40) / `tickCount?` (5) / `strokeWidth?` (1.75) / `dotRadius?` (3) / `lineOpacity?` (1) / `gridColor?` / `axisColor?` / `hiddenSeries?` / `defaultHiddenSeries?` / `onHiddenSeriesChange?` / `showAxis?` (true) / `showGrid?` (true) / `showDots?` (true) / `showLegend?` (true) / `showTooltip?` (true) / `animate?` (true) / `className?` / `ariaLabel?` (default 'Smooth line chart') / `ariaDescription?` / `formatValue?` / `formatX?` / `xLabel?` / `yLabel?` / `onPointClick?({series, point})` / `onSeriesToggle?({series, hidden})` / `style?`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_SMOOTH_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/LINE_OPACITY/TENSION/CURVE/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR/PALETTE`; `getLineSmoothDefaultColor(index)` -- 10-color palette modulo wrap; invalid index -> color 0; `getLineSmoothFinitePoints(points)` -- drops points with non-finite x or y; non-array -> []; `getLineSmoothBounds(series, hidden?)` -- per-axis bounds; empty -> unit square; collapsed +/- 0.5; `getLineSmoothTicks(min, max, count?)` -- evenly-spaced; collapsed -> single tick; invalid range -> []; `buildCatmullRomPath(points, tension?)` -- canonical CR-to-Bezier builder; tension clamped to [0, 1]; empty -> ''; single -> M; tension=0 collapses to straight lines (CP1=P[i], CP2=P[i+1]); `buildMonotonePath(points)` -- canonical Fritsch-Carlson monotone cubic builder; empty -> ''; single -> M; robust against d=0 and dx=0; `buildSmoothLinePath(points, curve, tension?)` -- dispatcher; `computeLineSmoothLayout(input)` -- canonical layout returning `{ series, xTicks, yTicks, xMin, xMax, yMin, yMax, curve, tension, innerWidth, innerHeight }`; per-series `{ id, label, index, color, points, finiteCount, totalCount, path }`; degenerate canvas / empty / all-hidden -> empty; drops non-finite points but keeps totalCount; monotone sorts points by px before path building; tension clamped to [0, 1]; preserves originalIndex through hidden-series filter; `describeLineSmoothChart(series, hidden?, curve?, formatValue?)` -- ARIA summary "Smooth line chart (<curve>) with N series and K points. x range <xMin> to <xMax>, y range <yMin> to <yMax>."; empty / no finite -> "No data". ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-smooth-aria-desc"` span; per-path `<path role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}>` with aria-label `"<series>: <curve> smoothed line with K points"`; per-dot `<circle role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}>` with aria-label `"<series>: x=<x>, y=<y>"`. data-section on every node (chart-line-smooth / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -x-label / -y-label / -series / -series-group / -path / -dot / -tooltip / -tooltip-label / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-y / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-button / -legend-swatch / -legend-label); root mirrors data-curve, data-tension, data-series-count, data-visible-series-count, data-total-points, data-animate; series groups mirror data-series-id, data-series-index, data-series-color, data-series-point-count, data-series-finite-count, data-hovered; dots mirror data-series-id, data-point-index, data-x, data-y, data-hovered; grid lines + ticks mirror data-axis (x/y), data-tick-value. 69 vitest cases (1 constants + 2 getLineSmoothDefaultColor + 2 getLineSmoothFinitePoints + 4 getLineSmoothBounds + 3 getLineSmoothTicks + 6 buildCatmullRomPath (empty, single, two points = 1 C, N-1 segments, tension=0 collapses to L, tension clamps to 1) + 6 buildMonotonePath (empty, single, N-1 segments, monotone ascending finite path, flat segments no div by zero, vertical segments no div by zero) + 2 buildSmoothLinePath dispatcher + 10 computeLineSmoothLayout (incl. paths differ between curves, tension clamping) + 4 describeLineSmoothChart + 30 component (incl. distinct paths for catmullRom vs monotone)) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for touched files. Exported via components/ui barrel. Out of scope (documented in docs/patches/11.498-ui-chart-line-smooth.md): cardinal/basis/bundle/step-after/step-before interpolations (use `<ChartLineStep>` for steps, `<ChartLine>` for straight); filled area below curve (use `<ChartArea>`); log-scale axes (linear only); animated transitions on data/curve/tension change (snap v1; mount fade only); per-point marker shapes (circle only). Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/c4-mgr-auto. Worker auto-w480. Bump 1.11.516. |
| 11.499 | UI: chart-line-dashed primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.517 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-dashed.tsx` ships `<ChartLineDashed>` -- pure-SVG line chart with multiple named dash patterns plus first-class per-segment styling. Five named patterns ship out of the box: `solid` (no dasharray), `dashed` (`'8 4'`), `dotted` (`'2 4'`), `dashDot` (`'8 4 2 4'`), `longDash` (`'14 6'`). All five are exported in the `LINE_DASHED_PATTERNS` dictionary so adopters can read or extend them. Raw `dashArray` overrides accepted at both series and per-point levels for fully custom patterns. Resolution order (first match wins): point `segmentDashArray` (raw) > point `segmentStyle` (named) > series `dashArray` (raw) > series `dashStyle` (named). The segment "ending at point i" reads the style from `points[i]` so an adopter marks the first point of a new style to transition mid-series (canonical use case: solid history -> dashed forecast, dotted projection, dashDot scenario). Canonical exported segment builder `buildLineDashedSegments(points, seriesDashStyle, seriesDashArray?)` walks pairs of consecutive points; consecutive segments sharing the same effective dash array merge into a single `<path>` (so the DOM stays small even for long series with one or two style changes); a dash transition closes the current sub-path at the transition point and opens a new sub-path starting at that same point so the line stays visually continuous (no gap). Edge cases: empty input or single point -> [] (no segments to render); two points -> exactly one segment; N same-dash points -> 1 segment; N alternating-dash points -> N-1 segments. Distinct from `<ChartLine>` (linear stroke without dasharray management), `<ChartLineStep>` (11.497) (step interpolation with H+V segments; chart-line-dashed uses straight L segments), `<ChartLineSmooth>` (11.498) (Bezier / monotone curves; chart-line-dashed is linear only), `<ChartArea>` (filled area below line; chart-line-dashed strokes only), `<ChartScatter>` (2D points without connecting line). Multi-series with per-series colour via 10-color palette + per-series override. `getLineDashedBounds` auto-derives `(xMin, xMax, yMin, yMax)` from every finite point across visible series; collapsed axes expand `+/- 0.5`; empty -> unit square `(0..1, 0..1)`; explicit per-axis props override. Hover/focus on a dot opens a tooltip showing series label + formatted `x` (via `formatX`) + formatted `y` (via `formatValue`) and dims every other dot to opacity 0.3 plus every other series stroke to opacity 0.3 (the hovered series stays at full opacity). Per-dot click handler fires with `{series, point}`. Legend toggles series visibility (controlled + uncontrolled). Each legend swatch is a tiny `<svg>` line stroked with the series's default dash array so the pattern is visible at a glance. `onSeriesToggle({series, hidden})` fires on legend click. Mount fade via `motion-safe:animate-fade-in` (suppressed via `animate=false`). API: `series: readonly ChartLineDashedSeries[]` ({id, label, data: readonly ChartLineDashedPoint[], color?, dashStyle?, dashArray?}) where `ChartLineDashedPoint` is `{x, y, segmentStyle?, segmentDashArray?}` / `xMin?` / `xMax?` / `yMin?` / `yMax?` / `width?` (560) / `height?` (320) / `padding?` (40) / `tickCount?` (5) / `strokeWidth?` (1.75) / `dotRadius?` (3) / `lineOpacity?` (1) / `gridColor?` / `axisColor?` / `hiddenSeries?` / `defaultHiddenSeries?` / `onHiddenSeriesChange?` / `showAxis?` (true) / `showGrid?` (true) / `showDots?` (true) / `showLegend?` (true) / `showTooltip?` (true) / `animate?` (true) / `className?` / `ariaLabel?` (default 'Dashed line chart') / `ariaDescription?` / `formatValue?` / `formatX?` / `xLabel?` / `yLabel?` / `onPointClick?({series, point})` / `onSeriesToggle?({series, hidden})` / `style?`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_DASHED_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/LINE_OPACITY/DASH_STYLE/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR/PALETTE`; `LINE_DASHED_PATTERNS` (named pattern dictionary: solid='', dashed='8 4', dotted='2 4', dashDot='8 4 2 4', longDash='14 6'); `getLineDashedDefaultColor(index)` -- 10-color palette modulo wrap; invalid index -> color 0; `getLineDashedStrokeDashArray(name)` -- named -> raw dash string; unknown / undefined -> ''; `getLineDashedFinitePoints(points)` -- drops points with non-finite x or y; non-array -> []; `getLineDashedBounds(series, hidden?)` -- per-axis bounds; empty -> unit square; collapsed +/- 0.5; `getLineDashedTicks(min, max, count?)` -- evenly-spaced; collapsed -> single tick; invalid range -> []; `resolveSegmentDashArray(point, seriesDashStyle, seriesDashArray?)` -- returns `{dashArray, styleName}` per the resolution order; `buildLineDashedSegments(points, seriesDashStyle, seriesDashArray?)` -- canonical segment builder returning `{ index, startIndex, endIndex, dashArray, styleName, path }[]`; empty / single -> []; merges same-dash consecutive segments; splits at transitions with continuous line; `computeLineDashedLayout(input)` -- canonical layout returning `{ series, xTicks, yTicks, xMin, xMax, yMin, yMax, innerWidth, innerHeight }`; per-series `{ id, label, index, color, dashStyle, defaultDashArray, points, segments, finiteCount, totalCount }`; degenerate canvas / empty / all-hidden -> empty; drops non-finite points but keeps totalCount; preserves originalIndex through hidden-series filter; segment startIndex/endIndex correspond to indices in the original data array; `describeLineDashedChart(series, hidden?, formatValue?)` -- ARIA summary "Dashed line chart with N series and K points. x range <xMin> to <xMax>, y range <yMin> to <yMax>."; empty / no finite -> "No data". ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-dashed-aria-desc"` span; per-segment `<path role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}>` with aria-label `"<series> <style> segment <startIndex> to <endIndex>"`; per-dot `<circle role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}>` with aria-label `"<series>: x=<x>, y=<y>"`. data-section on every node (chart-line-dashed / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -x-label / -y-label / -series / -series-group / -path / -dot / -tooltip / -tooltip-label / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-y / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-button / -legend-swatch / -legend-label); root mirrors data-series-count, data-visible-series-count, data-total-points, data-total-segments, data-animate; series groups mirror data-series-id, data-series-index, data-series-color, data-series-dash-style, data-series-default-dash-array, data-series-segment-count, data-series-point-count, data-series-finite-count, data-hovered; paths mirror data-series-id, data-segment-index, data-segment-start, data-segment-end, data-segment-style, data-segment-dash-array; dots mirror data-series-id, data-point-index, data-x, data-y, data-hovered; grid lines + ticks mirror data-axis (x/y), data-tick-value. 70 vitest cases (1 constants + 1 named patterns + 2 getLineDashedDefaultColor + 2 getLineDashedStrokeDashArray + 2 getLineDashedFinitePoints + 3 getLineDashedBounds + 3 getLineDashedTicks + 6 resolveSegmentDashArray + 7 buildLineDashedSegments (empty, single point, all same dash = 1 segment, mid-series transition = 2 segments, two points = 1 segment, per-segment custom dash, alternating styles = 3 segments) + 9 computeLineDashedLayout (incl. dashStyle attaches default dash, dashArray override beats named, segment splits at transitions, startIndex/endIndex from original data) + 4 describeLineDashedChart + 30 component (incl. one path per segment, dasharray per segment, legend swatch reflects pattern, distinct paths for all 5 named patterns)) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for touched files. Exported via components/ui barrel. Out of scope (documented in docs/patches/11.499-ui-chart-line-dashed.md): per-point opacity/colour transitions (one colour per series); curved segments (use `<ChartLineSmooth>`); animated dash offset (no `stroke-dashoffset` animation v1); pattern fills via `<pattern>` element (only `stroke-dasharray`); log-scale axes (linear only). Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/c4-mgr-auto. Worker auto-w481. Bump 1.11.517. |
| 11.500 | UI: chart-line-multi primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.518 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-multi.tsx` ships `<ChartLineMulti>` -- pure-SVG multi-series line chart whose defining feature is a **synchronised crosshair tooltip** that reads the y value of every visible series at the hovered x. A transparent overlay `<rect>` captures pointer events across the inner plot area; on move, the overlay snaps the crosshair to the nearest x in the union of all visible series' x values, draws a vertical dashed rule, highlights one marker per series (white-bordered to pop against the stroke), and opens a single tooltip listing the snapped x plus one row per series (swatch + label + y value). The canonical multi-series time-series dashboard interaction (Grafana / Datadog / CloudWatch). Distinct from `<ChartLine>` (per-dot tooltip on a single series), `<ChartLineStep>` (11.497) (H+V step segments; per-dot tooltip), `<ChartLineSmooth>` (11.498) (Bezier / monotone curves; per-dot tooltip), `<ChartLineDashed>` (11.499) (dash patterns; per-dot tooltip). Snap algorithm: (1) build `xUnion = collectLineMultiXValues(series, hidden)` once per render = sorted unique x values across every visible series, drops non-finite; (2) on pointer move, convert pointer's client x to data space via `xData = xMin + ((pointerLocalX - padding) / innerWidth) * xRange`; (3) `findNearestXIndex(xUnion, xData)` returns closest index via binary search (ties to lower); (4) snapped x = `xUnion[idx]`, pixel = `xUnionPx[idx]` (pre-computed); (5) per visible series, `findNearestPointInSeries(layoutPoints, snappedX)` returns the closest point in THAT series -- so series with sparse / missing samples still contribute a row to the tooltip (each series has its own nearest match, not constrained to the same sample index). `onCrosshairChange(info | null)` fires on every snap change and on pointer-leave (with null); payload is `{ x, px, xIndex, points: [{series, point}] }` -- adopters can mirror the crosshair into a sibling chart or detail panel. Per-dot `data-crosshair-hit="true"` exposes which dots are highlighted to test selectors. Multi-series with per-series colour via 10-color palette + per-series override. `getLineMultiBounds` auto-derives `(xMin, xMax, yMin, yMax)` from every finite point across visible series; collapsed axes expand `+/- 0.5`; empty -> unit square `(0..1, 0..1)`; explicit per-axis props override. Legend toggles series visibility (controlled via `hiddenSeries` / `onHiddenSeriesChange`, uncontrolled via `defaultHiddenSeries`). Hidden series drop from `xUnion` so the crosshair only snaps to x values of currently visible series and the tooltip only lists visible series. `onSeriesToggle({series, hidden})` fires on legend click. Mount fade via `motion-safe:animate-fade-in` (suppressed via `animate=false`). API: `series: readonly ChartLineMultiSeries[]` ({id, label, data: readonly {x, y}[], color?}) / `xMin?` / `xMax?` / `yMin?` / `yMax?` / `width?` (560) / `height?` (320) / `padding?` (40) / `tickCount?` (5) / `strokeWidth?` (1.75) / `dotRadius?` (3) / `lineOpacity?` (1) / `crosshairColor?` ('#94a3b8') / `gridColor?` / `axisColor?` / `hiddenSeries?` / `defaultHiddenSeries?` / `onHiddenSeriesChange?` / `showAxis?` (true) / `showGrid?` (true) / `showDots?` (true) / `showCrosshair?` (true) / `showLegend?` (true) / `showTooltip?` (true) / `animate?` (true) / `className?` / `ariaLabel?` (default 'Multi-series line chart') / `ariaDescription?` / `formatValue?` / `formatX?` / `xLabel?` / `yLabel?` / `onCrosshairChange?(info\|null)` / `onSeriesToggle?({series, hidden})` / `style?`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_MULTI_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/LINE_OPACITY/CROSSHAIR_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR/PALETTE`; `getLineMultiDefaultColor(index)` -- 10-color palette modulo wrap; invalid index -> color 0; `getLineMultiFinitePoints(points)` -- drops points with non-finite x or y; non-array -> []; `getLineMultiBounds(series, hidden?)` -- per-axis bounds; empty -> unit square; collapsed +/- 0.5; `getLineMultiTicks(min, max, count?)` -- evenly-spaced; collapsed -> single tick; invalid range -> []; `collectLineMultiXValues(series, hidden?)` -- sorted unique x values across visible series; drops non-finite; empty / all-hidden -> []; `findNearestXIndex(sortedXs, target)` -- binary search returning closest index; ties break to lower index; empty / non-finite target -> -1; clamps to lo / hi for out-of-range targets; `findNearestPointInSeries(layoutPoints, targetX)` -- linear scan returning closest point; ties to lower; empty / non-finite -> null; `buildLineMultiPath(points)` -- simple `M x y L x y ...` builder; empty -> ''; single -> M only; `computeLineMultiLayout(input)` -- canonical layout returning `{ series, xTicks, yTicks, xUnion, xUnionPx, xMin, xMax, yMin, yMax, innerWidth, innerHeight }`; per-series `{ id, label, index, color, points, finiteCount, totalCount, path }`; xUnion + xUnionPx precomputed so pointer event handlers don't recompute them; degenerate canvas / empty / all-hidden -> empty; drops non-finite points but keeps totalCount; preserves originalIndex through hidden-series filter; `describeLineMultiChart(series, hidden?, formatValue?)` -- ARIA summary "Multi-series line chart with N series and K points (synchronised crosshair). x range <xMin> to <xMax>, y range <yMin> to <yMax>."; empty / no finite -> "No data". ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-multi-aria-desc"` span; per-path `<path role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}>` with aria-label `"<series>: line with K points"`. data-section on every node (chart-line-multi / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -x-label / -y-label / -series / -series-group / -path / -dot / -crosshair / -crosshair-line / -crosshair-marker / -overlay / -tooltip / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-list / -tooltip-row / -tooltip-swatch / -tooltip-label / -tooltip-value / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-button / -legend-swatch / -legend-label); root mirrors data-series-count, data-visible-series-count, data-total-points, data-x-union-count, data-crosshair-active, data-crosshair-index, data-animate; series groups mirror data-series-id, data-series-index, data-series-color, data-series-point-count, data-series-finite-count; dots mirror data-series-id, data-point-index, data-x, data-y, data-crosshair-hit; crosshair line mirrors data-x-index, data-x-value; crosshair markers mirror data-series-id; tooltip rows mirror data-series-id; grid lines + ticks mirror data-axis (x/y), data-tick-value. 73 vitest cases (1 constants + 2 getLineMultiDefaultColor + 2 getLineMultiFinitePoints + 3 getLineMultiBounds + 2 getLineMultiTicks + 4 collectLineMultiXValues (empty, dedupe + sort, hidden filter, drops non-finite) + 7 findNearestXIndex (empty, NaN target, below range, above range, exact match, between, ties to lower) + 4 findNearestPointInSeries (empty, NaN target, closest, ties) + 3 buildLineMultiPath + 10 computeLineMultiLayout (incl. xUnion/xUnionPx built, hidden excluded from xUnion) + 4 describeLineMultiChart + 35 component (incl. core crosshair contract: hover triggers synchronised tooltip listing all visible series with one row per series + crosshair line + per-series markers, pointer-leave closes it, onCrosshairChange fires with full payload, onCrosshairChange(null) on leave, data-crosshair-hit marks correct dots, hidden series omitted from tooltip rows, showCrosshair/showTooltip toggles, formatX in x-header + formatValue in row values)) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for touched files. Exported via components/ui barrel. Out of scope (documented in docs/patches/11.500-ui-chart-line-multi.md): linear y interpolation between samples for series with sparse x (v1 snaps to nearest data point in each series); drag-to-zoom / brush selection (hover only); vertical-only or horizontal-only crosshair toggle (vertical only); animated dash on crosshair line (snap, static dasharray); log-scale axes (linear only). Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/c4-mgr-auto. Worker auto-w482. Bump 1.11.518. |
| 11.501 | UI: chart-line-zoom primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.519 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-zoom.tsx` ships `<ChartLineZoom>` -- pure-SVG multi-series line chart with **brush-to-zoom** interaction. Drag horizontally across the inner plot to define an x range; on pointer release the chart zooms into the selected window. A "Reset zoom" button appears at the top-right of the canvas while zoomed; double-clicking the overlay also resets the zoom. `setPointerCapture(pointerId)` keeps the brush tracking even when the cursor leaves the chart bounds during the drag. Brushes narrower than `minBrushWidth` pixels (default 4) are treated as a click and discarded (so accidental clicks don't collapse the view). The canonical drag-select range zoom pattern from time-series dashboards (Grafana / Datadog). Distinct from `<ChartLine>` (no interaction), `<ChartLineMulti>` (11.500) (synchronised crosshair tooltip on hover, no brush), `<ChartLineStep>` (11.497), `<ChartLineSmooth>` (11.498), `<ChartLineDashed>` (11.499). Brush lifecycle: pointer down on overlay starts brush at clamped local px; pointer move updates endPx (drawing the rect between min/max of start/end so left-to-right and right-to-left drags both work); pointer up commits via `isLineZoomBrushValid` threshold check + `clampLineZoomRange` normalisation; pointer cancel aborts without committing. Commit goes through `clampLineZoomRange(range, fullXMin, fullXMax)` which normalises reversed inputs (xMin > xMax -> swap), clamps both endpoints to full data bounds, and returns null for degenerate ranges (xMin >= xMax after clamping). A valid range goes into internal state (uncontrolled) or fires `onZoomChange(range)` (controlled via `zoom` prop). `onZoomChange(null)` fires on reset. `defaultZoom` sets the initial zoom in uncontrolled mode. Layout: `computeLineZoomLayout` accepts an optional `zoom` range; null / non-finite / degenerate falls back to full data range with `zoomActive=false`; valid zoom overrides xMin/xMax (y-bounds unchanged so vertical context stays steady). `fullXMin` / `fullXMax` always exposed so consumers can detect when zoom is active. Per-series paths use `buildLineZoomPath` (simple `M x y L x y ...`); the series `<g>` is wrapped by `clip-path: url(#clipId)` so line strokes crop cleanly at the inner plot edge -- partial points outside the visible window do not dangle outside. Cursor changes to `zoom-out` when zoomed and `crosshair` when not. Multi-series with per-series colour via 10-color palette + per-series override. Legend toggles series visibility (controlled + uncontrolled). `onSeriesToggle({series, hidden})` fires on legend click. Mount fade via `motion-safe:animate-fade-in` (suppressed via `animate=false`). API: `series: readonly ChartLineZoomSeries[]` ({id, label, data: readonly {x, y}[], color?}) / `zoom?` (controlled ChartLineZoomRange\|null) / `defaultZoom?` (uncontrolled) / `onZoomChange?(range\|null)` / `minBrushWidth?` (4) / `xMin?` / `xMax?` / `yMin?` / `yMax?` / `width?` (560) / `height?` (320) / `padding?` (40) / `tickCount?` (5) / `strokeWidth?` (1.75) / `dotRadius?` (3) / `lineOpacity?` (1) / `gridColor?` / `axisColor?` / `brushFill?` ('#3b82f6') / `brushOpacity?` (0.15) / `brushBorder?` ('#2563eb') / `hiddenSeries?` / `defaultHiddenSeries?` / `onHiddenSeriesChange?` / `showAxis?` (true) / `showGrid?` (true) / `showDots?` (true) / `showBrush?` (true) / `showResetButton?` (true) / `showLegend?` (true) / `animate?` (true) / `className?` / `ariaLabel?` (default 'Zoomable line chart') / `ariaDescription?` / `formatValue?` / `formatX?` / `xLabel?` / `yLabel?` / `resetButtonLabel?` ('Reset zoom') / `onSeriesToggle?({series, hidden})` / `style?`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_ZOOM_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/LINE_OPACITY/MIN_BRUSH_WIDTH/BRUSH_FILL/BRUSH_OPACITY/BRUSH_BORDER/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR/PALETTE`; `getLineZoomDefaultColor(index)` -- 10-color palette modulo wrap; `getLineZoomFinitePoints(points)` -- drops non-finite; non-array -> []; `getLineZoomBounds(series, hidden?)` -- per-axis bounds; empty -> unit square; collapsed +/- 0.5; `getLineZoomTicks(min, max, count?)` -- evenly-spaced; collapsed -> single tick; invalid range -> []; `buildLineZoomPath(points)` -- simple `M x y L x y ...` builder; empty -> ''; single -> M only; `clampLineZoomRange(range, fullXMin, fullXMax)` -- normalises reversed, clamps to full bounds, returns null for null/non-finite/degenerate; `isLineZoomBrushValid(startPx, endPx, minBrushWidth?)` -- pixel-width threshold check; sign-agnostic; minBrushWidth=0 always commits; non-finite endpoints -> false; `computeLineZoomLayout(input)` -- canonical layout returning `{ series, xTicks, yTicks, xMin, xMax, yMin, yMax, fullXMin, fullXMax, innerWidth, innerHeight, zoomActive }`; per-series `{ id, label, index, color, points, finiteCount, totalCount, path }`; degenerate canvas / empty / all-hidden -> empty; drops non-finite points but keeps totalCount; ignores degenerate / out-of-bounds zoom (falls back to full range); preserves originalIndex through hidden-series filter; `describeLineZoomChart(series, hidden?, zoom?, formatValue?)` -- ARIA summary; with no zoom: "Zoomable line chart with N series and K points (full range, brush-to-zoom). x range ..."; with zoom: "(zoomed to <xMin> to <xMax>)"; empty / no finite -> "No data". ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-zoom-aria-desc"` span; per-path `<path role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}>` with aria-label `"<series>: line with K points"`. data-section on every node (chart-line-zoom / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -x-label / -y-label / -series / -series-group / -path / -dot / -brush-rect / -overlay / -reset / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-button / -legend-swatch / -legend-label); root mirrors data-series-count, data-visible-series-count, data-total-points, data-zoom-active, data-zoom-min, data-zoom-max, data-full-x-min, data-full-x-max, data-brushing, data-animate; series groups mirror data-series-id, data-series-index, data-series-color, data-series-point-count, data-series-finite-count; dots mirror data-series-id, data-point-index, data-x, data-y; brush rect mirrors data-brush-start, data-brush-end; grid lines + ticks mirror data-axis (x/y), data-tick-value. 72 vitest cases (1 constants + 2 getLineZoomDefaultColor + 2 getLineZoomFinitePoints + 3 getLineZoomBounds + 2 getLineZoomTicks + 3 buildLineZoomPath + 5 clampLineZoomRange (null, non-finite, reversed, clamp to bounds, degenerate) + 5 isLineZoomBrushValid (wide valid, narrow, threshold=0, non-finite, sign-agnostic) + 11 computeLineZoomLayout (incl. zoom range applied, clamp over-wide zoom, ignore degenerate zoom, pixel mapping against zoomed range, hidden filter index preservation) + 4 describeLineZoomChart + 34 component (incl. brush rect during drag, commit on pointer up, narrow brush rejected, onZoomChange fires with payload, controlled zoom prop respected, defaultZoom on mount, reset button shown only when zoomed, reset button clears zoom, double-click resets, pointer cancel aborts, showBrush=false disables brush, ranges rendered with shifted x-tick values when zoomed)) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for touched files. Exported via components/ui barrel. Out of scope (documented in docs/patches/11.501-ui-chart-line-zoom.md): y-axis brush (only x; the canonical time-series pattern); wheel / pinch zoom (explicit drag-select only); pan-while-zoomed (reset + re-brush); synchronised crosshair tooltip while zoomed (use `<ChartLineMulti>`); animated zoom transitions (snap v1; mount fade only); log-scale axes (linear only). | 11.502 | UI: chart-line-confidence primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.520 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-confidence.tsx` ships `<ChartLineConfidence>` -- pure-SVG multi-series line chart with **confidence band overlay**: each series carries optional per-point `yLower` / `yUpper` bounds; the primitive paints a filled polygon between the two bound curves and overlays the central line on top. Canonical pattern for forecasting (point estimate + prediction interval), metric dashboards (median + p5/p95), and statistical models. Distinct from `<ChartLine>` (no band), `<ChartLineZoom>` (11.501) (brush-to-zoom, no band), `<ChartLineMulti>` (11.500) (synchronised crosshair, no band), `<ChartLineDashed>` (11.499) (dash patterns, no band), `<ChartLineSmooth>` (11.498) (curve interpolation, no band), `<ChartLineStep>` (11.497) (step interpolation, no band), `<ChartErrorBars>` (per-point vertical bars rather than a continuous filled polygon), `<ChartArea>` (filled area from baseline to single y; chart-line-confidence fills between TWO curves -- yLower and yUpper). Per-point shape `ChartLineConfidencePoint = { x: number; y: number; yLower?: number; yUpper?: number }`. A point participates in the band only when both bounds are finite AND `yLower <= yUpper` (inverted bounds are rejected; the test `hasFiniteBand` enforces this); inverted / partial / non-finite bounds collapse the point to a center-only point (`hasBand=false`). Canonical exported builder `buildLineConfidenceBandSegments(points)` walks the points in input order, pushes consecutive `hasBand=true` points into the current run, flushes the run as one closed polygon `M low[0] L low[1] ... L low[n-1] L up[n-1] ... L up[0] Z` when the run breaks (invalid band point or end of array); runs of <2 points are discarded (a single point cannot form a polygon); a series with all valid bounds produces exactly one polygon; gaps emit one polygon per contiguous run. Series-level `showBand: false` disables the band so a single chart can mix forecast series (with band) and baseline series (line only); the band fill respects `series.bandColor` if set, otherwise falls back to `series.color`. `getLineConfidenceBounds` expands the y range to include `yLower` and `yUpper` for every band-valid visible point so the band is fully visible without clipping; series with `showBand=false` are excluded from band-driven range expansion. Hover/focus on a dot opens a tooltip showing series label + formatted `x` (via `formatX`) + formatted `y` (via `formatValue`; bolded) and (when the dot has a band) a `band:` row with `yLower` to `yUpper` formatted via `formatValue`. Per-dot `data-has-band` exposes the band state to test selectors. Hover/focus also dims every other series stroke + band to ~30% so the focused series visually dominates. Per-dot click handler fires with `{series, point}`. Legend toggles series visibility (controlled via `hiddenSeries` / `onHiddenSeriesChange`, uncontrolled via `defaultHiddenSeries`). `onSeriesToggle({series, hidden})` fires on legend click. Each legend swatch is a tiny band rectangle (at `bandOpacity`) plus a line at full opacity so adopters see both styles at a glance. Mount fade via `motion-safe:animate-fade-in` (suppressed via `animate=false`). API: `series: readonly ChartLineConfidenceSeries[]` ({id, label, data: readonly {x, y, yLower?, yUpper?}[], color?, bandColor?, showBand?}) / `xMin?` / `xMax?` / `yMin?` / `yMax?` / `width?` (560) / `height?` (320) / `padding?` (40) / `tickCount?` (5) / `strokeWidth?` (1.75) / `dotRadius?` (3) / `lineOpacity?` (1) / `bandOpacity?` (0.2) / `bandStrokeWidth?` (0) / `gridColor?` / `axisColor?` / `hiddenSeries?` / `defaultHiddenSeries?` / `onHiddenSeriesChange?` / `showAxis?` (true) / `showGrid?` (true) / `showDots?` (true) / `showLegend?` (true) / `showTooltip?` (true) / `animate?` (true) / `className?` / `ariaLabel?` (default 'Line chart with confidence band') / `ariaDescription?` / `formatValue?` / `formatX?` / `xLabel?` / `yLabel?` / `onPointClick?({series, point})` / `onSeriesToggle?({series, hidden})` / `style?`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_CONFIDENCE_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/LINE_OPACITY/BAND_OPACITY/BAND_STROKE_WIDTH/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR/PALETTE`; `getLineConfidenceDefaultColor(index)` -- 10-color palette modulo wrap; `getLineConfidenceFinitePoints(points)` -- drops points with non-finite x or y (band fields not required); non-array -> []; `getLineConfidenceBounds(series, hidden?)` -- per-axis bounds with band expansion when valid; empty -> unit square; collapsed +/- 0.5; ignores partial / inverted bands; excludes hidden series; respects per-series showBand=false; `getLineConfidenceTicks(min, max, count?)` -- evenly-spaced; collapsed -> single tick; invalid range -> []; `buildLineConfidenceLinePath(points)` -- `M x y L x y ...` central line; empty -> ''; single -> M only; `buildLineConfidenceBandSegments(points)` -- canonical closed polygon builder; one polygon per contiguous run of `hasBand=true` points; runs <2 points discarded; empty / no bands -> []; emits forward then reversed path so the polygon traces lower left-to-right then upper right-to-left; `computeLineConfidenceLayout(input)` -- canonical layout returning `{ series, xTicks, yTicks, xMin, xMax, yMin, yMax, innerWidth, innerHeight }`; per-series `{ id, label, index, color, bandColor, showBand, points, bandSegments, linePath, finiteCount, bandCount, totalCount }`; per-point `{ index, x, y, yLower, yUpper, px, py, pyLower, pyUpper, hasBand }`; degenerate canvas / empty / all-hidden -> empty; drops non-finite center points but keeps totalCount; preserves originalIndex through hidden-series filter; splits band segments around invalid points; `describeLineConfidenceChart(series, hidden?, formatValue?)` -- ARIA summary "Line chart with confidence band: N series and K points, B with bands. x range <xMin> to <xMax>, y range <yMin> to <yMax>."; empty / no finite -> "No data". ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-confidence-aria-desc"` span; per-line-path `<path role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}>` with aria-label `"<series>: line with K points and B band points"`; per-dot `<circle role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}>` with band-aware aria-label (includes range when hasBand). data-section on every node (chart-line-confidence / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -x-label / -y-label / -series / -series-group / -band / -path / -dot / -tooltip / -tooltip-label / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-y / -tooltip-band / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-button / -legend-swatch / -legend-label); root mirrors data-series-count, data-visible-series-count, data-total-points, data-band-count, data-animate; series groups mirror data-series-id, data-series-index, data-series-color, data-series-band-color, data-series-show-band, data-series-point-count, data-series-finite-count, data-series-band-count, data-series-segment-count, data-hovered; band paths mirror data-series-id, data-segment-index, data-segment-start, data-segment-end, data-segment-point-count; dots mirror data-series-id, data-point-index, data-x, data-y, data-has-band, data-hovered; tooltips mirror data-has-band; grid lines + ticks mirror data-axis (x/y), data-tick-value. 70 vitest cases (1 constants + 2 getLineConfidenceDefaultColor + 2 getLineConfidenceFinitePoints + 5 getLineConfidenceBounds (incl. band inclusion, showBand=false excluded, hidden filter, inverted bands rejected) + 2 getLineConfidenceTicks + 3 buildLineConfidenceLinePath + 6 buildLineConfidenceBandSegments (empty, no bands, full coverage = 1 segment, polygon command count, gap splitting, single-point runs discarded, forward-then-reversed point order) + 11 computeLineConfidenceLayout (incl. band segments built, showBand=false honoured, gap splitting, pixel mapping, hidden filter, per-point hasBand flag for valid + invalid + inverted bounds) + 4 describeLineConfidenceChart + 34 component (incl. one band path per series with bands, no band paths for showBand=false series, tooltip band row appears only when point has band, per-dot data-has-band, bandColor override, y-range expands to include band, hidden filter)) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for touched files. Exported via components/ui barrel. Out of scope (documented in docs/patches/11.502-ui-chart-line-confidence.md): multiple stacked confidence levels per series (one band per series v1; host composes multiple instances); curved band edges via Bezier / monotone (linear segments only); animated band transitions (snap v1; mount fade only); log-scale axes (linear only); brush-to-zoom or synchronised crosshair (use `<ChartLineZoom>` / `<ChartLineMulti>` for those interactions). | 11.503 | UI: chart-line-threshold primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.521 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-threshold.tsx` ships `<ChartLineThreshold>` -- pure-SVG multi-series line chart with one or more **horizontal threshold reference lines** plus optional **zone shading** above or below each threshold. The canonical SLO / SLA / alert-threshold pattern: render a metric over time, drop a horizontal rule at the threshold value, shade the warning or danger zone, and surface which thresholds the hovered point has crossed in the tooltip. Note: the threshold spec interface is exported as `ChartLineThresholdSpec` to avoid a name clash with the component `ChartLineThreshold`. Each threshold spec ships as `ChartLineThresholdSpec = { id, label, value, color?, zone?, zoneColor?, dashArray? }`: `value` (required, must be finite) is the y position of the horizontal reference line; `zone` is `'above'` (shades from the threshold up to the top of the plot), `'below'` (shades from the threshold down to the bottom), or `'none'` (default, no zone shading); `color` (default `'#dc2626'`) drives both the reference line stroke and the zone fill unless overridden by `zoneColor`; `dashArray` (default `'6 4'`) gives the reference line a dashed look so it visually contrasts with the series strokes. The y range auto-expands via `getLineThresholdBounds` to include every finite threshold value so reference lines are always visible. Each threshold computes a `py` pixel position + `inRange` flag (skipped from rendering when out of explicit y bounds) + a zone rectangle (`above`: `{x: padding, y: padding, width: innerWidth, height: py - padding}`; `below`: `{x: padding, y: py, width: innerWidth, height: padding + innerHeight - py}`). Per-point `thresholdState[]` records `isAbove` / `isBelow` / `isInZone` for every threshold so adopters and tests can ask "did this point cross a threshold?" -- a point exactly on the threshold is neither above nor below. Render order (back-to-front): zones, grid, axes, series paths + dots, then threshold reference lines + labels on top so the crossing point is unambiguous. Threshold reference lines are focusable (`role="graphics-symbol"` + `tabIndex={0}`) with aria-labels like `"Threshold Warning at 80, above zone"`. Distinct from `<ChartLineConfidence>` (11.502) which paints per-point upper/lower bounds as a filled polygon between curves -- chart-line-threshold's zone is a fixed horizontal band defined by a single value; `<ChartLineZoom>` (11.501) (brush-to-zoom, no thresholds), `<ChartLineMulti>` (11.500) (synchronised crosshair, no thresholds), `<ChartLineDashed>` (11.499) (dash patterns; thresholds use a dashed reference line by default but support custom dashArray), `<ChartLineSmooth>` (11.498), `<ChartLineStep>` (11.497). Hover/focus on a dot dims every other series stroke + dot to ~30% and opens a tooltip with series label + formatted x + formatted y, and (when the dot has crossed any zone-shaded threshold) an `above:` row listing each crossed threshold by label + formatted value. Per-dot `data-in-zone="true"` exposes the in-zone state to tests / adopters. Per-dot click handler fires with `{series, point}`. Legend toggles series visibility (controlled + uncontrolled); the legend also shows one `data-section="chart-line-threshold-legend-threshold"` item per threshold with the dash pattern reflected in the swatch -- thresholds themselves are not toggleable. Mount fade via `motion-safe:animate-fade-in` (suppressed via `animate=false`). API: `series: readonly ChartLineThresholdSeries[]` ({id, label, data: readonly {x, y}[], color?}) / `thresholds?: readonly ChartLineThresholdSpec[]` ({id, label, value, color?, zone?, zoneColor?, dashArray?}) / `xMin?` / `xMax?` / `yMin?` / `yMax?` / `width?` (560) / `height?` (320) / `padding?` (40) / `tickCount?` (5) / `strokeWidth?` (1.75) / `dotRadius?` (3) / `lineOpacity?` (1) / `referenceOpacity?` (0.9) / `referenceWidth?` (1.25) / `zoneOpacity?` (0.1) / `gridColor?` / `axisColor?` / `hiddenSeries?` / `defaultHiddenSeries?` / `onHiddenSeriesChange?` / `showAxis?` (true) / `showGrid?` (true) / `showDots?` (true) / `showLegend?` (true) / `showTooltip?` (true) / `showThresholdLabels?` (true) / `showZones?` (true) / `animate?` (true) / `className?` / `ariaLabel?` (default 'Line chart with thresholds') / `ariaDescription?` / `formatValue?` / `formatX?` / `xLabel?` / `yLabel?` / `onPointClick?({series, point})` / `onSeriesToggle?({series, hidden})` / `style?`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_THRESHOLD_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/LINE_OPACITY/REFERENCE_OPACITY/REFERENCE_WIDTH/REFERENCE_DASH/ZONE_OPACITY/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR/DEFAULT_COLOR/PALETTE`; `getLineThresholdDefaultColor(index)` -- 10-color palette modulo wrap; `getLineThresholdFinitePoints(points)` -- drops non-finite x/y; non-array -> []; `getLineThresholdFiniteThresholds(thresholds)` -- drops thresholds with non-string ids or non-finite values; non-array -> []; `getLineThresholdBounds(series, thresholds, hidden?)` -- per-axis bounds with threshold y-range expansion; empty -> unit square; collapsed +/- 0.5; falls back to x=(0..1) when only thresholds (no series); excludes hidden series; `getLineThresholdTicks(min, max, count?)` -- evenly-spaced; collapsed -> single tick; invalid range -> []; `buildLineThresholdPath(points)` -- `M x y L x y ...` line builder; empty -> ''; single -> M only; `classifyLineThresholdPoint(pointY, threshold)` -- returns `{isAbove, isBelow, isInZone}`; point exactly on threshold -> neither; non-finite -> all false; zone='above' marks isInZone when isAbove; zone='below' when isBelow; zone='none' never; `computeLineThresholdLayout(input)` -- canonical layout returning `{ series, thresholds, xTicks, yTicks, xMin, xMax, yMin, yMax, innerWidth, innerHeight }`; per-series `{ id, label, index, color, points, finiteCount, totalCount, path }`; per-point `{ index, x, y, px, py, thresholdState }` where thresholdState[i] = `{thresholdId, isAbove, isBelow, isInZone}`; per-threshold `{ id, label, index, value, color, zoneColor, zone, dashArray, py, inRange, zoneY, zoneHeight }`; degenerate canvas -> empty; empty everywhere -> empty; emits thresholds even when no series; drops non-finite points but keeps totalCount; preserves originalIndex through hidden-series filter; honours per-threshold color and dash overrides; falls back to defaults otherwise; `describeLineThresholdChart(series, thresholds, hidden?, formatValue?)` -- ARIA summary "Line chart with N series, K points, and M threshold[s]. Thresholds: <label> at <value> (zone <zone>); ..."; empty everywhere -> "No data". ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-threshold-aria-desc"` span; per-line-path `<path role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}>` with aria-label `"<series>: line with K points"`; per-dot `<circle role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}>` with aria-label `"<series>: x=<x>, y=<y>"`; per-threshold-line `<line role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}>` with aria-label `"Threshold <label> at <value>, <zone> zone"` (zone omitted when zone='none'). data-section on every node (chart-line-threshold / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -zones / -zone / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -x-label / -y-label / -series / -series-group / -path / -dot / -references / -reference / -reference-line / -reference-label / -tooltip / -tooltip-label / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-y / -tooltip-crossed / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-button / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-threshold / -legend-threshold-swatch / -legend-threshold-label); root mirrors data-series-count, data-visible-series-count, data-total-points, data-threshold-count, data-animate; series groups mirror data-series-id, data-series-index, data-series-color, data-series-point-count, data-series-finite-count, data-hovered; dots mirror data-series-id, data-point-index, data-x, data-y, data-in-zone, data-hovered; zones mirror data-threshold-id, data-threshold-zone, data-zone-y, data-zone-height; references mirror data-threshold-id, data-threshold-index, data-threshold-value, data-threshold-zone, data-threshold-color, data-threshold-py; grid lines + ticks mirror data-axis (x/y), data-tick-value. 73 vitest cases (1 constants + 2 getLineThresholdDefaultColor + 2 getLineThresholdFinitePoints + 3 getLineThresholdFiniteThresholds + 5 getLineThresholdBounds (incl. series-only, threshold-only with x fallback, threshold expansion, hidden filter) + 2 getLineThresholdTicks + 3 buildLineThresholdPath + 7 classifyLineThresholdPoint (above / below / equal / above-zone / below-zone / none-zone / non-finite) + 13 computeLineThresholdLayout (incl. thresholds-only layout, inRange flag for out-of-range thresholds, above-zone rectangle, below-zone rectangle, zone='none' yields zoneHeight=0, per-point thresholdState classification, default color/dash fallback, per-threshold overrides) + 4 describeLineThresholdChart + 31 component (incl. one reference line per threshold, one zone rectangle per zone-shaded threshold, threshold legend items, per-dot data-in-zone flag, tooltip crossed row appears only when point crosses zone-shaded thresholds, threshold label includes formatted value, threshold aria-label with value + zone)) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for touched files. Exported via components/ui barrel. Out of scope (documented in docs/patches/11.503-ui-chart-line-threshold.md): slanted / per-x thresholds (v1 horizontal only; compose a second series as a slanted limit line); animated zone fills (snap; mount fade only); min/max zones (two thresholds defining a band; host adds one above + one below); log-scale axes (linear only); per-segment classification (per-point only in v1). Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/c4-mgr-auto. Worker auto-w485. Bump 1.11.521. |
| 11.504 | UI: chart-line-area-gradient primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.522 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-area-gradient.tsx` ships `<ChartLineAreaGradient>` -- pure-SVG multi-series line + area chart whose defining feature is a **vertical linear gradient fill** from the line stroke down to a configurable baseline. Each series declares its own `<linearGradient>` in `<defs>` (id = `${useId}-${series.id}-grad`) with `gradientUnits="userSpaceOnUse"` and y endpoints anchored to the inner-plot top + baseline pixel. Two stops: top (`stop-opacity = gradientTopOpacity`, default 0.5) and bottom (`stop-opacity = gradientBottomOpacity`, default 0) both using `series.fillColor ?? series.color` as the stop color. The area polygon below the line fills with `url(#...)`. The canonical "marketing chart" look: line on top, soft gradient shading below. `resolveLineAreaGradientBaseline(bounds, explicit?)` returns the explicit baseline when finite, otherwise falls back to `bounds.yMin` so the gradient anchors to the actual bottom of the data range; adopters who want a hard floor pass `baseline={0}` explicitly. The baseline pixel is clamped to the inner plot area so the area path stays visually meaningful even when the baseline is outside the y range. Series-level `fillColor` is separate from `color` so adopters can use a contrast colour for the line vs the fill (e.g. white line over a coloured gradient). `buildLineAreaGradientAreaPath(points, baselineY)` emits a closed polygon `M p[0] L p[1] ... L p[N-1] L p[N-1].x baselineY L p[0].x baselineY Z` -- traces the line forward then closes along the baseline back to the start; empty -> ''; single point -> a degenerate zero-width sliver for completeness; non-finite baseline -> defaults to 0. Distinct from `<ChartLine>` (line stroke only, no filled area), `<ChartArea>` (flat solid area below line; no gradient), `<ChartLineConfidence>` (11.502) (polygon between two CURVES yLower/yUpper; this primitive fills between the line and a flat baseline), `<ChartLineThreshold>` (11.503) (horizontal threshold rules with zone shading; no gradient under the line), `<ChartStackedArea>` (stacked solid areas; no per-series gradient), `<ChartLineZoom>` (11.501) / `<ChartLineMulti>` (11.500) (interactions; no gradient fill), `<ChartLineDashed>` (11.499) / `<ChartLineSmooth>` (11.498) / `<ChartLineStep>` (11.497) (line variants; no fill). Hover/focus on a dot dims every other series stroke + area to ~30%; tooltip shows series label + formatted x + formatted y; per-dot `onPointClick({series, point})`. Legend toggles series visibility (controlled + uncontrolled); each legend swatch is a tiny gradient block + horizontal line so the styling is visible at a glance. Optional baseline guideline (`showBaseline=false` by default) renders a dashed horizontal line at the baseline pixel for adopters who want the floor explicitly visible. Mount fade via `motion-safe:animate-fade-in` (suppressed via `animate=false`). API: `series: readonly ChartLineAreaGradientSeries[]` ({id, label, data: readonly {x, y}[], color?, fillColor?}) / `baseline?: number` / `gradientTopOpacity?` (0.5) / `gradientBottomOpacity?` (0) / `xMin?` / `xMax?` / `yMin?` / `yMax?` / `width?` (560) / `height?` (320) / `padding?` (40) / `tickCount?` (5) / `strokeWidth?` (1.75) / `dotRadius?` (3) / `lineOpacity?` (1) / `gridColor?` / `axisColor?` / `hiddenSeries?` / `defaultHiddenSeries?` / `onHiddenSeriesChange?` / `showAxis?` (true) / `showGrid?` (true) / `showDots?` (true) / `showLegend?` (true) / `showTooltip?` (true) / `showBaseline?` (false) / `animate?` (true) / `className?` / `ariaLabel?` (default 'Line and gradient area chart') / `ariaDescription?` / `formatValue?` / `formatX?` / `xLabel?` / `yLabel?` / `onPointClick?({series, point})` / `onSeriesToggle?({series, hidden})` / `style?`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_AREA_GRADIENT_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/LINE_OPACITY/TOP_OPACITY/BOTTOM_OPACITY/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR/PALETTE`; `getLineAreaGradientDefaultColor(index)` -- 10-color palette modulo wrap; `getLineAreaGradientFinitePoints(points)` -- drops non-finite; non-array -> []; `resolveLineAreaGradientBaseline(bounds, explicit?)` -- explicit when finite, else bounds.yMin; non-finite explicit -> bounds.yMin; `getLineAreaGradientBounds(series, hidden?, baseline?)` -- per-axis bounds; expands y range to include explicit baseline both above and below the data; empty -> unit square; collapsed +/- 0.5; `getLineAreaGradientTicks(min, max, count?)` -- evenly-spaced; collapsed -> single tick; invalid range -> []; `buildLineAreaGradientLinePath(points)` -- `M x y L x y ...`; empty -> ''; single -> M only; `buildLineAreaGradientAreaPath(points, baselineY)` -- closed polygon line + baseline closure; empty -> ''; single -> degenerate sliver; non-finite baseline -> 0; `computeLineAreaGradientLayout(input)` -- canonical layout returning `{ series, xTicks, yTicks, xMin, xMax, yMin, yMax, baseline, baselineY, topY, innerWidth, innerHeight }`; per-series `{ id, label, index, color, fillColor, points, finiteCount, totalCount, linePath, areaPath }`; degenerate canvas / empty / all-hidden -> empty; baseline pixel clamped to plot; topY = padding; preserves originalIndex through hidden-series filter; `describeLineAreaGradientChart(series, hidden?, baseline?, formatValue?)` -- ARIA summary "Line + gradient area chart with N series and K points[, baseline <baseline>]. x range ..."; empty / no finite -> "No data". ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-area-gradient-aria-desc"` span; per-line-path `<path role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}>` with aria-label `"<series>: line with K points"`; per-dot `<circle role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}>` with aria-label `"<series>: x=<x>, y=<y>"`. data-section on every node (chart-line-area-gradient / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -gradient-def / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -x-label / -y-label / -baseline / -series / -series-group / -area / -path / -dot / -tooltip / -tooltip-label / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-y / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-button / -legend-swatch / -legend-label); root mirrors data-series-count, data-visible-series-count, data-total-points, data-baseline, data-baseline-y, data-animate; gradient defs mirror data-series-id; series groups mirror data-series-id, data-series-index, data-series-color, data-series-fill-color, data-series-point-count, data-series-finite-count, data-hovered; areas + lines + dots mirror data-series-id; dots also mirror data-point-index, data-x, data-y, data-hovered; baseline (when shown) mirrors data-baseline-value; grid lines + ticks mirror data-axis (x/y), data-tick-value. 74 vitest cases (1 constants + 2 getLineAreaGradientDefaultColor + 2 getLineAreaGradientFinitePoints + 3 resolveLineAreaGradientBaseline + 5 getLineAreaGradientBounds (incl. baseline above + below data, hidden filter) + 2 getLineAreaGradientTicks + 3 buildLineAreaGradientLinePath + 5 buildLineAreaGradientAreaPath (empty, single = degenerate sliver, closed polygon line-then-baseline, command order, non-finite baseline defaults to 0) + 11 computeLineAreaGradientLayout (incl. baseline + baselineY recorded, fallback to yMin without explicit baseline, baselineY clamped to plot when baseline outside range, fillColor separate from color, topY = padding) + 5 describeLineAreaGradientChart + 35 component (incl. one gradient def + area + line path per series, area fill bound to url(#...) gradient, gradient endpoints use topY + baselineY, gradient stop opacities use prop values, fillColor flows into stop-color, baseline guideline only when showBaseline=true, multi-series renders one gradient per series)) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for touched files. Exported via components/ui barrel. Out of scope (documented in docs/patches/11.504-ui-chart-line-area-gradient.md): stacked gradient areas (use `<ChartStackedArea>`); pattern fills (linear gradient only); horizontal gradients (vertical only -- the canonical fade-to-baseline pattern); animated gradient transitions on data change (snap v1; mount fade only); multi-stop gradients (one top + one bottom stop v1; adopters compose custom `<linearGradient>` if needed); log-scale axes (linear only). | 11.505 | UI: chart-line-area-stacked primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.523 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-area-stacked.tsx` ships `<ChartLineAreaStacked>` -- pure-SVG stacked line + area chart with **two stacking modes**. `'absolute'` (default) cumulatively sums layers; the y axis shows raw totals. `'percent'` normalises per-x so each column sums to 1.0; the y axis shows percentages (`fmtPercent` formatter; defaults to `'{n*100}%'`). Distinct from the v1 `<ChartStackedArea>` which paints only the absolute mode and lacks the per-x normalisation that `'percent'` provides; chart-line-area-stacked also strokes the top edge of each layer (line on top of fill) so layer boundaries stay visually crisp even when `areaOpacity` is low. The canonical sample grid is the sorted unique union of x values across visible series (via `collectLineAreaStackedXValues`); each layer is evaluated at every x in the union. Series that lack a sample at some x contribute 0 at that x (zero-fill); negative contributions clamp to 0 so stacks never invert. For each x, `total[x] = sum(clamp(raw_i, 0))` is computed once. In absolute mode each layer's normalised value is the raw value; in percent mode it is `raw / total[x]` (or 0 if `total[x] === 0`). Stacking: `yBottom = cumulative sum of layers below`; `yTop = yBottom + normalised`; layers stack from series-index 0 (bottom) upward. y range auto: absolute -> `(0, max(total[x]))`; percent -> `(0, 1)`; explicit `yMin` / `yMax` override either mode. Each layer's `linePath` traces the top edge `M p[0] L p[1] ... L p[n-1]`; `areaPath` traces the top forward + bottom reversed + Z so the area exactly fills the band between adjacent layer tops. Distinct from `<ChartLineAreaGradient>` (11.504) (single-series area below line with vertical gradient; not stacked), `<ChartLineThreshold>` (11.503), `<ChartLineConfidence>` (11.502), `<ChartLineZoom>` (11.501), `<ChartLineMulti>` (11.500) (synchronised crosshair but independent lines, not stacked), `<ChartLineDashed>` (11.499), `<ChartLineSmooth>` (11.498), `<ChartLineStep>` (11.497). Hover/focus on a dot dims every other series stroke + area to ~30%; tooltip shows series label + formatted x + raw y + a `share:` row with formatted percent + total at that x (canonical chart value in percent mode, useful secondary read in absolute mode). Per-dot `onPointClick({series, point})` callback. Legend toggles series visibility (controlled + uncontrolled). Each legend swatch is the layer's solid fill at `areaOpacity`. Mount fade via `motion-safe:animate-fade-in` (suppressed via `animate=false`). API: `series: readonly ChartLineAreaStackedSeries[]` ({id, label, data: readonly {x, y}[], color?}) / `stackMode?` ('absolute'\|'percent'; default 'absolute') / `xMin?` / `xMax?` / `yMin?` / `yMax?` / `width?` (560) / `height?` (320) / `padding?` (40) / `tickCount?` (5) / `strokeWidth?` (1.4) / `dotRadius?` (3) / `lineOpacity?` (1) / `areaOpacity?` (0.6) / `gridColor?` / `axisColor?` / `hiddenSeries?` / `defaultHiddenSeries?` / `onHiddenSeriesChange?` / `showAxis?` (true) / `showGrid?` (true) / `showDots?` (true) / `showLegend?` (true) / `showTooltip?` (true) / `animate?` (true) / `className?` / `ariaLabel?` (default 'Stacked line + area chart') / `ariaDescription?` / `formatValue?` / `formatX?` / `formatPercent?` / `xLabel?` / `yLabel?` / `onPointClick?({series, point})` / `onSeriesToggle?({series, hidden})` / `style?`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_AREA_STACKED_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/LINE_OPACITY/AREA_OPACITY/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR/STACK_MODE/PALETTE`; `getLineAreaStackedDefaultColor(index)` -- 10-color palette modulo wrap; `getLineAreaStackedFinitePoints(points)` -- drops non-finite x/y; non-array -> []; `collectLineAreaStackedXValues(series, hidden?)` -- sorted unique union of x values across visible series; drops non-finite; empty / all-hidden -> []; `buildLineAreaStackedYLookup(series)` -- Map<x, y> of finite samples; `computeLineAreaStackedLayout(input)` -- canonical layout returning `{ series, xTicks, yTicks, xUnion, totals, xMin, xMax, yMin, yMax, innerWidth, innerHeight, stackMode }`; per-series `{ id, label, index, color, points, areaPath, linePath, finiteCount, totalCount, raw }`; per-point `{ index, x, yRaw, yBottom, yTop, px, pyBottom, pyTop, share }`; degenerate canvas / empty / all-hidden -> empty; preserves originalIndex through hidden-series filter; zero-fills missing x values; clamps negatives to 0; percent normalisation collapses to 0 share when total === 0; `describeLineAreaStackedChart(series, hidden?, stackMode?, formatValue?)` -- ARIA summary "Stacked line + area chart (<mode>) with N series and K points across M x samples. x range <xMin> to <xMax>."; empty / no finite -> "No data". ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-area-stacked-aria-desc"` span; per-line-path `<path role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}>` with aria-label `"<series>: stacked layer top with K samples"`; per-dot `<circle role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}>` with band-aware aria-label (includes share in percent mode). data-section on every node (chart-line-area-stacked / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -x-label / -y-label / -series / -series-group / -area / -path / -dot / -tooltip / -tooltip-label / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-y / -tooltip-share / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-button / -legend-swatch / -legend-label); root mirrors data-stack-mode, data-series-count, data-visible-series-count, data-total-points, data-x-union-count, data-animate; series groups mirror data-series-id, data-series-index, data-series-color, data-series-point-count, data-series-finite-count, data-hovered; dots mirror data-series-id, data-point-index, data-x, data-y-raw, data-y-bottom, data-y-top, data-share, data-hovered; grid lines + ticks mirror data-axis (x/y), data-tick-value. 56 vitest cases (1 constants + 2 getLineAreaStackedDefaultColor + 2 getLineAreaStackedFinitePoints + 3 collectLineAreaStackedXValues + 1 buildLineAreaStackedYLookup + 16 computeLineAreaStackedLayout (incl. stackMode + xUnion recorded, layer N bottom = layer N-1 top, peak total drives auto yMax in absolute, percent normalisation = 1, share matches yTop - yBottom, zero-total at x collapses layer, zero-fills missing x values, negative clamps to 0, linePath + areaPath built, hidden filter preserves originalIndex, tick positions inside canvas) + 4 describeLineAreaStackedChart + 27 component (incl. one area + line per series, dots per finite point, tooltip with share row, formatValue for absolute axis, % for percent axis, axis switches when stackMode prop changes, hidden filter, per-dot stacked coordinates exposed as data attrs)) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for touched files. Exported via components/ui barrel. Out of scope (documented in docs/patches/11.505-ui-chart-line-area-stacked.md): curved layer tops (Bezier / monotone; linear only v1); wiggle / silhouette baselines (use `<ChartStreamgraph>`); negative values stacked below baseline (clamps to 0 in v1; host normalises); animated stack transitions on data / stackMode change (snap v1; mount fade only); log-scale axes (linear only).
