Legend shows "(window W)" per series. 60 vitest cases (4 defaults + 2 getLineRollingMinMaxDefaultColor + 2 getLineRollingMinMaxFinitePoints + 3 normaliseLineRollingMinMaxWindow + 7 computeRollingMinMax trailing (leading nulls, w=1 passthrough, w>n -> all null, non-finite within window, all-non-finite window, non-array/empty, normalises invalid window) + 1 centered + 1 edge + 3 buildLineRollingMinMaxBandPaths + 3 buildLineRollingMinMaxEnvelopePath + 12 computeLineRollingMinMaxLayout (empty, degenerate, paths + bands, y expansion, per-series window + mode overrides, hide band, hidden filter, bounds, tick step, per-point min/max populated when window fills) + 2 describeLineRollingMinMaxChart + 20 component render (empty, raw line + min/max envelopes + bands, hide line / envelopes / band, dots with data-rolling-min + data-rolling-max attrs, hide dots, ARIA, tooltip rolling min/max + n/a fallback, hide on leave + via prop, onPointClick, legend window, uncontrolled + controlled visibility + onHiddenSeriesChange, hide legend, data-window + data-mode root attrs, animate flag + class, ref, displayName)) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for the new files. Exported via components/ui barrel. Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/c4-mgr-auto. Worker auto-w508. Bump 1.11.544. |
| 11.527 | UI: chart-line-velocity primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.545 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-velocity.tsx` ships `<ChartLineVelocity>` -- pure-SVG multi-series line chart that **decorates every point with a directional arrow marker** indicating velocity (up / down / flat) and optionally angled along the local tangent. Two arrow modes: `'tangent'` (default; chevron tip at the point with the head pointing along the pixel-space slope from the previous point, so it visually follows the line) and `'sign'` (chevron points straight up / down / right based on the sign of dy). Per-arrow color reflects direction: green for up, red for down, slate for flat -- making the visual flow of the data immediately obvious without reading values. The canonical "where is this metric heading" visualisation -- adds a glanceable directional cue to a standard line chart. Distinct from `<ChartLineRate>` (11.521; plots the DERIVATIVE as a separate line chart -- velocity DECORATES the original line with directional arrows), `<ChartLineMinMax>` (11.517; triangle markers at the ABSOLUTE extremes ONLY; velocity puts an arrow at every point), `<ChartLineMarker>` (11.506; configurable STATIC markers per point; velocity arrows ENCODE direction dynamically from the data), `<ChartLineEvent>` (11.515; event icons on a DEDICATED TRACK at specific timestamps; velocity is per-point and inline with the line), `<ChartLineCumulative>` (11.520; running sum transform), `<ChartLineAnomaly>` (11.524; z-score outliers). API: `series: readonly ChartLineVelocitySeries[]` (`{id, label, data: readonly {x, y}[], color?, positiveColor?, negativeColor?, zeroColor?, arrowMode?}`); chart-level `arrowMode?` (default 'tangent') / `arrowSize?` (default 8); `hiddenSeries` / `defaultHiddenSeries` / `onHiddenSeriesChange`; `xMin`/`xMax`/`yMin`/`yMax`; `width` (560) / `height` (320) / `padding` (40) / `tickCount` (5); `strokeWidth` (1.75) / `arrowStrokeWidth` (1.5) / `dotRadius` (2.5); `lineOpacity` (1) / `arrowOpacity` (0.85); `gridColor` / `axisColor`; `showAxis` / `showGrid` / `showDots` / `showLegend` / `showTooltip` / `showArrows` / `showLine` / `animate` (all true by default); `className` / `ariaLabel` (default 'Velocity line chart with arrow markers') / `ariaDescription`; `formatValue(n)` / `formatX(n)` / `formatMagnitude(n)`; `xLabel` / `yLabel`; `onPointClick({series, point})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_VELOCITY_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/ARROW_SIZE/ARROW_STROKE_WIDTH/LINE_OPACITY/ARROW_OPACITY/POSITIVE_COLOR/NEGATIVE_COLOR/ZERO_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR/ARROW_MODE/PALETTE`; `ALL_CHART_LINE_VELOCITY_ARROW_MODES`; `getLineVelocityDefaultColor(index)` -- 10-color cycle, NaN / +-Inf / negative -> color 0; `getLineVelocityFinitePoints(points)` -- finite-only filter; `computeVelocity(prev, curr)` -- returns `{dx, dy, magnitude, angle, direction}` or null on missing / non-finite inputs; angle in DATA space (math convention: 0 is +x, +pi/2 is +y); direction: 'up' when dy > 0, 'down' when dy < 0, 'right' when dy === 0; `buildLineVelocityArrowPath(tipX, tipY, angleRadians, size, wingDegrees?)` -- canonical chevron path builder; emits two stroke segments (no fill needed) from the tip backward at `+/- wingDegrees` (default 150 deg) from the reverse direction; non-finite or `size <= 0` -> ''; in SVG pixel space y is inverted (down is +y visually), so callers passing a DATA-space angle should negate (-angle) to render visual up; `computeLineVelocityLayout({series, arrowMode?, arrowSize?, hiddenSeries?, width, height, padding, tickCount?, xMin?, xMax?, yMin?, yMax?})` returns `{series, xTicks, yTicks, xMin, xMax, yMin, yMax, innerWidth, innerHeight, totalPoints, visibleSeriesCount}`; per-series `{id, label, index (stable original), color, positiveColor, negativeColor, zeroColor, arrowMode, points, path, stats, finiteCount, totalCount}`; stats: `{finiteCount, upCount, downCount, flatCount, maxMagnitude, meanMagnitude}`; per-point `{index, x, y, px, py, velocity, arrowAngle, arrowPath, arrowColor}`; first point of each series has no previous neighbour -> no arrow (`arrowPath: ''`, `arrowAngle: null`); for tangent mode, the arrow angle is computed from the PIXEL-space segment delta so it visually aligns with the slope; for sign mode, pixel angles are -PI/2 (up), +PI/2 (down), 0 (right); per-series arrowMode + direction color overrides win; honors hidden filter / bounds overrides; degenerate canvas / empty / all-hidden -> empty layout; `describeLineVelocityChart(series, arrowMode?, hidden?, formatValue?)` -- ARIA summary "Velocity chart (<mode> arrows) across N series (K points). A: <u> up, <d> down, <f> flat, max |v| <m>, mean |v| <m>; ..." (No data when empty / no finite). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-velocity-aria-desc"` span; line path + dots `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; dot aria-label includes velocity direction + magnitude. data-section on every node (chart-line-velocity / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -x-label / -y-label / -series / -series-group / -path / -arrow / -dot / -tooltip / -tooltip-label / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-y / -tooltip-direction / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-button / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats); root mirrors data-series-count, data-visible-series-count, data-total-points, data-arrow-mode, data-animate; series group mirrors data-series-id, data-series-index, data-series-color, data-series-arrow-mode, data-series-point-count, data-series-finite-count, data-series-up-count, data-series-down-count, data-series-flat-count, data-series-max-magnitude, data-series-mean-magnitude, data-hovered; arrows mirror data-series-id, data-point-index, data-direction, data-magnitude; dots mirror data-series-id, data-point-index, data-x, data-y, data-direction, data-magnitude, data-hovered. Tooltip surfaces series label + x + bold y + coloured `velocity: <direction> (<magnitude>)` row (the first point shows `velocity: start of series` since there's no previous neighbour). Legend shows `(U+ / D- / F=)` counts per series. 58 vitest cases (6 defaults + 2 getLineVelocityDefaultColor + 2 getLineVelocityFinitePoints + 6 computeVelocity (null cases, non-finite, dx/dy/magnitude, up/down/right classification, angle math convention) + 3 buildLineVelocityArrowPath (empty cases, two-segment chevron with tip preserved, different angles produce different paths) + 11 computeLineVelocityLayout (empty, degenerate, arrows for all but first, up/down/flat counts, max+mean magnitude, sign-mode pixel angles, tangent-mode follows pixel slope, per-series arrowMode override, hidden filter, color overrides, bounds + tick step) + 3 describeLineVelocityChart + 25 component render (empty case, line + arrows (n-1 for n points), hide arrows, hide line, data-direction + data-magnitude per arrow, dots, hide dots, ARIA, tooltip direction+magnitude, first-point "start of series" branch, hide on leave + via prop, onPointClick, data-series-* stats, legend +/- counts, uncontrolled + controlled visibility + onHiddenSeriesChange, hide legend, data-arrow-mode root attr, animate flag + class, ref, displayName)) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for the new files. Exported via components/ui barrel. Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/c4-mgr-auto. Worker auto-w509. Bump 1.11.545. |
| 11.528 | UI: chart-line-acceleration primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.546 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-acceleration.tsx` ships `<ChartLineAcceleration>` -- pure-SVG multi-series line chart that **plots the discrete second derivative** (acceleration / "rate of rate change") of an input series. For every triple of consecutive finite samples sorted by x ascending the chart computes `v_left = (y_b - y_a) / (x_b - x_a)`, `v_right = (y_c - y_b) / (x_c - x_b)`, and `accel = (v_right - v_left) / ((x_c - x_a) / 2)` -- the standard non-uniform finite-difference second derivative anchored at the middle index. Result rendered against a horizontal zero-acceleration baseline with sign-shaded regions painting concavity (amber positive = concave up "accelerating", cyan negative = concave down "decelerating"); segments crossing zero are split exactly at the inflection point. Three anchor modes: 'center' (default; anchor at middle x), 'left' (anchor at leftmost x), 'right' (anchor at rightmost x). The canonical inflection-point / second-order momentum / "is growth itself accelerating or decelerating" visualisation. Distinct from `<ChartLineRate>` (11.521; FIRST derivative -- this is the SECOND), `<ChartLineVelocity>` (11.527; decorates original line with directional arrows; acceleration plots a transformed series), `<ChartLineCumulative>` (11.520; running SUM), `<ChartLineMovingAvg>` (11.513; windowed average), `<ChartLineTrend>` (11.512; regression), `<ChartLineMinMax>` (11.517; data extremes), and `<ChartLineAnomaly>` (11.524; z-score outliers). API: `series: readonly ChartLineAccelerationSeries[]` (`{id, label, data: readonly {x,y}[], color?, mode?}`); chart-level `mode?` (default 'center'); `hiddenSeries` / `defaultHiddenSeries` / `onHiddenSeriesChange`; `xMin`/`xMax`/`yMin`/`yMax`; `width` (560) / `height` (320) / `padding` (40) / `tickCount` (5); `strokeWidth` (1.75) / `dotRadius` (3); `lineOpacity` (1) / `fillOpacity` (0.18); `zeroDashArray` ('4 3') / `zeroColor` ('#94a3b8') / `positiveColor` ('#f59e0b') / `negativeColor` ('#0891b2'); `gridColor` / `axisColor`; `showAxis` / `showGrid` / `showDots` / `showLegend` / `showTooltip` / `showSignFill` / `showZeroLine` / `animate` (all true by default); `className` / `ariaLabel` (default 'Acceleration (second-derivative) line chart') / `ariaDescription`; `formatValue(n)` / `formatX(n)` / `formatAcceleration(n)` (sign-aware fallback); `xLabel` / `yLabel`; `onSampleClick({series, sample})` / `onRegionClick({series, region})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_ACCELERATION_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/LINE_OPACITY/FILL_OPACITY/ZERO_DASH/ZERO_COLOR/POSITIVE_COLOR/NEGATIVE_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR/MODE/PALETTE`; `ALL_CHART_LINE_ACCELERATION_MODES`; `getLineAccelerationDefaultColor(index)` -- 10-color cycle, NaN / +-Inf / negative -> color 0; `getLineAccelerationFinitePoints(points)` -- finite-only filter; `computeAcceleration(points, mode?)` -- canonical second-derivative helper; returns (n-2) samples for n finite inputs sorted by x ascending; drops triples where ANY of left/right segment dx is zero; output's `index` is position in OUTPUT array; `leftIndex` / `midIndex` / `rightIndex` reference ORIGINAL input array; less than 3 finite samples / non-array -> []; sorts unsorted input; modes: 'center' anchors at b.x (default), 'left' anchors at a.x, 'right' anchors at c.x; y = x^2 yields constant +2 acceleration; linear yields 0; `findLineAccelerationZeroCrossing(x1, a1, x2, a2)` -- crossing x via absolute-value proportion; null on same-sign / endpoint-on-zero / degenerate / non-finite; `computeLineAccelerationStats(samples)` -- returns `{finiteCount, maxAcceleration, minAcceleration, averageAcceleration, positiveCount, negativeCount, zeroCount, totalAbsoluteArea}`; totalAbsoluteArea is the trapezoid sum of |acceleration| between successive samples; empty -> all zeros; `computeLineAccelerationLayout({series, mode?, hiddenSeries?, showSignFill?, positiveColor?, negativeColor?, width, height, padding, tickCount?, xMin?, xMax?, yMin?, yMax?})` returns `{series, xTicks, yTicks, xMin, xMax, yMin, yMax, zeroY, innerWidth, innerHeight, totalSamples, visibleSeriesCount}`; per-series `{id, label, index (stable original), color, mode, samples, regions, path, stats, finiteCount, totalCount}`; per-sample `{index, x, acceleration, px, py, isPositive, isNegative, leftIndex, midIndex, rightIndex}`; per-region `{index, startX, endX, startAcceleration, endAcceleration, isPositive, path (closed `M..L..L..L..Z`), fillColor, area}`; y range ALWAYS expanded to include 0; degenerate / empty / all-hidden -> empty layout; honors hidden filter / showSignFill / bounds overrides; per-series mode override wins; `describeLineAccelerationChart(series, mode?, hidden?, formatValue?)` -- ARIA summary "Acceleration (second-derivative) line chart across N series (K samples). A: avg <a>, max <m>, min <m> (P accel, N decel); ..." (No data when empty / no finite). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-acceleration-aria-desc"` span; acceleration line + dots `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; zero baseline `role="graphics-symbol"`; dot aria-label includes acceleration value + concavity tag. data-section on every node (chart-line-acceleration / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -x-label / -y-label / -regions / -region / -zero-line / -series / -series-group / -path / -dot / -tooltip / -tooltip-label / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-acceleration / -tooltip-mode / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-button / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats); root mirrors data-series-count, data-visible-series-count, data-total-samples, data-mode, data-animate; series group mirrors data-series-id, data-series-index, data-series-color, data-series-mode, data-series-sample-count, data-series-finite-count, data-series-max-acceleration, data-series-min-acceleration, data-series-average-acceleration, data-series-positive-count, data-series-negative-count, data-series-zero-count, data-hovered; dots mirror data-series-id, data-sample-index, data-x, data-acceleration, data-left-index, data-mid-index, data-right-index, data-acceleration-sign ('positive'|'negative'|'zero'), data-hovered; regions mirror data-series-id, data-region-index, data-region-is-positive, data-region-start-x, data-region-end-x, data-region-fill-color, data-region-area. Tooltip surfaces series label + x + coloured `acceleration:` row tinted by sign with `(concave up)` / `(concave down)` suffix + `mode: <kind> (from #i - #j - #k)` row indicating the three original-array indices that produced the sample. Per-dot fill is positive / negative / line color based on sign so accelerating vs decelerating intervals pop visually. Legend stats show `(P+/N-)` counts per series. 68 vitest cases (5 defaults + 2 getLineAccelerationDefaultColor + 2 getLineAccelerationFinitePoints + 11 computeAcceleration (empty / <3, n-2 outputs, y=x^2 constant +2, linear gives 0, anchor modes center/left/right, sorts unsorted, preserves ORIGINAL fromIndex/midIndex/rightIndex, drops dx=0 triples, sign-flip detection in flipping series, non-array) + 5 findLineAccelerationZeroCrossing + 4 computeLineAccelerationStats + 12 computeLineAccelerationLayout (empty, degenerate, paths + samples + regions + stats, y expansion to include zero, zeroY, crossing splits into 2 regions, hide sign fill, hidden filter, per-series mode override, bounds overrides, tick step, per-sample isPositive/isNegative) + 3 describeLineAccelerationChart + 24 component render (empty case, line, dots (n-2), sign-fill + hide, zero baseline + hide, data-direction + leftIndex/midIndex/rightIndex on dots, hide dots, ARIA, tooltip acceleration + concavity tag + mode + indices, hide on leave + via prop, onSampleClick + onRegionClick payloads, legend +/- counts, uncontrolled + controlled visibility + onHiddenSeriesChange, hide legend, data-mode root attr, animate flag + class, ref, displayName)) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for the new files. Exported via components/ui barrel. Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/c4-mgr-auto. Worker auto-w510. Bump 1.11.546. |
| 11.529 | UI: chart-line-correlation primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.547 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-correlation.tsx` ships `<ChartLineCorrelation>` -- pure-SVG **dual-axis line chart** that pairs a `primary` series on the left y-axis with a `secondary` series on the right y-axis, each normalised to its own independent y range, and computes the **Pearson correlation coefficient r** between the two over their shared x samples. The r value is surfaced as a coloured badge in the top-left corner with magnitude + qualitative strength (strong/moderate/weak/none) + direction (positive/negative/neutral). The canonical "two metrics on different scales -- do they correlate?" view: temperature vs ice cream sales, ad spend vs revenue, traffic vs errors. Distinct from `<ChartLineComparison>` (11.511; two curves on the SAME y scale with difference shading -- correlation uses SEPARATE y axes so vastly different units can sit on the same chart, plus surfaces the statistical correlation as a badge), `<ChartLineMulti>` (11.500; multi-series with synchronised crosshair tooltips, SAME y scale), `<ChartLineResidual>` (11.525; observed minus predicted at the SAME x -- correlation pairs two INDEPENDENT series), `<ChartLineTrend>` (11.512; single-series regression line -- correlation is the BILATERAL statistic between two series), `<ChartLineAnomaly>` (11.524; outliers in one series), and external-reference primitives. API: `primary: ChartLineCorrelationSeries | null` / `secondary: ChartLineCorrelationSeries | null` (`{id, label, data: readonly {x,y}[], color?}`); `xMin`/`xMax`; `primaryYMin`/`primaryYMax`/`secondaryYMin`/`secondaryYMax` (separate per axis); `width` (560) / `height` (320) / `padding` (48) / `tickCount` (5); `strokeWidth` (1.75) / `dotRadius` (3) / `lineOpacity` (1); `gridColor` / `axisColor` / `positiveColor` ('#16a34a') / `negativeColor` ('#dc2626') / `neutralColor` ('#64748b'); `strongThreshold` (0.7) / `moderateThreshold` (0.4) / `weakThreshold` (0.2); `showAxis` / `showGrid` / `showDots` / `showLegend` / `showTooltip` / `showCorrelationBadge` / `showRightAxis` / `animate` (all true by default); `className` / `ariaLabel` (default 'Dual-axis correlation line chart') / `ariaDescription`; `formatValue(n)` / `formatX(n)` / `formatR(n)` (sign-aware fallback); `xLabel` / `primaryYLabel` / `secondaryYLabel`; `onPointClick({series, point})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_CORRELATION_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/LINE_OPACITY/PRIMARY_COLOR/SECONDARY_COLOR/POSITIVE_COLOR/NEGATIVE_COLOR/NEUTRAL_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR/STRONG_THRESHOLD/MODERATE_THRESHOLD/WEAK_THRESHOLD/PALETTE`; `getLineCorrelationDefaultColor(index)` -- 10-color cycle, NaN / +-Inf / negative -> color 0; `getLineCorrelationFinitePoints(points)` -- finite-only filter; `pairLineCorrelationByX(a, b)` -- returns one `ChartLineCorrelationPair` per shared x value where both series have a finite sample; `indexA` / `indexB` reference ORIGINAL input arrays; output sorted by x ascending; null inputs / empty / no overlap -> []; non-finite samples dropped; `computePearsonCorrelation(pairs)` -- canonical formula `r = Σ (ya-mean_a)(yb-mean_b) / sqrt(Σ (ya-mean_a)^2 * Σ (yb-mean_b)^2)`; returns `{r, ok, meanA, meanB}`; ok=false when <2 pairs or either side has zero variance (constant signal -> no meaningful correlation); clamps r to [-1, 1] to defend against floating-point overshoot; `classifyLineCorrelationStrength(r, strong?, moderate?, weak?)` -- `'strong' | 'moderate' | 'weak' | 'none'` based on |r|; non-finite r -> 'none'; non-finite thresholds fall back to defaults (0.7/0.4/0.2); `classifyLineCorrelationDirection(r)` -- `'positive' | 'negative' | 'neutral'`; non-finite -> 'neutral'; `computeLineCorrelation(primary, secondary, ...thresholds?)` -- one-call wrapper returning `{r, ok, pairCount, strength, direction, meanA, meanB}`; `computeLineCorrelationLayout({primary, secondary, width, height, padding, tickCount?, xMin?, xMax?, primaryYMin?, primaryYMax?, secondaryYMin?, secondaryYMax?, strongThreshold?, moderateThreshold?, weakThreshold?})` returns `{primary, secondary, correlation, xTicks, yLeftTicks, yRightTicks, xMin, xMax, innerWidth, innerHeight, totalPoints}`; per-series `{id, label, axis ('left'|'right'), color, points, path, finiteCount, totalCount, yMin, yMax}`; each axis uses its INDEPENDENT y range so a 60-80 series can share the chart with a 100-320 series without either being squished; degenerate canvas / null both -> empty layout; bounds overrides per axis; `describeLineCorrelationChart(primary, secondary, formatValue?)` -- ARIA summary "Dual-axis correlation chart: Temperature (left axis, N points) and Sales (right axis, M points). Pearson r = <r> (<strength> <direction>) across K paired samples." (Falls back to "No correlation computable" when r is NaN). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-correlation-aria-desc"` span; line paths + dots `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; dot aria-label includes axis side. data-section on every node (chart-line-correlation / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis (data-axis 'x'|'y-left'|'y-right') / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -x-label / -y-label / -series / -series-group / -path / -dot / -tooltip / -tooltip-label / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-y / -badge / -badge-r / -badge-strength / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-axis); root mirrors data-total-points, data-r, data-pair-count, data-strength, data-direction, data-correlation-ok, data-animate; series group mirrors data-series-id, data-series-axis, data-series-color, data-series-point-count, data-series-finite-count, data-series-y-min, data-series-y-max, data-hovered; dots mirror data-series-id, data-series-axis, data-point-index, data-x, data-y, data-hovered; badge mirrors data-r, data-strength, data-direction, data-pair-count, data-correlation-ok. Left y-axis is coloured by the primary series's color (so ticks visually associate with the left curve), right y-axis is coloured by the secondary. Badge sits in the top-left corner with a coloured border + text matching the direction (green positive, red negative, slate neutral); when r is not computable the badge shows "r = n/a" and "no correlation". Tooltip surfaces the series label (in series colour) + axis side suffix + x + bold y. 59 vitest cases (4 defaults + 2 getLineCorrelationDefaultColor + 2 getLineCorrelationFinitePoints + 6 pairLineCorrelationByX (null inputs, empty, exact x match with ORIGINAL indices, drops unmatched, drops non-finite, sorts ascending) + 6 computePearsonCorrelation (<2 pairs, perfect +1, perfect -1, ok=false on constant, mean recording, clamping under huge values) + 4 classifyLineCorrelationStrength (strong/moderate/weak/none, custom thresholds, non-finite r, non-finite threshold) + 2 classifyLineCorrelationDirection + 3 computeLineCorrelation + 7 computeLineCorrelationLayout (both null, degenerate, separate axes, correlation result on layout, separate tick sets, separate y ranges per axis, bounds overrides per axis) + 3 describeLineCorrelationChart + 20 component render (empty case, paths per axis, left + right axes + omit right, badge with r + strength, badge "no correlation" fallback, omit badge, ARIA, dots per axis, hide dots, tooltip with axis label, hide on leave + via prop, onPointClick, legend with left/right axes, omit legend, data-* root attrs (r, pair-count, strength, direction, ok), animate flag + class, ref, displayName)) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for the new files. Exported via components/ui barrel. Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/c4-mgr-auto. Worker auto-w511. Bump 1.11.547. |
| 11.530 | UI: chart-line-distribution primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.548 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-distribution.tsx` ships `<ChartLineDistribution>` -- pure-SVG multi-series line chart **with an inline marginal histogram on the right side** showing the distribution of the y values. Main panel takes the bulk of the canvas (`1 - histogramRatio` ~= 80% by default) for the time series; the right panel renders a sideways histogram with shared y axis -- bin heights match bin widths in y space, bin bar widths scale to the counts. Each bin segments per-series so multiple series stack into the same bin with distinct colours. Mean + median reference lines span both panels so the central tendency is unambiguous. The canonical "metric over time + what's the typical range" visualisation used in monitoring dashboards. Distinct from `<ChartHistogram>` (11.473; standalone histogram chart -- this primitive EMBEDS the histogram as a marginal next to a line chart, sharing the y axis), `<ChartLinePercentile>` (11.514; percentile bands derived from SAMPLE GROUPS AT EACH X; distribution bins all y values globally), `<ChartLineConfidence>` (11.502; per-point bounds), `<ChartLineAnomaly>` (11.524; outlier highlighting via z-score), `<ChartLineMinMax>` (11.517; markers at extremes only), `<ChartLineRollingMinMax>` (11.526; sliding-window envelope), `<ChartLineSparkBar>` (11.523; sparkline form factor with bar + line for TWO METRICS -- distribution overlays a histogram of the LINE's y values). API: `series: readonly ChartLineDistributionSeries[]` (`{id, label, data: readonly {x,y}[], color?}`); `binCount?` (default 12; positive integer >= 1, non-finite / <1 clamps to 1, fractional floored); `histogramRatio?` (default 0.2; clamped to [0, 0.6]); `histogramGap?` (default 8); `binGap?` (default 1); `hiddenSeries` / `defaultHiddenSeries` / `onHiddenSeriesChange`; `xMin`/`xMax`/`yMin`/`yMax`; `width` (560) / `height` (320) / `padding` (40) / `tickCount` (5); `strokeWidth` (1.75) / `dotRadius` (3); `lineOpacity` (1) / `binOpacity` (0.85); `histogramBgColor` ('#f8fafc') / `meanColor` ('#0f172a') / `medianColor` ('#9333ea') / `meanDashArray` ('4 3') / `medianDashArray` ('3 2'); `gridColor` / `axisColor`; `showAxis` / `showGrid` / `showDots` / `showLegend` / `showTooltip` / `showHistogram` / `showHistogramBg` / `showMeanLine` / `showMedianLine` / `animate` (all true by default); `className` / `ariaLabel` (default 'Line chart with inline distribution histogram') / `ariaDescription`; `formatValue(n)` / `formatX(n)`; `xLabel` / `yLabel`; `onPointClick({series, point})` / `onBinClick({bin})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_DISTRIBUTION_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/LINE_OPACITY/BIN_OPACITY/BIN_COUNT/HISTOGRAM_RATIO/HISTOGRAM_GAP/BIN_GAP/HISTOGRAM_BG/MEAN_COLOR/MEDIAN_COLOR/MEAN_DASH/MEDIAN_DASH/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR/PALETTE`; `getLineDistributionDefaultColor(index)` -- 10-color cycle, NaN / +-Inf / negative -> color 0; `getLineDistributionFinitePoints(points)` -- finite-only filter; `normaliseLineDistributionBinCount(value)` -- positive integer >= 1 (floor fractional, non-finite / <1 / non-numeric -> 1); `computeLineDistributionMean(values)` -- arithmetic mean, drops non-finite; empty / non-array -> 0; `computeLineDistributionMedian(values)` -- sorted-ascending median; drops non-finite; odd n returns middle, even n averages two middles; empty / non-array -> 0; `computeLineDistributionBins(series, binCount, rangeMin, rangeMax)` -- bins all finite y values across every series into binCount equal-width bins spanning `[rangeMin, rangeMax]`; values exactly equal to rangeMax go into the LAST bin (inclusive upper bound); values outside the range are dropped; non-array / `rangeMin >= rangeMax` -> []; returns one entry per bin with `{index, binMin, binMax, binMid, total, perSeries}`; only non-zero `perSeries` entries are emitted (compact); input order of perSeries preserves the input series order; `computeLineDistributionLayout({series, hiddenSeries?, binCount?, histogramRatio?, histogramGap?, binGap?, width, height, padding, tickCount?, xMin?, xMax?, yMin?, yMax?})` returns `{series, bins, xTicks, yTicks, xMin, xMax, yMin, yMax, innerWidth, innerHeight, mainTrackLeft, mainTrackRight, histogramTrackLeft, histogramTrackRight, combinedMean, combinedMedian, combinedFiniteCount, totalPoints, visibleSeriesCount, binCount, maxBinTotal}`; per-series `{id, label, index (stable original), color, points, path, finiteCount, totalCount, stats: {finiteCount, mean, median, min, max}}`; per-bin `{index, binMin, binMax, total, y, height, segments}` with per-series segments inheriting the series colour; bin segment widths scale to `maxBinTotal` so the longest bin always fills the histogram panel; canvas split horizontally with main track (1 - ratio) on left + histogram track (ratio) on right separated by histogramGap; degenerate canvas / empty / all-hidden -> empty layout; honors hidden filter (both layout series + histogram bins); bounds overrides; `describeLineDistributionChart(series, binCount?, hidden?, formatValue?)` -- ARIA summary "Line chart with inline distribution histogram (N bins) across M series (K points). Combined mean <m>, median <m>. A: mean <a>, median <a>; B: mean <b>, median <b>." (No data when empty / no finite). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-distribution-aria-desc"` span; line paths + dots + bin segments `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; mean + median reference lines `role="graphics-symbol"`. data-section on every node (chart-line-distribution / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -x-label / -y-label / -histogram-bg / -mean-line / -median-line / -histogram / -bin-group / -bin / -series / -series-group / -path / -dot / -tooltip / -bin-tooltip / -bin-tooltip-range / -bin-tooltip-total / -bin-tooltip-series / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-button / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats); root mirrors data-series-count, data-visible-series-count, data-total-points, data-bin-count, data-combined-mean, data-combined-median, data-combined-finite-count, data-max-bin-total, data-animate; series group mirrors data-series-id, data-series-index, data-series-color, data-series-point-count, data-series-finite-count, data-series-mean, data-series-median, data-hovered; dots mirror data-series-id, data-point-index, data-x, data-y, data-hovered; bin segments mirror data-bin-index, data-series-id, data-segment-index, data-count; bin groups mirror data-bin-index, data-bin-total, data-bin-min, data-bin-max, data-hovered. Two tooltip kinds: POINT tooltip (hovering a dot) shows series label in its colour + x + bold y; BIN tooltip (hovering a histogram segment) shows the bin range + bold total + a per-series row in each series's colour with its count; bin tooltip takes precedence when both are hovered. Legend shows mean per series. 67 vitest cases (4 defaults + 2 getLineDistributionDefaultColor + 2 getLineDistributionFinitePoints + 3 normaliseLineDistributionBinCount + 3 computeLineDistributionMean (empty, computes, drops non-finite) + 4 computeLineDistributionMedian (empty, odd, even, drops non-finite with even-n result) + 9 computeLineDistributionBins (invalid range, non-array, equal-width, placement at boundaries, rangeMax inclusive in last bin, combines per-series counts, out-of-range dropped, non-finite dropped, only non-zero perSeries emitted) + 13 computeLineDistributionLayout (empty, degenerate, paths + stats, bins, track split, ratio clamp, combined mean/median, maxBinTotal, hidden filter affects bins, tick step, bounds overrides, bin range uses active y) + 3 describeLineDistributionChart + 24 component render (empty, paths, histogram bars + hide, histogram bg + hide, mean + median + hide, data-bin-* on segments, dots + hide, ARIA, point tooltip on dot, bin tooltip on bar with per-series rows, hide tooltips via prop, onPointClick + onBinClick payloads, legend mean, uncontrolled + controlled visibility + onHiddenSeriesChange, hide legend, data-bin-count + combined-mean/median root attrs, animate flag + class, ref, displayName)) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.531 | UI: chart-line-period-compare primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.549 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-period-compare.tsx` ships `<ChartLinePeriodCompare>` -- pure-SVG line chart overlaying **the same metric across two periods** (current vs prior) on a shared x axis. Current period renders solid; prior period renders dashed (`priorDashArray='4 3'`) and slightly faded (`priorOpacity=0.6`) so the two read as primary signal + reference. A coloured change badge in the top-left surfaces the total period-over-period percent change with direction (up / down / flat). Each current-period dot carries per-point `data-delta`, `data-percent-change`, and `data-direction` attrs derived from the matching prior-period sample; prior dots leave them empty / `'flat'`. The canonical "this week vs last week" / "Q4 vs Q3" / "YoY change" visualisation used in monitoring dashboards, sales reports, and any case where the question is "are we doing better or worse than last time". Distinct from `<ChartLineComparison>` (11.511; two **different** curves with difference shading -- period-compare is the SAME metric across two periods), `<ChartLineCorrelation>` (11.529; dual-axis for two **unrelated** metrics -- period-compare uses a SINGLE shared y axis), `<ChartLineForecast>` (11.519; historical solid + forecast dashed split on the TIME axis -- period-compare overlays two periods on a SHARED x), `<ChartLineTarget>` (11.518; vs a flat target -- period-compare's reference is another curve), `<ChartLineMulti>` (11.500; multi-series + crosshair, no semantic "current vs prior" distinction), and `<ChartLineResidual>` (11.525; observed minus predicted -- a model diagnostic vs period-compare's business comparison). API: `current` / `prior` each `{id, label, data, color?}` (either can be `null` for a single-period view); `flatEpsilon` (default 0) widens the "flat" equality band for the direction classification; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showLegend`/`showTooltip`/`showChangeBadge`/`animate`; `priorDashArray`/`priorOpacity`; `upColor`/`downColor`/`flatColor` for the badge + per-point tooltip tinting; standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX` / `formatPercent`; `onPointClick({series, point})`. Pure helpers exported: `getLinePeriodCompareDefaultColor(index)` -- 10-color cycle, NaN / +-Inf / negative -> color 0; `getLinePeriodCompareFinitePoints(points)` -- finite-only filter; `pairLinePeriodCompareByX(current, prior)` -- pairs the two series by **exact x match**; returns one entry per shared x value where both periods have a finite sample, sorted ascending by x; `currentIndex` / `priorIndex` reference ORIGINAL input arrays; `delta = currentY - priorY`; `percentChange = delta / |priorY|` with `priorY === 0` collapsing to 0 to avoid division by zero; `classifyLinePeriodCompareDirection(percentChange, epsilon?)` -- `'up' | 'down' | 'flat'`; non-finite -> `'flat'`; non-finite / negative epsilon falls back to default; `computeLinePeriodCompareTotals(current, prior, flatEpsilon?)` -- returns `{currentTotal, priorTotal, totalDelta, totalPercentChange, ok, direction, pairCount, currentCount, priorCount}`; `totalPercentChange = totalDelta / |priorTotal|` with priorTotal=0 collapsing to 0; `ok=false` when both series empty; `computeLinePeriodCompareLayout({current, prior, ...})` -- returns `{current, prior, totals, xTicks, yTicks, xMin, xMax, yMin, yMax, innerWidth, innerHeight, totalPoints}`; per-series carries `{id, label, role ('current' | 'prior'), color, points, path, finiteCount, totalCount}`; per-point on CURRENT additionally carries `{delta, percentChange, direction}` derived from the prior-period pair; per-point on PRIOR leaves these `null` / `'flat'`; degenerate canvas / both null / no series -> empty layout; `describeLinePeriodCompareChart(current, prior, formatValue?, formatPercent?)` -- ARIA summary (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-period-compare-aria-desc"` span; per-line path + per-dot `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; dot aria-label includes the period role. data-section on every node (chart-line-period-compare / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -x-label / -y-label / -series / -series-group / -path / -dot / -tooltip / -tooltip-label / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-y / -tooltip-delta / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-button / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -badge / -badge-direction / -badge-percent / -badge-delta); root mirrors `data-pair-count`, `data-current-total`, `data-prior-total`, `data-total-delta`, `data-total-percent-change`, `data-direction`, `data-totals-ok`, `data-animate`; series group mirrors `data-series-id`, `data-series-role` (`'current' | 'prior'`), `data-series-color`, `data-series-finite-count`; dots mirror `data-series-id`, `data-series-role`, `data-point-index`, `data-x`, `data-y`, `data-delta`, `data-percent-change`, `data-direction`; badge mirrors the same direction + percent + delta + pair-count. Tooltip on current dots shows the series label in its colour with a `(current)` suffix, x, bold y, and a coloured `vs prior: +N (+P%)` row tinted by direction; prior dots omit the delta row since they ARE the reference. 56 vitest cases (2 defaults + 2 getLinePeriodCompareDefaultColor + 2 getLinePeriodCompareFinitePoints + 9 pairLinePeriodCompareByX (null inputs, empty, exact x match with ORIGINAL indices, drops unmatched, `|prior|` denominator, prior=0 collapse, non-finite filter, ascending sort) + 4 classifyLinePeriodCompareDirection + 5 computeLinePeriodCompareTotals + 9 computeLinePeriodCompareLayout (both null, degenerate, per-role series, attaches per-point delta/percent/direction to current series, records totals on layout, flatEpsilon honored, per-point delta=null when no match in prior, joint y range, bounds overrides, tick step) + 2 describeLinePeriodCompareChart + 21 component (empty, both paths with role attr, prior path has stroke-dasharray, change badge with percent + direction, omit badge, dots with role attr, current dots have data-delta + data-percent-change + data-direction, prior dots have empty data-delta + flat direction, hide dots, ARIA, tooltip with vs-prior row for current, omit delta row for prior, hide tooltip on leave + via prop, onPointClick payload, legend with current + prior items, omit legend, data-* root attrs, animate flag + class, ref, displayName)) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for the new files. Exported via components/ui barrel. Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/c4-mgr-auto. Worker auto-w513. Bump 1.11.549. |
| 11.532 | UI: chart-line-streak primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.550 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-streak.tsx` ships `<ChartLineStreak>` -- pure-SVG line chart highlighting **consecutive same-direction runs** ("streaks") in a series. Base line renders as a dimmed gray reference (`baseOpacity=0.4`); each detected streak overlays a thicker coloured stroke segment on top of it -- green for up runs, red for down runs, slate for flat runs. A run badge in the top-left calls out the longest streak across the chart with direction icon (`↑`/`↓`/`→`), length, and label. The canonical "5 consecutive winning days" / "3 quarters in a row of decline" / "win streak / loss streak" visualisation used in sports, trading, and operational monitoring. Distinct from `<ChartLineVelocity>` (11.527; per-point directional ARROW markers -- streak is run-based, a contiguous SEGMENT, not isolated arrows), `<ChartLineAcceleration>` (11.528; second derivative magnitudes per-point), `<ChartLineAnomaly>` (11.524; z-score outlier highlighting -- rare DEVIATING points -- streak is the OPPOSITE, consecutive AGREEING points), `<ChartLineMinMax>` (11.517; single global min/max marker), `<ChartLineTrend>` (11.512; one global linear regression rule per series), and `<ChartLinePeriodCompare>` (11.531; two-period overlay). API: `series: readonly ChartLineStreakSeries[]` (`{id, label, data, color?}`); `minLength` (default 2); `flatEpsilon` (default 0); toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showLegend`/`showTooltip`/`showRunBadge`/`animate`; `upColor`/`downColor`/`flatColor`/`baseColor`; `highlightWidth`/`baseOpacity`/`highlightOpacity`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility; `formatValue`/`formatX`; `onPointClick({series, point})`. Pure helpers exported: `getLineStreakDefaultColor`, `getLineStreakFinitePoints`, `normaliseLineStreakMinLength` (clamps to >= 2; floors fractional; non-finite -> 2), `normaliseLineStreakFlatEpsilon` (non-finite / negative -> 0), `classifyLineStreakStep` (non-finite -> flat), `computeLineStreaks` (scans sorted-by-x finite points and returns runs `{startIndex, endIndex, length, direction, startX, endX, startY, endY, delta}`; boundary point belongs to both adjacent runs; short-runs dropped per minLength; sorts ascending; drops non-finite), `computeLineStreakLongest` (picks the longest; `{0, 'flat'}` when empty), `computeLineStreakLayout` (returns per-series `{id, label, color, points, path, runs, finiteCount, totalCount, longestRunLength, longestRunDirection, upRunCount, downRunCount, flatRunCount}`; per-point `{index, x, y, px, py, direction, inStreak, streakIndex}`; per-run `{startIndex, endIndex, length, direction, color, path, startX, endX, startY, endY, delta}`; degenerate canvas / empty / all-hidden -> empty layout), and `describeLineStreakChart`. ARIA: root `role="region"` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"`, per-shape `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`. Run aria-label includes direction + length; dot aria-label includes "in <direction> streak" when applicable. data-attrs: root `data-series-count`, `data-visible-series-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-min-length`, `data-longest-length`, `data-longest-direction`, `data-animate`; base path `data-kind="base"`; run paths `data-direction`, `data-length`, `data-start-index`, `data-end-index`, `data-delta`; dots `data-direction`, `data-in-streak`, `data-streak-index`; series groups `data-series-id`, `data-series-color`, `data-series-finite-count`, `data-series-run-count`, `data-series-longest-length`, `data-series-longest-direction`, `data-series-up-run-count`, `data-series-down-run-count`, `data-series-flat-run-count`. Tooltip surfaces series label, x, bold y, and (when in-streak) a coloured `<direction> streak` row. Legend shows per-series run count. 65 vitest cases (defaults, helpers, computeLineStreaks incl. boundary-shared peak / single up run / drops short runs / keeps length=2 at minLength=2 / flatEpsilon classifies small steps as flat into one run / sort ascending / drops non-finite / run endpoint metadata, computeLineStreakLongest empty + picks longest, computeLineStreakLayout incl. per-series runs/points / marks points in run with direction / up/down/flat counts / up/down color on runs / bounds overrides / visible series count, describeLineStreakChart incl. No data + per-series longest, component render incl. empty / base + overlays / data-length on overlays / dots + hide / in-streak attr / direction attr / run badge with direction + length + omit / ARIA / root data-* attrs / tooltip with streak row + hide on leave + omit via prop / onPointClick payload / legend with run count + toggle from legend uncontrolled + omit legend / alternating series 0 streaks at minLength=3 / animate flag + class / ref / displayName) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for the new files. Exported via components/ui barrel. Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/c4-mgr-auto. Worker auto-w514. Bump 1.11.550. |
| 11.533 | UI: chart-line-momentum primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.551 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-momentum.tsx` ships `<ChartLineMomentum>` -- pure-SVG line chart with a **momentum oscillator** in a dedicated sub-panel below the main price line. Momentum at index `i` = `value[i] - value[i - period]` (classic technical-analysis indicator) -- first `<period>` indices are null because the look-back window is unfilled. Sub-panel y axis **centers on zero** when both signs are present, with a dashed zero reference line spanning the chart, and the momentum line above zero is shaded green while below is shaded red. The canonical "momentum oscillator" view used in finance / trading dashboards to spot bullish vs bearish phases and divergences from price. Distinct from `<ChartLineRate>` (11.521; per-segment first derivative `(y[i+1] - y[i]) / (x[i+1] - x[i])` depending on x step -- momentum is a fixed-lag y-only diff), `<ChartLineAcceleration>` (11.528; second derivative -- momentum is single-stage), `<ChartLineVelocity>` (11.527; per-point directional arrows -- momentum is a continuous line in a separate panel), `<ChartLineMovingAvg>` (11.513; SMA smoothing -- momentum produces a separate derived signal in its own panel), `<ChartLineTrend>` (11.512; global linear regression line), and `<ChartLineCumulative>` (11.520; running sum -- momentum is a DIFFERENCE, not an accumulation). API: `series: readonly ChartLineMomentumSeries[]` (`{id, label, data, color?, period?}`); `period` (default 10); `subHeightRatio` (default 0.32; clamped to [0.1, 0.9]); `positiveColor` / `negativeColor` / `zeroColor`; `fillOpacity` (default 0.2); toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showLegend`/`showTooltip`/`showSignBadge`/`showMomentumFill`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility; `formatValue` / `formatMomentum` / `formatX`; `momentumLabel` (default 'Momentum'); `onPointClick({series, point})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_MOMENTUM_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/MOMENTUM_STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/PERIOD/SUB_HEIGHT_RATIO/FILL_OPACITY/POSITIVE_COLOR/NEGATIVE_COLOR/ZERO_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR/PALETTE`; `getLineMomentumDefaultColor` (10-color cycle; NaN / negative -> color 0); `getLineMomentumFinitePoints`; `normaliseLineMomentumPeriod` (clamps to >= 1; floors fractional; non-finite -> 10); `normaliseLineMomentumSubHeightRatio` (clamps to [0.1, 0.9]; non-finite -> 0.32); `classifyLineMomentumSign` (`'positive' | 'negative' | 'zero'`; null / non-finite -> zero; exact 0 -> zero); `computeLineMomentumValues(points, period?)` -- canonical helper; sorts ascending; drops non-finite; returns same-length `(number | null)[]` with first `<period>` entries null and subsequent entries `y[i] - y[i - period]`; period >= length yields all-null; empty / null -> []; `buildLineMomentumSamples(points, period?)` -- combines above into per-point `{index, x, y, momentum, sign}` records; `computeLineMomentumLayout({series, ...})` -- returns `{series, xTicks, yTicks, momentumTicks, xMin, xMax, yMin, yMax, momentumMin, momentumMax, innerWidth, mainHeight, subHeight, subTop, totalPoints, visibleSeriesCount}`; per-series carries `{id, label, color, period, points, path, momentumPath, positivePath, negativePath, finiteCount, totalCount, momentumValidCount, positiveCount, negativeCount, zeroCount, latestMomentum, latestSign}`; per-point carries `{index, x, y, px, py, momentum, momentumPy, sign}` (momentumPy null whenever momentum null); main + sub panels sum to inner height minus gap; degenerate canvas / empty / all-hidden -> empty layout; `describeLineMomentumChart(series, hidden?, period?, formatMomentum?)` -- ARIA summary including per-series period + latest momentum (n/a when unfilled). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-momentum-aria-desc"` span; main path + momentum path + every dot `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; momentum aria-label includes period; dot aria-label includes momentum value + sign (or n/a). data-section on every node (chart-line-momentum / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -x-label / -y-label / -sub-label / -series / -series-group / -path / -momentum-path / -fill / -zero-line / -dot / -tooltip / -tooltip-label / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-y / -tooltip-momentum / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-period / -legend-total-points / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-value / -badge-label); root mirrors `data-series-count`, `data-visible-series-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-period`, `data-dominant-sign`, `data-animate`; main path `data-kind="main"`; momentum path `data-kind="momentum"`; momentum fills `data-sign` (positive / negative); dots `data-momentum`, `data-sign`; series groups `data-series-id`, `data-series-color`, `data-series-period`, `data-series-finite-count`, `data-series-momentum-valid-count`, `data-series-positive-count`, `data-series-negative-count`, `data-series-zero-count`, `data-series-latest-sign`. Tooltip surfaces series label, x, bold y, and a coloured `momentum(<period>): <value> (<sign>)` row (n/a when unfilled). Sign badge in top-left calls out the dominant sign (strongest abs latest) with icon ▲/▼/◆. Legend shows per-series period (n=<period>). 68 vitest cases (defaults, helpers, computeLineMomentumValues incl. leading nulls + diff / all-null when period >= length / default period fallback / sort ascending / drops non-finite, buildLineMomentumSamples, computeLineMomentumLayout incl. centered momentum range / latestMomentum / per-point momentumPy / sign-split paths / per-series period override / main + sub heights, describeLineMomentumChart incl. No data + period + n/a, component render incl. empty / main path kind=main / momentum path kind=momentum / dashed zero line / pos + neg fills + hide via prop / sign badge with dominant sign + omit / dots with momentum + sign + data-momentum / early dots empty momentum / hide dots / ARIA / root data-* attrs / tooltip momentum row + n/a + hide on leave + omit via prop / onPointClick payload / legend (n=<period>) + toggle + omit / sub-panel label / animate flag + class / ref / displayName) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.534 | UI: chart-line-control primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.552 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-control.tsx` ships `<ChartLineControl>` -- pure-SVG **Statistical Process Control (SPC)** chart. Each series renders against three horizontal reference rules: the **center line (CL)** at the series mean, an **upper control limit (UCL)** at `mean + kSigma * sigma`, and a **lower control limit (LCL)** at `mean - kSigma * sigma`. Default `kSigma = 3` (canonical Western Electric Rule 1 threshold). A faint in-control band fills the area between UCL and LCL; points inside the band render in the in-control colour while points outside render in the out-of-control colour (red by default) at a slightly larger radius. A control badge in the top-left calls out the total count of out-of-control points with an alert / ok state. The canonical SPC chart used in manufacturing quality control, SRE error-budget tracking, and any context where the question is "is the process in statistical control?". Distinct from `<ChartLineConfidence>` (11.502; per-point upper/lower bounds supplied EXTERNALLY -- control chart DERIVES limits statistically from the series), `<ChartLineThreshold>` (11.503; arbitrary user-defined horizontal thresholds + zones -- control chart's limits are the standardized SPC mean +- k*sigma rules), `<ChartLineBaseline>` (11.510; single flat reference -- control chart has THREE coordinated rules driven by the same statistics), `<ChartLinePercentile>` (11.514; distribution percentile bands per x bucket requiring multi-sample input -- control chart takes single-sample input and applies global statistics), `<ChartLineMinMax>` (11.517; extrema markers -- control chart's rules are STATISTICAL limits, not observed extrema), `<ChartLineTarget>` (11.518; vs flat user-supplied target with over/under shading -- control chart has BOTH upper and lower limits derived from data), `<ChartLineForecast>` (11.519; historical/forecast split on time axis -- control chart applies same rules across the whole series), and `<ChartLineAnomaly>` (11.524; per-point z-score outliers with optional rolling window -- control chart adds the canonical CL/UCL/LCL **rules** as the centrepiece and emphasizes the COUNT of out-of-control points as an alert metric). API: `series: readonly ChartLineControlSeries[]` (`{id, label, data, color?, kSigma?, centerLine?, sigma?}`; per-series kSigma/centerLine/sigma always beats chart-level defaults; `centerLine` overrides data-derived mean for target chart; `sigma` overrides data-derived population standard deviation); `kSigma` (default 3); `centerColor`/`limitColor`/`outColor`/`inColor`; `fillOpacity` (default 0.08); `limitDashArray` (default '6 4'); toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showLegend`/`showTooltip`/`showControlBadge`/`showInControlFill`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility; `formatValue` / `formatX` / `formatSigma`; `onPointClick({series, point})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_CONTROL_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/LIMIT_STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/K_SIGMA/LIMIT_OPACITY/FILL_OPACITY/LIMIT_DASH/CENTER_COLOR/LIMIT_COLOR/OUT_COLOR/IN_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR/PALETTE`; `getLineControlDefaultColor` (10-color cycle; NaN / negative -> color 0); `getLineControlFinitePoints` (finite-only filter); `normaliseLineControlKSigma` (non-finite / non-positive -> 3); `computeLineControlStats(points, {kSigma?, centerLine?, sigma?})` -- canonical SPC stats; returns `{mean, sigma, ucl, lcl, count, ok, kSigma}`; empty/null -> ok=false with zeros; sigma is the POPULATION standard deviation (divide by N) so single point yields sigma=0 with UCL=LCL=mean; centerLine/sigma overrides beat data-derived values while data-derived sigma is still computed from actual values when centerLine is overridden; `classifyLineControlState(value, stats)` -- `'in' | 'above' | 'below'`; non-finite -> 'in'; not-ok stats -> 'in'; boundary values treated as 'in' (only STRICT excursions flag); `computeLineControlLayout({series, ...})` -- returns `{series, xTicks, yTicks, xMin, xMax, yMin, yMax, innerWidth, innerHeight, totalPoints, visibleSeriesCount}`; y range expanded to cover both data envelope AND UCL/LCL limits so rules are always visible; per-series carries `{id, label, color, stats, points, path, centerPy, uclPy, lclPy, outOfControlCount, aboveCount, belowCount, finiteCount, totalCount}`; per-point carries `{index, x, y, px, py, state, outOfControl, deviation}` (deviation = z-score (y-mean)/sigma, 0 when sigma=0); degenerate canvas / empty / all-hidden -> empty layout; `describeLineControlChart(series, hidden?, kSigma?, formatValue?)` -- ARIA summary with per-series CL/sigma/UCL/LCL/out count (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-control-aria-desc"` span; CL + UCL + LCL `role="graphics-symbol"` with descriptive aria-label (including sigma multiple); process path + dots `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; dot aria-label includes the state. data-section on every node (chart-line-control / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -x-label / -y-label / -series / -series-group / -path / -center-line / -limit / -in-control-fill / -dot / -tooltip / -tooltip-label / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-y / -tooltip-cl / -tooltip-state / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -legend-total-points / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-count / -badge-label); root mirrors `data-series-count`, `data-visible-series-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-k-sigma`, `data-out-of-control-count`, `data-animate`; limits `data-kind="ucl"`/`"lcl"` + `data-value`; CL `data-kind="cl"` + `data-value`; dots `data-state`, `data-out-of-control`, `data-deviation`; series groups `data-series-id`, `data-series-color`, `data-series-mean`, `data-series-sigma`, `data-series-ucl`, `data-series-lcl`, `data-series-k-sigma`, `data-series-out-of-control-count`, `data-series-above-count`, `data-series-below-count`, `data-series-finite-count`. Tooltip surfaces label, x, bold y, `CL: <mean>` row, and a coloured `out of control (<state>) (<deviation> sigma)` / `in control (<deviation> sigma)` row tinted by state. Sign badge in top-left mirrors total out-of-control count with alert ('!') / ok ('✓') icon. | 11.535 | UI: chart-line-burndown primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.553 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-burndown.tsx` ships `<ChartLineBurndown>` -- pure-SVG **burndown chart**, the canonical agile / project-management view of remaining work over time. Two specific lines share an x axis: the **ideal** trajectory (a dashed straight line from the initial scope at the start date down to zero at the deadline, computed entirely from `startTotal` + `startX` + `endX`) and the **actual** trajectory (the observed remaining work, supplied as `data`). The region between them is shaded: green where the team is **ahead** of schedule (actual below ideal -- less remaining than planned) and red where the team is **behind** (actual above ideal -- more remaining than planned). A status badge in the top-left calls out the current status (`ahead` / `behind` / `on-track`) with the signed delta from ideal. The canonical "sprint health" / "release tracking" visualisation used by every agile team -- answering "are we tracking the plan?" at a glance. Distinct from `<ChartLineTarget>` (11.518; vs a FLAT user-supplied target with over/under shading -- burndown's reference is a DIAGONAL ideal trajectory computed from inputs), `<ChartLineForecast>` (11.519; historical solid + forecast dashed split on time axis -- burndown overlays ideal AND actual on the SAME time axis), `<ChartLineCumulative>` (11.520; running cumulative SUM rising over time -- burndown is the opposite, remaining work DECREASING ideally), `<ChartLineComparison>` (11.511; two arbitrary curves with difference shading -- burndown's "ideal" is COMPUTED from inputs and shading is colour-coded by which side wins), `<ChartLinePeriodCompare>` (11.531; two periods of the SAME metric -- burndown overlays REMAINING WORK vs an IDEAL plan, not two recurrences), and `<ChartLineBaseline>` (11.510; single flat reference -- burndown's reference is a DIAGONAL rule). API: `data: readonly ChartLineBurndownPoint[]` (`{x, y}` where x is the time index and y is the remaining work); `startTotal` (defaults to first sample y); `startX` / `endX` (defaults to first / last sample x); `flatEpsilon` (default 0) -- equality band for on-track classification; `aheadColor` / `behindColor` / `onTrackColor` / `actualColor` / `idealColor`; `idealDashArray` (default '6 4'); toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showLegend`/`showTooltip`/`showStatusBadge`/`showAheadFill`/`showBehindFill`/`showIdeal`/`animate`; standard sizing / format / a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX` / `formatDelta`; `actualLabel` (default 'Actual') / `idealLabel` (default 'Ideal'); `onPointClick({point})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_BURNDOWN_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/IDEAL_STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/AHEAD_OPACITY/BEHIND_OPACITY/IDEAL_DASH/AHEAD_COLOR/BEHIND_COLOR/ON_TRACK_COLOR/ACTUAL_COLOR/IDEAL_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getBurndownFinitePoints` (finite-only filter); `computeBurndownIdealY(x, startX, endX, startTotal)` -- canonical ideal-trajectory helper; returns `startTotal` for `x <= startX`, `0` for `x >= endX`, linear interpolation between for any x in the open interval; non-finite inputs -> NaN; collapse (`startX === endX`) holds the curve as a step at the seam; `classifyBurndownStatus(delta, flatEpsilon?)` -- `'ahead' | 'behind' | 'on-track'`; positive delta (actual > ideal) -> behind; negative -> ahead; |delta| <= epsilon -> on-track; non-finite -> on-track; `computeBurndownLayout({data, ...})` -- returns `{ok, ..., actualPoints, actualPath, idealStartPx/Py/EndPx/Py, idealPath, aheadFillPath, behindFillPath, startTotal, startX, endX, currentRemaining, currentDelta, currentStatus, aheadCount, behindCount, onTrackCount, finiteCount, totalCount, daysRemaining}`; y range always expanded to include both 0 and startTotal so ideal endpoints sit at axis extremes; per-point carries `{index, x, y, px, py, idealY, idealPy, delta, status}`; sorts ascending by x; drops non-finite from data; currentStatus derived from the last sample; daysRemaining = max(0, endX - lastX); degenerate canvas / empty data -> ok=false; shading construction: list of contiguous run segments where the per-point status matches the target side; each run yields one closed polygon walking actual forward then ideal backward closed with Z; `describeBurndownChart(data, options?)` -- ARIA summary (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-burndown-aria-desc"` span; ideal line + actual line + every dot `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; dot aria-label surfaces the day, remaining, ideal, status, and absolute delta. data-section on every node (chart-line-burndown / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -x-label / -y-label / -actual / -ideal / -fill / -dot / -tooltip / -tooltip-label / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-actual / -tooltip-ideal / -tooltip-status / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-total-points / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-status / -badge-delta); root mirrors `data-total-points`, `data-start-total`, `data-current-remaining`, `data-current-delta`, `data-status`, `data-ahead-count`, `data-behind-count`, `data-on-track-count`, `data-days-remaining`, `data-animate`; ideal line `data-kind="ideal"`; actual line `data-kind="actual"`; shading paths `data-status` ('ahead' / 'behind'); dots `data-ideal-y`, `data-delta`, `data-status`. Tooltip surfaces actual label in actual-color, x, bold `remaining: <y>` row, `ideal: <idealY>` row, and a coloured `<status> (+/-delta)` row tinted by status. Status badge in the top-left calls out current status with icon ▼/▲/◆ and signed delta. Legend has actual (solid swatch) + ideal (dashed swatch) + status item + total-points count. 57 vitest cases (defaults, helpers incl. computeBurndownIdealY endpoint + midpoint + clamp before/after + collapse + non-finite, classifyBurndownStatus positive/negative/zero/epsilon/non-finite, computeBurndownLayout empty / degenerate / derived inputs / explicit overrides / per-point ideal+delta+status / ahead vs behind counts / currentStatus from last sample / ideal endpoint projection / ahead vs behind shading paths / y range includes 0 + startTotal / bounds overrides / drops non-finite / daysRemaining > 0 / sorts ascending, describeBurndownChart No data + scope + remaining + status, component render incl. empty / ideal kind=ideal / actual kind=actual / ideal dashed / hide ideal via prop / ahead fill / behind fill / hide ahead+behind via prop / dots with status + delta + ideal-y / hide dots / status badge + omit / ARIA / root data-status + counts / tooltip ideal + status rows + hide on leave + omit via prop / onPointClick payload / legend actual + ideal items + omit / animate flag + class / ref / displayName) all pass under vitest 4.1.5.
