| 11.546 | UI: chart-line-changepoint primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.564 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-changepoint.tsx` ships `<ChartLineChangepoint>` -- pure-SVG line chart that DETECTS structural change points via a **variance-shift heuristic** and renders dashed vertical markers at each detected boundary. Between markers, the chart shades each "regime" with a different segment color (cycled from a 6-color palette) so the regime boundaries are visible at a glance. An optional segment-mean overlay draws the per-segment mean as a dashed horizontal rule within the segment. Algorithm: for each candidate index `i` in `[minSegment, N-minSegment]` compute `score = |log(var(y[i..N]) / var(y[0..i]))|` with variance floored at 1e-9 to avoid log(0); threshold filter keeps `score >= threshold`; non-maximum suppression walks surviving candidates in descending score order and keeps one only if no already-kept detection is within `suppressionWindow` indices. Each detected index marks the FIRST sample of the new segment (left-edge convention). The canonical "find me the regime shifts in this time series" primitive used in monitoring, financial regime detection, EEG state changes, and any context where the question is "where did the behavior change?". Distinct from `<ChartLineAnomaly>` (11.524; per-point z-score outlier flag -- changepoint detects PERSISTENT regime shifts not isolated outliers), `<ChartLineControl>` (11.534; global SPC mean+/-k*sigma reference rules -- changepoint splits series into multiple regimes each with own mean/variance), `<ChartLineEvent>` (11.515; external event markers supplied by caller -- changepoint markers are DETECTED from data), `<ChartLineStreak>` (11.527; consecutive same-direction RUNS -- changepoint marks ANY regime shift not direction-specific), and `<ChartLineDecompose>` (11.543; periodic trend + seasonal split -- changepoint marks ABRUPT structural breaks with no periodicity assumption). API: `series: readonly ChartLineChangepointSeries[]` (`{id, label, data, color?, minSegment?, threshold?, suppressionWindow?}`; per-series detector overrides always beat chart-level); `minSegment` (default 5; clamped to >= 2); `threshold` (default 0.5; allows 0; non-finite/negative -> default); `suppressionWindow` (default 3; clamped to >= 1); `markerColor` (default red); `segmentColors` (6-color palette); `markerDashArray` (default '4 3'); `segmentOpacity` (default 0.06); toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showLegend`/`showTooltip`/`showCountBadge`/`showMarkers`/`showSegmentShading`/`showSegmentMeans`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility; standard sizing / format / a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX` / `formatScore`; `onPointClick({series, point})` / `onMarkerClick({series, detection})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_CHANGEPOINT_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/STROKE_WIDTH/MARKER_STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/MIN_SEGMENT/THRESHOLD/SUPPRESSION_WINDOW/VARIANCE_FLOOR/MARKER_DASH/SEGMENT_OPACITY/PALETTE/MARKER_COLOR/SEGMENT_COLORS/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineChangepointDefaultColor` (10-color cycle); `getLineChangepointSegmentColor` (cycles segment palette); `getLineChangepointFinitePoints`; `normaliseLineChangepointMinSegment` (clamps to >= 2; floors fractional; non-finite -> 5); `normaliseLineChangepointThreshold` (non-finite / negative -> 0.5; zero allowed; identity for valid); `normaliseLineChangepointSuppressionWindow` (clamps to >= 1; non-finite -> 3); `computeLineChangepointScores(values, {minSegment?})` -- per-index variance-shift score; returns null at indices where window not yet filled; non-array -> []; input shorter than `2*minSegment` -> all null; variance floored at 1e-9 to avoid log(0); `detectLineChangepoints(points, {minSegment?, threshold?, suppressionWindow?})` -- canonical detector pipeline; sorts ascending; drops non-finite; computes scores; applies threshold; applies non-max suppression; builds segments from cut points; returns `{ok, samples, scores, detections, segments, minSegment, threshold, suppressionWindow}`; each detection carries `{index, x, leftMean, rightMean, leftVariance, rightVariance, score, meanShift}`; each sample carries `{index, x, y, segmentIndex}`; detections sorted ascending by index; `computeLineChangepointLayout({series, ...})` -- projects per-series path + detection markers + segment shading rects + segment-mean overlay positions; returns `{ok, width, height, panel, xTicks, yTicks, xMin, xMax, yMin, yMax, innerWidth, innerHeight, series, totalPoints, totalDetections, visibleSeriesCount}`; per-series carries `{id, label, color, minSegment, threshold, suppressionWindow, points, path, detections, segments, finiteCount, totalCount}`; each layout point carries `{index, x, y, px, py, segmentIndex, segmentColor}`; each layout segment carries projected `{...segment, px0, px1, color, meanPy}`; each layout detection carries projected `{...detection, px, topPy, bottomPy}`; degenerate canvas / empty / all-hidden -> ok=false; `describeLineChangepointChart(series, options?)` -- ARIA summary (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-changepoint-aria-desc"` span; series path + markers + dots `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; marker aria-label includes index + score + mean shift; dot aria-label includes segment number. data-section on every node (chart-line-changepoint / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -x-label / -y-label / -series / -series-group / -path / -segment / -segment-mean / -marker / -dot / -tooltip / -tooltip-label / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-y / -tooltip-score / -tooltip-mean-shift / -tooltip-variance / -tooltip-segment / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -legend-total-points / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-count / -badge-label / -badge-threshold); root mirrors `data-series-count`, `data-visible-series-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-total-detections`, `data-min-segment`, `data-threshold`, `data-suppression-window`, `data-animate`; path `data-kind="signal"`; markers `data-index`, `data-score`, `data-left-mean`, `data-right-mean`, `data-left-variance`, `data-right-variance`, `data-mean-shift`; segments `data-segment-index`, `data-start-index`, `data-end-index`, `data-mean`, `data-variance`, `data-std`, `data-count`; dots `data-segment-index`. Tooltip on markers shows "<label> changepoint", x, bold score, mean shift (left -> right values), variance shift. Tooltip on dots shows label, x, bold y, and segment number with `(mean <m>, sigma <s>)`. Detection badge in top-left with `⌇` icon + count + threshold. Legend shows per-series `(cp count; segment count)`. 74 vitest cases (defaults, helpers, computeLineChangepointScores incl. non-array -> [] / all-null when too short / null edges + finite interior / **high variance-shift produces high score near boundary** / flat input produces zero scores, detectLineChangepoints empty -> ok=false / **detects variance shift around true boundary index 30 in fixture** / no detections on flat at default threshold / higher threshold suppresses all / lower threshold allows more / sorted ascending by index / NMS spacing > suppressionWindow / segments cover ALL samples / per-sample segmentIndex valid / **right/left variance ratio > 10x for variance-shift fixture** / sort ascending / drops non-finite, computeLineChangepointLayout empty / degenerate / path + markers + segments where segments = detections + 1 / markers within panel bounds / hidden / bounds overrides / per-series threshold override / totalDetections sums / per-point segmentColor matches palette / segments px0 <= px1, describeLineChangepointChart No data + summary, component render incl. empty / signal path kind=signal / detection markers / markers dashed vertical / hide markers via prop / segment shading + hide / segment mean lines via showSegmentMeans=true / dots via showDots + hide by default / detection badge with count + threshold + hide / ARIA / root data-* incl. min-segment + threshold + suppression-window / tooltip marker (score + mean shift + variance) + tooltip point (segment info) + hide on leave + omit via prop / onMarkerClick payload + onPointClick payload / legend cp + seg counts + toggle + omit / animate flag + class / ref / displayName) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for the new files. Exported via components/ui barrel. Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/c4-mgr-auto. Worker auto-w528. Bump 1.11.564. |
| 11.547 | UI: chart-line-savgol primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.565 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-savgol.tsx` ships `<ChartLineSavgol>` -- pure-SVG line chart with a **Savitzky-Golay** polynomial smoothing overlay. For each interior position, fits a polynomial of degree p (default 2) to a sliding window of windowLength samples (default 7, must be odd) by least squares and uses the polynomial's value at the window center as the smoothed estimate. The first and last `m = (W-1)/2` samples have no smoothed value because the window cannot be centered there. The canonical scipy `savgol_filter` reference smoother used in spectroscopy, sensor signal processing, and any context where peak shapes matter -- SG fits a polynomial LOCALLY so it preserves features that match the polynomial's complexity (order 0 == SMA; order 1 preserves linear trends; order 2 preserves quadratic features like peaks and valleys; order 3 additionally preserves cubic asymmetries). Distinct from `<ChartLineMovingAvg>` (11.513; SMA == SG with polyOrder=0; doesn't preserve peaks), `<ChartLineEwma>` (11.539; exponential decay weights -- recursive single-parameter smoother), `<ChartLineKalman>` (11.538; recursive Bayesian filter with Q/R noise model), `<ChartLineBollinger>` (11.537; rolling SMA + envelope -- no polynomial fit), `<ChartLineFftWindow>` (11.544; frequency-domain filtering -- SG is time-domain polynomial fit), and `<ChartLineDecompose>` (11.543; seasonal split with centered moving average -- different decomposition goal). Algorithm: build symmetric `(p+1) x (p+1)` matrix `M[j][k] = sum_{i=-m..m} i^(j+k)`; solve `M alpha = e_0` via Gauss-Jordan elimination on the augmented matrix; smoothing kernel `c_i = sum_j alpha_j * (i - m)^j` for i=0..N-1; apply via `smoothed[i] = sum_k coeffs[k] * y[i - m + k]` for i in `[m, N - m)`. The kernel is symmetric about the center and its coefficients sum to 1 (preserves DC). Verified canonical kernels: W=5/p=2 -> `[-3, 12, 17, 12, -3] / 35`; W=7/p=2 -> `[-2, 3, 6, 7, 6, 3, -2] / 21`. Polynomial-preservation invariant verified by test: a quadratic input is reproduced EXACTLY by order >= 2 smoothing at every interior position. API: `series: readonly ChartLineSavgolSeries[]` (`{id, label, data, color?, windowLength?, polyOrder?}`; per-series overrides always beat chart-level); `windowLength` (default 7; clamped to odd >= 3; even input rounded up to next odd; floored from fractional); `polyOrder` (default 2; clamped to >= 0; floored from fractional; effective order silently capped at `windowLength - 1`); `rawColor`; `residualPosColor` / `residualNegColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showLegend`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showRaw`/`showResidualSticks`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility; standard sizing / format / a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX` / `formatCoefficient`; `onPointClick({series, point})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_SAVGOL_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/RAW_STROKE_WIDTH/SMOOTH_STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/WINDOW/POLY_ORDER/RAW_OPACITY/RESIDUAL_OPACITY/PALETTE/RAW_COLOR/RESIDUAL_POS_COLOR/RESIDUAL_NEG_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineSavgolDefaultColor` (10-color cycle); `getLineSavgolFinitePoints`; `normaliseLineSavgolWindow` (clamps to odd >= 3; even up to next odd; floors fractional; non-finite -> 7); `normaliseLineSavgolPolyOrder` (clamps to >= 0; floors fractional; non-finite -> 2); `computeSavgolCoefficients(windowLength, polyOrder)` -- returns the smoothing kernel array; null when invalid (window even / non-positive / polyOrder >= window / non-finite); `classifyLineSavgolResidualSign` ('positive' | 'negative' | 'zero'); `applyLineSavgol(values, windowLength, polyOrder)` -- smooths a numeric array; returns same-length array with leading/trailing m nulls; non-finite within window produces null at that position; polyOrder silently capped at W-1; `runLineSavgol(points, options?)` -- canonical pipeline; sorts ascending; drops non-finite; returns `{samples, coefficients, windowLength, polyOrder}` where each sample carries `{index, x, raw, smoothed, residual, residualSign}`; `computeLineSavgolLayout({series, ...})` -- projects raw + smoothed paths + residual segments; per-series carries `{id, label, color, windowLength, polyOrder, coefficients, points, rawPath, smoothedPath, residualSegments, finiteCount, totalCount, smoothedValidCount, positiveResidualCount, negativeResidualCount, zeroResidualCount, rmseResidual, maxAbsResidual, finalSmoothed}`; per-point carries `{...sample, px, rawPy, smoothedPy}` with smoothedPy null at edges; degenerate canvas / empty / all-hidden -> ok=false; `describeLineSavgolChart(series, options?)` -- ARIA summary (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-savgol-aria-desc"` span; raw path + smoothed path + every dot `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; smoothed aria-label includes window + order; dot aria-label includes raw + smoothed values. data-section on every node (chart-line-savgol / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -x-label / -y-label / -series / -series-group / -raw-path / -smoothed-path / -residual-stick / -dot / -tooltip / -tooltip-label / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-raw / -tooltip-smoothed / -tooltip-residual / -tooltip-config / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -legend-total-points / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-window / -badge-order / -badge-sum); root mirrors `data-series-count`, `data-visible-series-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-window-length`, `data-poly-order`, `data-dominant-coefficient-sum`, `data-animate`; raw path `data-kind="raw"`; smoothed path `data-kind="smoothed"`; dots `data-raw`, `data-smoothed`, `data-residual`, `data-residual-sign`; residual sticks `data-sign`; series groups `data-series-window-length`, `data-series-poly-order`, `data-series-smoothed-valid-count`, `data-series-rmse`, `data-series-max-abs-residual`, `data-series-positive-residual-count`, `data-series-negative-residual-count`, `data-series-zero-residual-count`, `data-series-finite-count`, `data-series-final-smoothed`. Tooltip on dots shows the series label, x, raw value, bold smoothed value (or n/a at the edges), residual (with +/- prefix), and SG config `W=<w>, p=<p>`. Config badge in top-left calls out `SG W=<w> p=<p> sum=<coefficient sum>` with the kernel sum verified at 1.0 by test. Legend shows per-series `(W=<w>; p=<p>; rmse <value>)`. 76 vitest cases (defaults + helpers, computeSavgolCoefficients incl. invalid configs -> null + polyOrder 0 gives uniform 1/N kernel + **canonical SG W=5 p=2 coefficients verified at [-3,12,17,12,-3]/35** + **canonical SG W=7 p=2 coefficients verified at [-2,3,6,7,6,3,-2]/21** + kernel symmetric around center + coefficient sum = 1 verified, applyLineSavgol incl. non-array -> [] / all-null when input < window / **first and last m entries null at edges verified** / **constant input passes through unchanged** / **linear input preserved exactly when order >= 1** / **quadratic input preserved exactly when order >= 2** (SG-specific guarantee SMA cannot give) / non-finite within window -> null / polyOrder silently capped at W-1, runLineSavgol incl. empty samples for null / per-sample smoothed + residual / **smoothing reduces RMSE below raw noise amplitude on noisy parabola** / sort ascending / drops non-finite, computeLineSavgolLayout incl. ok=false for empty / degenerate / per-series raw + smoothed paths / window + poly order + coefficients / RMSE + counts / drops hidden / bounds overrides / per-series window + polyOrder override / per-point smoothedPy null at edges / totalPoints sums + visibleSeriesCount, describeLineSavgolChart No data + mentions window + order, component render incl. empty / raw path kind=raw / smoothed path kind=smoothed / hide raw via prop / residual sticks via showResidualSticks=true + omit by default / dots via showDots + hide by default / config badge with window + order + **coefficient sum = 1 verified** + hide via prop / ARIA / root data-* including coefficient-sum / group data-* / tooltip with raw + smoothed + residual + config rows + hide on leave + omit via prop / onPointClick payload / legend with W=<w> + p=<p> + rmse + toggle + omit / animate flag + class / ref / displayName) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.548 | UI: chart-line-hampel primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.566 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-hampel.tsx` ships `<ChartLineHampel>` -- pure-SVG line chart with a **Hampel** robust outlier filter (median + MAD) and an **original-vs-filtered** overlay. For each index, the filter builds a centered window of length W (default 7, odd), computes the window median m and `MAD = median(|y - m|)`, scales MAD by c (default 1.4826 -- canonical normality factor that makes c*MAD an unbiased sigma estimator under Gaussian noise), and flags index i as an outlier when `|y[i] - m| > n * c * MAD` (default n=3); outliers are replaced with the window median. Edge windows are clamped to in-bounds samples (no leading/trailing nulls; the filter operates on shorter windows at the edges). The key property: median + MAD are **robust statistics** with a 50% breakdown point, so the Hampel filter detects outliers EVEN WHEN OTHER OUTLIERS ARE PRESENT in the same window -- unlike methods using mean + stddev where outliers inflate stddev and hide each other. The canonical robust outlier rejector used in sensor signal processing, financial cleanup, and any context where the question is "drop the spikes so the underlying signal is visible". Distinct from `<ChartLineAnomaly>` (11.524; z-score based on rolling mean+stddev -- non-robust, outliers inflate stddev and hide each other), `<ChartLineControl>` (11.534; global SPC mean+/-k*sigma -- non-robust + non-local), `<ChartLineChangepoint>` (11.546; regime shift detector finding persistent shifts -- Hampel finds isolated outliers), `<ChartLineSavgol>` (11.547; polynomial smoothing with no outlier flag -- Hampel replaces flagged points with median; SG smooths through everything), `<ChartLineMovingAvg>` (11.513; SMA -- non-robust), `<ChartLineKalman>` (11.538; Bayesian filter -- statistical not robust), and `<ChartLineBollinger>` (11.537; rolling SMA + envelope -- non-robust). API: `series: readonly ChartLineHampelSeries[]` (`{id, label, data, color?, windowLength?, nSigmas?, madScale?}`; per-series overrides always beat chart-level); `windowLength` (default 7; clamped to odd >= 3; even inputs rounded up); `nSigmas` (default 3; clamped to >= 0 with 0 allowed); `madScale` (default 1.4826; clamped to > 0); `outlierColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showLegend`/`showTooltip`/`showOutlierBadge`/`showRaw`/`showOutlierMarkers`/`showReplacementSticks`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility; `formatValue` / `formatX` / `formatScore`; `onPointClick({series, point})` / `onOutlierClick({series, point})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_HAMPEL_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/RAW_STROKE_WIDTH/FILTERED_STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/WINDOW/N_SIGMAS/MAD_SCALE/RAW_OPACITY/OUTLIER_OPACITY/PALETTE/RAW_COLOR/OUTLIER_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineHampelDefaultColor` (10-color cycle); `getLineHampelFinitePoints`; `normaliseLineHampelWindow` (clamps to odd >= 3); `normaliseLineHampelNSigmas` (clamps to >= 0); `normaliseLineHampelMadScale` (clamps to > 0; non-finite / non-positive -> 1.4826); `computeHampelMedian(values)` -- robust median; drops non-finite; empty / all non-finite -> NaN; verified at 50% breakdown point; `computeHampelMad(values, centerMedian?)` -- canonical MAD; accepts pre-computed center median; verified MAD([1..5]) = 1 and MAD([1..5], center=1) = 2; robust to outliers verified MAD([1,2,3,4,1e9]) = 1; `applyLineHampel(values, options?)` -- canonical filter pipeline; returns `{filtered, outlierIndices, medians, mads, deviations, scores, windowLength, nSigmas, madScale}`; edge windows clamped to in-bounds samples; non-finite slots produce NaN in filtered output; `runLineHampel(points, options?)` -- canonical pipeline; sorts ascending; drops non-finite; per-sample `{index, x, raw, filtered, windowMedian, windowMad, deviation, score, isOutlier}`; `computeLineHampelLayout({series, ...})` -- projects raw + filtered paths + outlier markers + replacement sticks; per-series carries `{id, label, color, outlierColor, windowLength, nSigmas, madScale, points, rawPath, filteredPath, outliers, finiteCount, totalCount, outlierCount, maxScore, rmseReplacement}`; per-point carries projected `{...sample, px, rawPy, filteredPy, windowMedianPy}`; tracks Infinity in maxScore (when MAD=0 outliers get score=Infinity, the strongest possible signal); degenerate canvas / empty / all-hidden -> ok=false; `describeLineHampelChart(series, options?)` -- ARIA summary (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-hampel-aria-desc"` span; raw path + filtered path + dots + outlier markers all `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; filtered aria-label includes window + sigma; outlier marker aria-label includes index + raw + median + score. data-section on every node (chart-line-hampel / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -x-label / -y-label / -series / -series-group / -raw-path / -filtered-path / -replacement-stick / -dot / -outlier-marker / -outlier-dot / -outlier-cross / -tooltip / -tooltip-label / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-raw / -tooltip-filtered / -tooltip-median / -tooltip-mad / -tooltip-score / -tooltip-config / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -legend-total-points / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-count / -badge-label / -badge-config); root mirrors `data-series-count`, `data-visible-series-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-total-outliers`, `data-window-length`, `data-n-sigmas`, `data-mad-scale`, `data-animate`; raw path `data-kind="raw"`; filtered path `data-kind="filtered"`; dots `data-raw`, `data-filtered`, `data-window-median`, `data-window-mad`, `data-deviation`, `data-score`, `data-outlier`; outlier markers carry the same per-point breakdown plus an X-cross built from two diagonal lines; series groups expose `data-series-window-length`, `data-series-n-sigmas`, `data-series-mad-scale`, `data-series-finite-count`, `data-series-outlier-count`, `data-series-max-score`, `data-series-rmse-replacement`. Tooltip on dots shows label (with `OUTLIER` suffix when flagged), x, raw, bold filtered, window median, window MAD, score in sigma units, and the config row `W=<w>, n=<n>, c=<c>`. Outlier markers are X-shaped (circle + two diagonal cross lines) at flagged points on the RAW y position. Outlier badge in top-left with `X <count> outliers (W=<w>; <n> sigma)`. Legend shows per-series `(<count> outliers; max <score> sigma)`. 84 vitest cases (defaults + helpers, computeHampelMedian incl. odd-length middle + even-length average + **robust to outliers verified median([1,2,3,4,1e9]) = 3** + drops non-finite + all-non-finite -> NaN, computeHampelMad incl. zero MAD for constant + **canonical MAD([1..5]) = 1 verified** + accepts pre-computed center (MAD with center=1 = 2) + **robust to outliers verified MAD([1,2,3,4,1e9]) = 1**, applyLineHampel incl. null -> empty / **flags both spikes in fixture and replaces with median** / no outliers on flat constant / **robust to adjacent outliers in same window (50% breakdown verified)** / higher nSigmas suppresses subtle / records all metadata arrays / non-finite slot preserves NaN / edge windows clamped to in-bounds / reflects W + n + c, runLineHampel incl. empty samples / per-sample carries filtered + windowMedian + windowMad + score + isOutlier / sort ascending / drops non-finite / counts outliers, computeLineHampelLayout incl. ok=false for empty / degenerate / per-series raw + filtered paths / outlier count + max score / outliers array extracted / hidden / bounds overrides / per-series windowLength + nSigmas override / totalOutliers sums / **per-point rawPy and filteredPy diverge at outliers verified**, describeLineHampelChart No data + summary mentions window + sigma + outlier count, component render incl. empty / raw path kind=raw / filtered path kind=filtered / hide raw / **outlier X-cross markers at 2 fixture spikes verified** / hide outlier markers / replacement sticks via showReplacementSticks=true + omit by default / dots via showDots with `data-outlier=true` on flagged and false otherwise / outlier badge with count + config + hide / ARIA / root data-* including windowLength + nSigmas + madScale (1.4826 verified) / group data-* / tooltip with raw + filtered + median + MAD + score + config rows + `OUTLIER` suffix in label for flagged + hide on leave + omit via prop / onPointClick payload + onOutlierClick payload / legend with outlier count + max score sigma + toggle + omit / animate flag + class / ref / displayName) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.549 | UI: chart-line-loess primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.567 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-loess.tsx` ships `<ChartLineLoess>` -- pure-SVG line chart with a **LOESS** (locally weighted scatterplot smoothing) overlay. For each query point x_i the smoother finds k = ceil(bandwidth * N) nearest neighbours by absolute distance (with k >= degree+1 so the fit has enough degrees of freedom), determines max neighbour distance d_max, computes tricube weights `w_j = (1 - (d_j/d_max)^3)^3` (returns 0 at d >= dMax), fits a weighted polynomial of degree p (0, 1, or 2) via weighted least squares (Gauss-Jordan on the normal equations in centred coordinates), and evaluates at x_i. The canonical R `loess` / scipy LOESS reference smoother used in exploratory data analysis where the x-y relationship is unknown and should be discovered from the data ("let the data speak"). Distinct from `<ChartLineSavgol>` (11.547; polynomial fit on a FIXED window with EQUAL weights -- LOESS uses VARIABLE k-NN neighbourhood and TRICUBE WEIGHTS that favour closer points), `<ChartLineMovingAvg>` (11.513; SMA == uniform-weight degree-0 fit on a fixed window -- LOESS handles non-uniform spacing because the neighbourhood is defined by COUNT not absolute distance), `<ChartLineEwma>` (11.539; exponential decay, recursive single-parameter smoother), `<ChartLineKalman>` (11.538; Bayesian filter with Q/R noise model), `<ChartLineHampel>` (11.548; robust outlier filter that REPLACES flagged points with median -- LOESS smooths through all points), `<ChartLineBollinger>` (11.537; rolling SMA + envelope -- no envelope in LOESS), and `<ChartLineDecompose>` (11.543; seasonal split with centered MA -- different goal). API: `series: readonly ChartLineLoessSeries[]` (`{id, label, data, color?, bandwidth?, degree?}`; per-series overrides always beat chart-level); `bandwidth` (default 0.3; clamped to (0, 1]; R `loess` calls this `span`); `degree` (default 2; clamped to {0, 1, 2}; floored from fractional); `rawColor`; `residualPosColor` / `residualNegColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showLegend`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showRaw`/`showResidualSticks`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility; standard sizing / format / a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX` / `formatBandwidth`; `onPointClick({series, point})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_LOESS_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/RAW_STROKE_WIDTH/SMOOTH_STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/BANDWIDTH/DEGREE/RAW_OPACITY/RESIDUAL_OPACITY/PALETTE/RAW_COLOR/RESIDUAL_POS_COLOR/RESIDUAL_NEG_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineLoessDefaultColor` (10-color cycle); `getLineLoessFinitePoints`; `normaliseLineLoessBandwidth` (clamps to (0, 1]; non-finite -> 0.3); `normaliseLineLoessDegree` (clamps to {0, 1, 2}; floors fractional; non-finite -> 2); `computeLineLoessTricubeWeight(distance, dMax)` -- canonical tricube weight `(1 - (d/dMax)^3)^3`; returns 0 when `d >= dMax`, when `dMax <= 0`, when `d < 0`, or when either argument is non-finite; `fitLineLoessWeightedPolynomialAtCenter({xs, ys, weights, degree, centerX})` -- canonical weighted polynomial fit in centred coordinates so output is `beta_0`; returns null for empty inputs or zero total weight; `classifyLineLoessResidualSign` ('positive' | 'negative' | 'zero'); `runLineLoess(points, options?)` -- canonical pipeline; sorts ascending; drops non-finite; per-sample `{index, x, raw, smoothed, residual, residualSign, neighborhoodSize, neighborhoodWidth}`; neighbour count clamped to at least `degree + 1`; `computeLineLoessLayout({series, ...})` -- projects raw + smoothed paths + residual segments; per-series carries `{id, label, color, bandwidth, degree, neighborhoodCount, points, rawPath, smoothedPath, residualSegments, finiteCount, totalCount, smoothedValidCount, positiveResidualCount, negativeResidualCount, zeroResidualCount, rmseResidual, maxAbsResidual, finalSmoothed}`; per-point carries projected `{...sample, px, rawPy, smoothedPy}`; degenerate canvas / empty / all-hidden -> ok=false; `describeLineLoessChart(series, options?)` -- ARIA summary (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-loess-aria-desc"` span; raw path + smoothed path + every dot `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; smoothed aria-label includes bandwidth + degree; dot aria-label includes raw + smoothed values. data-section on every node (chart-line-loess / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -x-label / -y-label / -series / -series-group / -raw-path / -smoothed-path / -residual-stick / -dot / -tooltip / -tooltip-label / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-raw / -tooltip-smoothed / -tooltip-residual / -tooltip-config / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -legend-total-points / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-bandwidth / -badge-degree / -badge-neighbors); root mirrors `data-series-count`, `data-visible-series-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-bandwidth`, `data-degree`, `data-dominant-neighborhood-count`, `data-animate`; raw path `data-kind="raw"`; smoothed path `data-kind="smoothed"`; dots `data-raw`, `data-smoothed`, `data-residual`, `data-residual-sign`, `data-neighborhood-width`; residual sticks `data-sign`; series groups `data-series-bandwidth`, `data-series-degree`, `data-series-neighborhood-count`, `data-series-smoothed-valid-count`, `data-series-rmse`, `data-series-max-abs-residual`, `data-series-positive-residual-count`, `data-series-negative-residual-count`, `data-series-zero-residual-count`, `data-series-finite-count`, `data-series-final-smoothed`. Tooltip on dots shows the series label, x, raw value, bold smoothed value (or n/a at the edges), residual (with +/- prefix), and the LOESS config `α=<bandwidth>, d=<degree>, k=<neighbour count>`. Config badge in top-left calls out `LO α=<bandwidth> d=<degree> k=<neighbours>`. Legend shows per-series `(α=<bandwidth>; d=<degree>; rmse <value>)`. 76 vitest cases (defaults + helpers, computeLineLoessTricubeWeight incl. d=0 -> 1 (full weight at center) + d>=dMax -> 0 + **canonical (7/8)^3 = 0.6699... verified at d/dMax = 0.5** + dMax <= 0 -> 0 + negative distance -> 0 + non-finite -> 0 + monotonically decreasing, fitLineLoessWeightedPolynomialAtCenter incl. empty / all-zero-weights -> null + **degree-1 linear fit verified exact (y = 2x+1 at center x=3 -> 7)** + **degree-2 quadratic fit verified exact (y = (x-3)^2 -> 0)** + degree-0 weighted-mean verified (weighted([1,0,1]) of [1,5,10] = 5.5) + weight influence (spike-weighted result > evenly-weighted), runLineLoess incl. empty samples / per-sample + **smoothing reduces noise RMSE < raw noise amplitude on noisy parabola** + **linear input reproduced exactly with degree >= 1** + **quadratic input reproduced exactly with degree 2** + **constant input passes through unchanged at all degrees (0, 1, 2)** + **non-uniformly spaced data handled via k-NN neighbourhood** + records bandwidth + degree + neighbour count + sort ascending + drops non-finite + neighbour count clamped to >= degree+1, computeLineLoessLayout empty / degenerate / per-series raw + smoothed paths / bandwidth + degree + neighbour count / RMSE + counts / hidden / bounds + overrides + totalPoints, describeLineLoessChart No data + bandwidth + degree + neighbours, component render incl. empty / raw path kind=raw / smoothed path kind=smoothed / hide raw via prop / residual sticks via showResidualSticks=true + omit by default / dots via showDots + hide by default / config badge with bandwidth + degree + neighbours + hide via prop / ARIA / root data-* / group data-* / tooltip with raw + smoothed + residual + config rows + hide on leave + omit via prop / onPointClick payload / legend with α + d + rmse + toggle + omit / animate flag + class / ref / displayName) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. TypeScript clean for the new files. Exported via components/ui barrel. Reference /root/c4/arps-design-system-v1/. Branch c4/c4-mgr-auto. Worker auto-w531. Bump 1.11.567. |
| 11.550 | UI: chart-line-spline-bspline primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.568 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-spline-bspline.tsx` ships `<ChartLineSplineBspline>` -- pure-SVG line chart with a **cubic B-spline interpolation overlay**. For each cubic B-spline segment with 4 consecutive control points `(P_0, P_1, P_2, P_3)` and parameter `t in [0, 1]`, the canonical uniform basis is `B(t) = (1/6) * [ (1-t)^3 * P_0 + (3t^3 - 6t^2 + 4) * P_1 + (-3t^3 + 3t^2 + 3t + 1) * P_2 + t^3 * P_3 ]`. Each segment converts to a cubic Bezier with control points `b_0 = (P_0 + 4*P_1 + P_2)/6`, `b_1 = (2*P_1 + P_2)/3`, `b_2 = (P_1 + 2*P_2)/3`, `b_3 = (P_1 + 4*P_2 + P_3)/6`, emitted as one SVG `C` command per segment. When `clamp = true` (default) the first and last raw points are duplicated twice so the curve passes through them exactly (b_0 of the first padded segment = (P_0 + 4*P_0 + P_0)/6 = P_0); when `clamp = false` the curve starts/ends at blended interior points and has the linear-precision property (linear input is reproduced exactly in the interior). Distinct from `<ChartLineSmooth>` Catmull-Rom and monotone-cubic (**INTERPOLATING** splines that pass through EVERY control point -- B-spline is **APPROXIMATING** and does not pass through interior controls; each control point has only LOCAL influence over 4 segments and is FILTERED by the basis), `<ChartLineSavgol>` (11.547; polynomial fit on a FIXED WINDOW with EQUAL weights -- discrete smoother, no curve in between samples), `<ChartLineLoess>` (11.549; local weighted regression per query point -- statistical smoother for noisy data), `<ChartLineMovingAvg>` (11.513; uniform-weight window), `<ChartLineEwma>` (11.539; exponential decay), `<ChartLineKalman>` (11.538; Bayesian state-space filter), `<ChartLineHampel>` (11.548; robust outlier filter that REPLACES outliers with window median), and `<ChartLineDecompose>` (11.543; seasonal split). B-spline's distinctive value: a **C^2-continuous** curve (continuous second derivative) suitable for visualising data that should appear smooth but does not need to interpolate every point exactly; the canonical de Boor / NURBS curve form used in CAD/CAM and computer graphics. API: `series: readonly ChartLineSplineBsplineSeries[]` (`{id, label, data, color?, clamp?, samplesPerSegment?}`; per-series overrides always beat chart-level); `clamp` (default `true`); `samplesPerSegment` (default 16; clamped to `[2, 256]`; floored from fractional); `rawColor`, `controlColor`, `residualPosColor` / `residualNegColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showLegend`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showRaw`/`showControlPolygon`/`showResidualSticks`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility; standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX`; `onPointClick({series, point})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_SPLINE_BSPLINE_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/RAW_STROKE_WIDTH/SMOOTH_STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/SAMPLES_PER_SEGMENT/DEGREE/RAW_OPACITY/RESIDUAL_OPACITY/PALETTE/RAW_COLOR/RESIDUAL_POS_COLOR/RESIDUAL_NEG_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR/CONTROL_COLOR`; `getLineSplineBsplineDefaultColor` (10-color palette cycle); `getLineSplineBsplineFinitePoints`; `normaliseLineSplineBsplineSamplesPerSegment` (clamps to `[2, 256]`; floors fractional; non-finite -> 16); `classifyLineSplineBsplineResidualSign` ('positive' \| 'negative' \| 'zero'); `buildLineSplineBsplineClampedControlPoints(raw)` -- duplicates first and last twice each (empty -> empty; single -> single passthrough); `evaluateCubicBSplineSegment(p0, p1, p2, p3, t)` -- canonical uniform basis evaluation; **verified canonical values: at t=0 = `(P0 + 4*P1 + P2)/6`, at t=1 = `(P1 + 4*P2 + P3)/6`, at t=0.5 = `(0.125*P0 + 2.875*P1 + 2.875*P2 + 0.125*P3)/6`**, t clamps outside `[0, 1]`, non-finite -> t=0, partition-of-unity verified via constant; `convertCubicBSplineSegmentToBezier(p0, p1, p2, p3)` -- canonical B-spline-to-Bezier conversion; **verified `b_0` and `b_3` match `evaluateCubicBSplineSegment` at t=0 and t=1**, constant control points yield all-equal Bezier controls; `buildLineSplineBsplineBezierPath(raw, options?)` -- SVG path string with `M` + cubic `C` commands (empty -> ''; single -> `M x y`; clamped vs. open segment count differs by exactly 4 from the padding contribution); `sampleLineSplineBsplineCurve(raw, options?)` -- dense parametric samples along the curve; **verified clamped curve starts EXACTLY at the first raw point and ends EXACTLY at the last raw point**, constant input -> constant curve samples, samplesPerSegment honoured; `interpolateLineSplineBsplineSmoothedAt(samples, xQuery)` -- linear interpolation between curve samples to find smoothed y at a given x (empty -> null; non-finite -> null; outside range -> null); `runLineBSpline(points, options?)` -- canonical pipeline; sorts ascending; drops non-finite; **constant input -> every smoothed = constant**, **clamped: first and last smoothed = raw exactly**, **open + linear input reproduces line in interior (linear precision property)**, returns `{samples, curveSamples, controlCount, segmentCount, samplesPerSegment, clamp}`; per-sample `{index, x, raw, smoothed, residual, residualSign}`; `computeLineSplineBsplineLayout({series, ...})` -- projects raw + smoothed paths + residual segments + curve samples; per-series carries `{id, label, color, clamp, samplesPerSegment, controlCount, segmentCount, curveSampleCount, points, curveSamples, controlPath, smoothedPath, residualSegments, finiteCount, totalCount, smoothedValidCount, positiveResidualCount, negativeResidualCount, zeroResidualCount, rmseResidual, maxAbsResidual, finalSmoothed}`; per-point projected `{...sample, px, rawPy, smoothedPy}`; degenerate canvas / empty / all-hidden -> ok=false; `describeLineSplineBsplineChart(series, options?)` -- ARIA summary (No data when missing; mentions cubic B-spline + clamped/open + segments). | 11.551 | UI: chart-line-autocorrelation primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.569 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-autocorrelation.tsx` ships `<ChartLineAutocorrelation>` -- pure-SVG line chart with an **autocorrelation function side panel**. Main panel renders the input time series; side panel shows the biased autocorrelation function (ACF) at each lag from `0` to `maxLag` (default 20; floored; clamped to [0, N-1]), with a 95% confidence band under the white-noise null hypothesis, and the lag with the highest `|ACF|` for `lag >= 1` highlighted as the *dominant* lag. Canonical biased ACF (R / numpy / statsmodels convention): `ACF(k) = sum_{i=0}^{N-1-k}(y_i - ybar)(y_{i+k} - ybar) / sum_{i=0}^{N-1}(y_i - ybar)^2`. ACF(0) = 1 always; values in [-1, 1] under the biased estimator; for a sine wave with period P, ACF(k) approximates `cos(2*pi*k/P) * (N - k) / N` with alternating-sign peaks at multiples of P/2. Confidence bound: `bound = z / sqrt(N)` where z=1.96 for 95% CI; lags with `|ACF(k)| > bound` are statistically significant (the standard "spike pokes out of the dashed band" diagnostic from R's `acf()` plot). Distinct from `<ChartLineCorrelation>` (Pearson r between TWO series at a single point -- autocorrelation correlates a series with ITSELF at many lags), `<ChartLineFft>` (11.540) and `<ChartLineFftWindow>` (11.544) (frequency-domain spectrum -- ACF is the inverse Fourier transform of the power spectrum via the Wiener-Khinchin theorem, but presented in LAG DOMAIN which is more interpretable for "what is the most likely period?"), `<ChartLineSpectrogram>` (11.545; STFT heatmap of frequency over time -- ACF collapses across time), `<ChartLineDecompose>` (11.543; seasonal split -- ACF is the diagnostic used to FIND the seasonal period that decompose then uses), `<ChartLineChangepoint>` (11.546; regime shift detector -- different question), and the smoothing primitives `<ChartLineLoess>` (11.549), `<ChartLineSavgol>` (11.547), `<ChartLineEwma>` (11.539), `<ChartLineKalman>` (11.538), `<ChartLineHampel>` (11.548) (ACF is a DIAGNOSTIC for whether smoothing is warranted -- high ACF at lag 1 means autocorrelated noise to filter). The canonical Box-Jenkins ACF plot used to identify AR order, MA order, seasonal periods, and to test model residuals. API: `series: ChartLineAutocorrelationSeries[]` (`{id, label, data, color?, maxLag?}`; per-series maxLag override always beats chart-level); `maxLag` (default 20; floored to integer; clamped to `[0, N - 1]`); `confidenceZ` (default 1.96; clamped to `>= 0`); `timePanelRatio` (default 0.55; clamped to `(0, 1)`); `positiveColor` / `negativeColor` / `insignificantColor` / `dominantColor` / `confidenceColor`; toggle flags `showAxis` / `showGrid` / `showDots` / `showLegend` / `showTooltip` / `showConfigBadge` / `showConfidenceBand` / `showDominantMarker` / `animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility; standard sizing + format + a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX` / `formatAcf`; `onPointClick({series, point})` (time panel) and `onLagClick({series, lag})` (ACF panel). Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_AUTOCORRELATION_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/GAP/TICK_COUNT/TIME_PANEL_RATIO/STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/LAG_STICK_WIDTH/MAX_LAG/CONFIDENCE_Z/PALETTE/POSITIVE_COLOR/NEGATIVE_COLOR/INSIGNIFICANT_COLOR/DOMINANT_COLOR/CONFIDENCE_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineAutocorrelationDefaultColor` (10-color cycle); `getLineAutocorrelationFinitePoints`; `normaliseLineAutocorrelationMaxLag(value, totalSamples?)` (defaults / floors / clamps non-negative / caps at N - 1 when N is provided); `normaliseLineAutocorrelationConfidenceZ` (defaults; clamps negative to 0); `normaliseLineAutocorrelationPanelRatio` (defaults; clamps to (0, 1)); `computeLineAutocorrelationMean(values)` (empty -> 0; skips non-finite); `computeLineAutocorrelationFunction(values, maxLag)` -- canonical biased ACF; **verified ACF(0) = 1 always for non-constant input**, **constant series gives ACF(0) = 1 and ACF(k > 0) = 0**, **sine wave with period 10 gives high positive ACF at lag 10 and negative ACF at lag 5**, linear input -> positive ACF at lag 1, all values in [-1, 1], returns maxLag + 1 entries, caps maxLag at N - 1; `computeLineAutocorrelationConfidenceBound(N, z?)` -- `z / sqrt(N)`; **verified canonical value 1.96 / sqrt(50) = 0.27718585**, returns 0 for non-positive N; `classifyLineAutocorrelationSignificance(value, bound)` -- 'positive-significant' \| 'negative-significant' \| 'insignificant'; bound 0 -> insignificant; non-finite -> insignificant; `findLineAutocorrelationDominantLag(acf)` -- picks lag in `[1, length)` with the largest `|ACF|`; returns `{lag: 0, value: 0}` when no lag >= 1 exists; skips non-finite; `runLineAutocorrelation(points, options?)` -- canonical pipeline; sorts ascending; drops non-finite; returns `{samples, acf, lags, confidenceBound, dominantLag, dominantValue, significantCount, positiveSignificantCount, negativeSignificantCount, maxLag, effectiveLag, totalSamples, mean}`; lag 0 always 'insignificant'; `computeLineAutocorrelationLayout({series, ...})` -- projects time + ACF panels side by side; per-series carries `{id, label, color, maxLag, effectiveLag, confidenceBound, mean, totalSamples, timePoints, timePath, lags, dominantLag, dominantValue, dominantAbsValue, significantCount, positiveSignificantCount, negativeSignificantCount, finiteCount, totalCount}`; per-lag carries projected `{lag, value, significance, px, py, stickX, stickY1, stickY2, isDominant, color}`; ACF y axis ticks are `[-1, -0.5, 0, 0.5, 1]`; ACF x axis spans `[0, lagMax]`; degenerate canvas / empty / all-hidden -> empty layout; `describeLineAutocorrelationChart(series, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions autocorrelation function + dominant lag + significant lags (No data when missing). Layout: two side-by-side SVG panels -- time panel (left, ~55% width) with raw line + optional dots; ACF panel (right, ~45% width) with one stick + dot per lag from 0 to maxLag, sticks anchored at ACF = 0 and extending to the lag value, confidence band drawn as translucent rectangle + two dashed reference lines at +/- bound, dominant lag with dashed vertical marker. Colour rules: positive-significant green (#16a34a), negative-significant red (#dc2626), insignificant slate (#94a3b8), dominant violet (#7c3aed) overrides significance. ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-autocorrelation-aria-desc"` span; time path + every dot (time + lag) `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; time path aria-label includes sample count; lag dot aria-label includes lag + ACF + significance + dominant flag. data-section on every node (chart-line-autocorrelation / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -x-label / -y-label / -lag-label / -acf-label / -confidence-band / -confidence-band-fill / -confidence-band-line / -series / -series-group / -time-path / -time-dot / -lag / -lag-stick / -lag-dot / -dominant-marker / -tooltip / -tooltip-label / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-y / -tooltip-lag / -tooltip-acf / -tooltip-significance / -tooltip-config / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -legend-total-points / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-max-lag / -badge-dominant-lag / -badge-confidence-bound / -badge-significant-count); root mirrors `data-series-count`, `data-visible-series-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-max-lag`, `data-confidence-z`, `data-confidence-bound`, `data-dominant-lag`, `data-dominant-value`, `data-significant-count`, `data-animate`; time path `data-kind="time"`; lag dots expose `data-lag`, `data-acf`, `data-significance`, `data-is-dominant`, `data-hovered`; series groups `data-series-max-lag`, `data-series-effective-lag`, `data-series-confidence-bound`, `data-series-dominant-lag`, `data-series-dominant-value`, `data-series-significant-count`, `data-series-positive-significant-count`, `data-series-negative-significant-count`, `data-series-total-samples`, `data-series-finite-count`, `data-series-mean`. Tooltip on lag dots shows label, lag, bold ACF, significance (coloured), `CI=<bound>`. Tooltip on time dots shows label, x, y. Config badge in top-left calls out `ACF max=<maxLag> dom k=<dominantLag> CI=<bound> sig=<count>`. Legend shows per-series `(dom k=<lag>; ACF <value>; sig <count>)`. 90 vitest cases (defaults + helpers, computeLineAutocorrelationFunction incl. **ACF(0) = 1 always for non-constant** + **constant series -> ACF(0) = 1, ACF(k>0) = 0** + **sine wave detection with period 10 gives high positive at lag 10 and negative at lag 5** + linear input + range [-1, 1] + caps maxLag at N-1 + returns maxLag+1 entries, computeLineAutocorrelationConfidenceBound incl. default z=1.96 + custom z + 0 for non-positive N + **canonical 1.96/sqrt(50) = 0.2771...**, classifyLineAutocorrelationSignificance, findLineAutocorrelationDominantLag, runLineAutocorrelation incl. **detects periodic structure for sine wave** + sorts ascending + drops non-finite + computes mean + lag 0 always insignificant, computeLineAutocorrelationLayout incl. side-by-side panels + time path + lag positions + dominant lag + confidence bound + per-series maxLag override + bounds overrides + ACF y ticks [-1, -0.5, 0, 0.5, 1] + totalPoints + marks dominant, describeLineAutocorrelationChart, component render incl. empty / time path kind=time / lag sticks count / lag 0 ACF ~ 1 verified / dominant marked is-dominant=true / confidence band shown + hide via prop / config badge with max+dominant+CI+sig + hide via prop / dominant marker shown + hide via prop / time dots via showDots / ARIA region + img + sr-only desc / root data-* / series group data-* / lag tooltip with acf + significance / time tooltip with x + y / omit tooltip via prop / onPointClick + onLagClick payloads / legend stats dom + ACF + sig + toggle uncontrolled + omit / animate flag + class / ref / displayName / xLabel + yLabel + lagLabel + acfLabel render) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.552 | UI: chart-line-arima primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.570 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-arima.tsx` ships `<ChartLineArima>` -- pure-SVG line chart with a simple **ARIMA(1,0,0) / AR(1) one-step prediction overlay**. Raw observations render as a dimmed reference line; the fitted AR(1) one-step prediction renders as a solid coloured overlay; an optional dashed forecast segment extends one step beyond the data. ARIMA(1,0,0) is a pure first-order autoregressive model (AR order 1, zero differencing, zero MA terms): `y_t = c + phi * y_{t-1} + epsilon_t`. The model is fitted by **conditional ordinary least squares** -- regress `y_t` on `y_{t-1}` over the pairs `(y_{t-1}, y_t)` for t = 1..N-1: `phi = sum (x_i - xbar)(y_i - ybar) / sum (x_i - xbar)^2`, `c = ybar - phi * xbar`. This is the standard estimator at the starting point of Box-Jenkins ARIMA fitting. One-step prediction: `yhat_t = c + phi * y_{t-1}` (index 0 null, no prior value). Forecast: `yhat_N = c + phi * y_{N-1}` placed at `x_{N-1} + step` where step is the mean x-spacing. Residual `e_t = y_t - yhat_t` is the one-step prediction error; RMSE = `sqrt(mean(e_t^2))` over valid predictions. Stationarity flag: `|phi| < 1`. Degenerate cases: `N < 2` or a constant series -> `phi = 0`, `c = mean`. **Verified properties: linear series [1,2,3,4,5] fits to phi = 1, c = 1 exactly (predictions reproduce the series, residuals 0, RMSE 0; phi = 1 is the boundary non-stationary unit-root case); constant series fits to phi = 0, c = mean; geometric decay y_t = 0.5*y_{t-1} recovers phi = 0.5, c = 0 exactly (stationary); explosive series y_t = 2*y_{t-1} fits to phi ~ 2 (non-stationary).** Distinct from `<ChartLineForecast>` (renders PRE-COMPUTED forecast data with a forecastFrom split index + optional yLower/yUpper bands -- it fits NO model; AR(1) FITS the model from the data and computes the prediction itself), `<ChartLineEwma>` (11.539; exponential smoothing, equivalent to an ARIMA(0,1,1) -- a different model class, MA-on-differences vs. autoregressive), `<ChartLineKalman>` (11.538; Bayesian state-space filter with process/measurement noise -- AR(1) is closed-form least squares with no noise model), `<ChartLineAutocorrelation>` (11.551; the ACF diagnostic plot -- ACF at lag 1 relates to phi but ACF is a DIAGNOSTIC whereas AR(1) is the FITTED model and prediction), `<ChartLineTrend>` (11.512; global linear regression of y on x -- AR(1) regresses y_t on y_{t-1}, the previous VALUE not the x position), and the smoothers `<ChartLineLoess>` (11.549), `<ChartLineSavgol>` (11.547), `<ChartLineMovingAvg>` (11.513) (noise filters -- AR(1) is a PREDICTIVE model where each point is predicted purely from the one before it). The simplest member of the ARIMA family, AR(1) is the canonical introduction to autoregressive time-series modelling and the natural baseline a one-step forecast is measured against. API: `series: readonly ChartLineArimaSeries[]` (`{id, label, data, color?}`; the AR(1) coefficients are always fitted from the data -- there are no per-series model parameters); `forecastColor` (default violet); `rawColor`; `residualPosColor` / `residualNegColor`; `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showLegend`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showRaw`/`showForecast`/`showResidualSticks`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility; standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX` / `formatCoefficient`; `onPointClick({series, point})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_ARIMA_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/RAW_STROKE_WIDTH/PREDICT_STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/RAW_OPACITY/RESIDUAL_OPACITY/PALETTE/RAW_COLOR/FORECAST_COLOR/RESIDUAL_POS_COLOR/RESIDUAL_NEG_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineArimaDefaultColor` (10-color cycle); `getLineArimaFinitePoints`; `classifyLineArimaResidualSign` ('positive' \| 'negative' \| 'zero'); `fitLineArimaAR1(values)` -- conditional OLS fit; returns `{phi, intercept, mean, stationary}`; empty / single -> phi 0; constant series -> phi 0 + intercept = mean; skips non-finite values; `predictLineArimaOneStep(values, fit)` -- one-step predictions; index 0 null; non-finite prior -> null; `forecastLineArimaNext(values, fit)` -- single forecast beyond the data; null for an empty series or a non-finite last value; `runLineArima(points)` -- canonical pipeline; sorts ascending; drops non-finite; fits the model; returns `{samples, phi, intercept, mean, stationary, rmse, maxAbsResidual, predictedValidCount, positiveResidualCount, negativeResidualCount, zeroResidualCount, forecast}`; per-sample `{index, x, raw, predicted, residual, residualSign}`; `computeLineArimaLayout({series, ...})` -- projects raw + predicted paths + residual segments + forecast point; per-series carries `{id, label, color, phi, intercept, mean, stationary, points, rawPath, predictedPath, residualSegments, forecast, forecastPx, forecastPy, finiteCount, totalCount, predictedValidCount, positiveResidualCount, negativeResidualCount, zeroResidualCount, rmseResidual, maxAbsResidual}`; per-point carries projected `{...sample, px, rawPy, predictedPy}`; `extendForForecast` option toggles whether the forecast x extends the x range; degenerate canvas / empty / all-hidden -> ok=false; `describeLineArimaChart(series, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions ARIMA(1,0,0) + AR(1) + phi + stationarity (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-arima-aria-desc"` span; raw path + predicted path + forecast dot + every dot `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; predicted path aria-label includes phi + intercept; dot aria-label includes raw + predicted values. data-section on every node (chart-line-arima / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -x-label / -y-label / -series / -series-group / -raw-path / -predicted-path / -forecast / -forecast-segment / -forecast-dot / -residual-stick / -dot / -tooltip / -tooltip-label / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-raw / -tooltip-predicted / -tooltip-residual / -tooltip-config / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -legend-total-points / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-phi / -badge-intercept / -badge-rmse / -badge-stationary); root mirrors `data-series-count`, `data-visible-series-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-phi`, `data-intercept`, `data-rmse`, `data-stationary`, `data-animate`; raw path `data-kind="raw"`; predicted path `data-kind="predicted"`; dots `data-raw`, `data-predicted`, `data-residual`, `data-residual-sign`; residual sticks `data-sign`; forecast group `data-forecast-x`, `data-forecast-value`; series groups `data-series-phi`, `data-series-intercept`, `data-series-mean`, `data-series-stationary`, `data-series-rmse`, `data-series-max-abs-residual`, `data-series-predicted-valid-count`, `data-series-positive-residual-count`, `data-series-negative-residual-count`, `data-series-zero-residual-count`, `data-series-finite-count`, `data-series-forecast`. Tooltip on dots shows the series label, x, raw value, bold predicted value (or n/a at index 0), residual (with +/- prefix), and the config `phi=<phi>, c=<intercept>`. Config badge in top-left calls out `AR1 phi=<phi> c=<intercept> rmse=<rmse> <stationary\|non-stationary>`. Legend shows per-series `(phi=<phi>; rmse <value>)`. 70 vitest cases (defaults + helpers, fitLineArimaAR1 incl. empty/single + **constant -> phi 0 + intercept = mean** + **linear [1..5] -> phi exactly 1 + intercept exactly 1 + non-stationary** + **geometric decay -> phi exactly 0.5 + intercept exactly 0 + stationary** + **explosive series -> phi ~ 2 + non-stationary** + skips non-finite, predictLineArimaOneStep incl. empty + **index 0 null** + predicts c + phi*y_{t-1} + linear reproduced exactly + non-finite prior -> null, forecastLineArimaNext incl. empty -> null + c + phi*last + non-finite last -> null, runLineArima incl. empty + **linear -> exact predictions + zero residuals + zero RMSE** + **constant -> flat predictions** + **geometric -> recovers phi 0.5** + sorts ascending + drops non-finite + **computes one-step forecast (linear -> 6 at x 5)** + predicted valid count + residual sign counts + stationary flag, computeLineArimaLayout incl. empty/degenerate/hidden + raw + predicted paths + phi/intercept/rmse/stationary exposed + forecast point with projected coords + extendForForecast toggles x range + hidden excluded + bounds overrides + totalPoints + residual segments only for predicted points, describeLineArimaChart incl. No data + summary mentions ARIMA(1,0,0) + AR(1) + phi + stationarity, component render incl. empty / raw path kind=raw / predicted path kind=predicted / hide raw / forecast segment + dot shown by default + hidden via prop / residual sticks via prop + omitted by default / dots via showDots / config badge with phi + intercept + rmse + stationary + hide via prop / ARIA region + img + sr-only desc / root data-* / series group data-* / tooltip with raw + predicted + residual + config rows + hide on leave + omit via prop / onPointClick payload / legend with phi + rmse + toggle + omit / animate flag + class / ref / displayName / custom ariaLabel / xLabel + yLabel / **linear input renders zero-RMSE config badge**) all pass under vitest 4.1.5. | 11.553 | UI: chart-line-holtwinters primitive | done | SHIPPED v1.11.571 (mgr-direct on c4/c4-mgr-auto). New `web/src/components/ui/chart-line-holtwinters.tsx` ships `<ChartLineHoltWinters>` -- pure-SVG line chart with an additive **Holt-Winters triple-exponential smoothing overlay**. Raw observations render as a dimmed reference line; the Holt-Winters one-step in-sample fitted value renders as a solid coloured overlay; an optional dashed multi-step forecast extends a full season beyond the data. Holt-Winters extends simple exponential smoothing with **three** coupled recursions -- one each for level, trend, and seasonality. For a season length m the additive variant updates: `level_t = alpha * (y_t - season_{t-m}) + (1-alpha) * (level_{t-1} + trend_{t-1})`, `trend_t = beta * (level_t - level_{t-1}) + (1-beta) * trend_{t-1}`, `season_t = gamma * (y_t - level_t) + (1-gamma) * season_{t-m}`. In-sample one-step fitted value: `yhat_t = level_{t-1} + trend_{t-1} + season_{t-m}`. Multi-step forecast h periods beyond the data: `yhat_{N-1+h} = level + h * trend + season_{(N-1+h) mod m}`. Three smoothing factors -- alpha (level, default 0.5), beta (trend, default 0.3), gamma (seasonal, default 0.3) -- all clamped to [0, 1]. Initialisation (standard textbook scheme): initialLevel = mean of the first season y_0..y_{m-1}; initialTrend = mean per-step slope between the first two seasons `(1/m) * sum_{i=0}^{m-1} (y_{m+i} - y_i) / m` (0 with fewer than two full seasons); initialSeasonals[i] = y_i - initialLevel (which sum to 0 by construction). The recursion runs for t = m..N-1 so the first m fitted values are null; the fit reports `ok = false` when there are fewer than m+1 finite values. **Verified properties: a purely seasonal series with no trend ([10,20,30,40] repeated) is fitted EXACTLY (every fitted value equals the raw value, residuals 0, RMSE 0) for any valid alpha/beta/gamma, and the forecast reproduces the continuing pattern [10,20,30,40]; a constant series is fitted exactly (level = mean, trend 0, seasonals 0); initialSeasonals sum to 0; initialTrend is 0 with one season and recovers the per-step slope exactly (1) with two seasons of a 0..7 ramp.** Distinct from `<ChartLineEwma>` (11.539; SINGLE exponential smoothing -- one recursion, level only -- Holt-Winters is TRIPLE exponential smoothing that additionally models trend and seasonality, which single ES cannot capture), `<ChartLineArima>` (11.552; an AR(1) autoregressive model fitted by least squares -- Holt-Winters is an exponential smoothing method, recursively weighted averages, not a regression, and explicitly decomposes seasonality), `<ChartLineDecompose>` (11.543; splits a series into trend + seasonal + residual COMPONENTS for inspection -- Holt-Winters uses the same three concepts as a FORECASTING model producing fitted values and a forward forecast, not a static decomposition), `<ChartLineKalman>` (11.538; Bayesian state-space filter with explicit process/measurement noise -- Holt-Winters is deterministic exponential smoothing with no noise model), `<ChartLineForecast>` (renders PRE-COMPUTED forecast data -- Holt-Winters fits the model itself and computes the forecast), and the smoothers `<ChartLineMovingAvg>` (11.513), `<ChartLineLoess>` (11.549), `<ChartLineSavgol>` (11.547) (no trend or seasonal model and no forecast). The canonical seasonal forecasting method taught alongside ARIMA -- the natural choice when a series has a clear repeating cycle plus drift. API: `series: readonly ChartLineHoltWintersSeries[]` (`{id, label, data, color?, alpha?, beta?, gamma?, seasonLength?}`; per-series factor / season overrides always beat chart-level); `alpha` (default 0.5), `beta` (default 0.3), `gamma` (default 0.3) all clamped to [0, 1]; `seasonLength` (default 4; integer >= 2); `forecastHorizon` (default 4; integer 0..366); `forecastColor` / `rawColor` / `residualPosColor` / `residualNegColor` / `gridColor` / `axisColor`; toggle flags `showAxis`/`showGrid`/`showDots`/`showLegend`/`showTooltip`/`showConfigBadge`/`showRaw`/`showForecast`/`showResidualSticks`/`animate`; controlled + uncontrolled visibility; standard sizing/format/a11y props; `formatValue` / `formatX` / `formatCoefficient`; `onPointClick({series, point})`. Pure helpers exported: `DEFAULT_CHART_LINE_HOLTWINTERS_WIDTH/HEIGHT/PADDING/TICK_COUNT/RAW_STROKE_WIDTH/FITTED_STROKE_WIDTH/DOT_RADIUS/RAW_OPACITY/RESIDUAL_OPACITY/ALPHA/BETA/GAMMA/SEASON_LENGTH/FORECAST_HORIZON/PALETTE/RAW_COLOR/FORECAST_COLOR/RESIDUAL_POS_COLOR/RESIDUAL_NEG_COLOR/GRID_COLOR/AXIS_COLOR`; `getLineHoltWintersDefaultColor` (10-color cycle); `getLineHoltWintersFinitePoints`; `normaliseLineHoltWintersSmoothingFactor(value, fallback)` (clamps to [0, 1]; non-finite -> fallback); `normaliseLineHoltWintersSeasonLength` (integer >= 2; non-finite -> 4; floors fractional); `normaliseLineHoltWintersForecastHorizon` (integer 0..366; non-finite -> 4; floors fractional); `classifyLineHoltWintersResidualSign` ('positive' \| 'negative' \| 'zero'); `fitLineHoltWinters(values, options?)` -- the additive Holt-Winters fit; returns `{ok, fitted, initialLevel, initialTrend, initialSeasonals, level, trend, seasonals, alpha, beta, gamma, seasonLength}`; skips non-finite values; `forecastLineHoltWinters(fit, horizon)` -- multi-step forecast values; `[]` when the fit failed or horizon <= 0; `runLineHoltWinters(points, options?)` -- canonical pipeline; sorts ascending; drops non-finite; fits the model; returns `{samples, fit, ok, level, trend, alpha, beta, gamma, seasonLength, rmse, maxAbsResidual, fittedValidCount, positiveResidualCount, negativeResidualCount, zeroResidualCount, forecast}`; per-sample `{index, x, raw, fitted, residual, residualSign}`; per-forecast `{horizon, x, value}`; `computeLineHoltWintersLayout({series, ...})` -- projects raw + fitted paths + residual segments + the forecast path; per-series carries `{id, label, color, alpha, beta, gamma, seasonLength, ok, level, trend, points, rawPath, fittedPath, residualSegments, forecastPoints, forecastPath, finiteCount, totalCount, fittedValidCount, positiveResidualCount, negativeResidualCount, zeroResidualCount, rmseResidual, maxAbsResidual}`; per-point projected `{...sample, px, rawPy, fittedPy}`; per-forecast-point projected `{...forecast, px, py}`; `extendForForecast` option toggles whether forecast x extends the x range; degenerate canvas / empty / all-hidden -> ok=false; `describeLineHoltWintersChart(series, options?)` -- ARIA summary mentions Holt-Winters + alpha/beta/gamma + season + RMSE (No data when missing). ARIA: root `<div role="region">` + `aria-label` + `aria-describedby`, SVG `role="img"` + same `aria-label`, sr-only `data-section="chart-line-holtwinters-aria-desc"` span; raw path + fitted path + forecast dots + every dot `role="graphics-symbol" tabIndex={0}`; fitted path aria-label includes all three factors + season length; dot aria-label includes raw + fitted values. data-section on every node (chart-line-holtwinters / -canvas / -aria-desc / -svg / -grid / -grid-line / -axes / -axis / -ticks / -tick / -tick-label / -x-label / -y-label / -series / -series-group / -raw-path / -fitted-path / -forecast / -forecast-path / -forecast-dot / -residual-stick / -dot / -tooltip / -tooltip-label / -tooltip-x / -tooltip-raw / -tooltip-fitted / -tooltip-residual / -tooltip-config / -legend / -legend-item / -legend-swatch / -legend-label / -legend-stats / -legend-total-points / -badge / -badge-icon / -badge-alpha / -badge-beta / -badge-gamma / -badge-season / -badge-rmse); root mirrors `data-series-count`, `data-visible-series-count`, `data-total-points`, `data-alpha`, `data-beta`, `data-gamma`, `data-season-length`, `data-rmse`, `data-fit-ok`, `data-animate`; raw path `data-kind="raw"`; fitted path `data-kind="fitted"`; dots `data-raw`, `data-fitted`, `data-residual`, `data-residual-sign`; residual sticks `data-sign`; forecast dots `data-horizon`, `data-forecast-x`, `data-forecast-value`; series groups `data-series-alpha`, `data-series-beta`, `data-series-gamma`, `data-series-season-length`, `data-series-fit-ok`, `data-series-level`, `data-series-trend`, `data-series-rmse`, `data-series-max-abs-residual`, `data-series-fitted-valid-count`, `data-series-positive-residual-count`, `data-series-negative-residual-count`, `data-series-zero-residual-count`, `data-series-finite-count`, `data-series-forecast-count`. Tooltip on dots shows the series label, x, raw value, bold fitted value (or n/a for the first season), residual (with +/- prefix), and the config `a=<alpha>, b=<beta>, g=<gamma>, m=<season>`. Config badge in top-left calls out `HW a=<alpha> b=<beta> g=<gamma> m=<season> rmse=<rmse>`. Legend shows per-series `(m=<season>; rmse <value>)`. 86 vitest cases (defaults + helpers, fitLineHoltWinters incl. **N<m+1 -> ok=false** + **initial level = first-season mean** + **initial seasonals sum to 0** + initial seasonals `[-15,-5,5,15]` for the periodic fixture + **initial trend 0 with one season / per-step slope with two seasons** + **first m fitted values null** + **periodic-no-trend series fitted exactly** + **constant series fitted exactly** + clamps factors + season + skips non-finite, forecastLineHoltWinters incl. `[]` when fit failed / horizon 0 + **periodic series forecasts the continuing pattern [10,20,30,40]** + constant forecast + honours horizon length, runLineHoltWinters incl. empty + **periodic -> fitted = raw + RMSE ~ 0** + constant -> RMSE ~ 0 + first m fitted null + sorts ascending + drops non-finite + **multi-step forecast with horizon points + continued x spacing** + fitted valid count = N - m + residual sign counts + exposes factors, computeLineHoltWintersLayout incl. empty/degenerate/hidden + raw + fitted paths + config exposed + forecast points + path + horizon 0 -> no forecast + per-series seasonLength + alpha overrides + short series renders raw but fit not ok + bounds overrides + totalPoints, describeLineHoltWintersChart incl. No data + summary mentions Holt-Winters + factors + season, component render incl. empty / raw path kind=raw / fitted path kind=fitted / hide raw / forecast path + dots shown by default + hidden via prop / residual sticks via prop + omitted by default / dots via showDots / config badge with all three factors + season + rmse + hide via prop / ARIA region + img + sr-only desc / root data-* / series group data-* / tooltip with raw + fitted + residual + config rows + hide on leave + omit via prop / onPointClick payload / legend with season + rmse + toggle + omit / animate flag + class / ref / displayName / custom ariaLabel / xLabel + yLabel / **periodic-no-trend input renders zero-RMSE config badge**) all pass under vitest 4.1.5.
